5268  CFLAGS="-O2"
5269else
5270  # if the user selected debug mode, don't use -O
5271  if test "$enable_debug" != yes; then
5272    CFLAGS="-O"
5273  fi
5274fi
5275
5276if test "$ac_env_CXXFLAGS_set" = set; then
5277  CXXFLAGS=$ac_env_CXXFLAGS_value
5278elif test "${CXXFLAGS+set}" = set; then
5279  : # (keep what template set)
5280elif test "$enable_coverage" = yes; then
5281  : # no optimization by default
5282elif test "$GCC" = yes; then
5283  CXXFLAGS="-O2"
5284else
5285  # if the user selected debug mode, don't use -O
5286  if test "$enable_debug" != yes; then
5287    CXXFLAGS="-O"
5288  fi
5289fi
5290
5291# When generating bitcode (for inlining) we always want to use -O2
5292# even when --enable-debug is specified. The bitcode is not going to
5293# be used for line-by-line debugging, and JIT inlining doesn't work
5294# without at least -O1 (otherwise clang will emit 'noinline'
5295# attributes everywhere), which is bad for testing.  Still allow the
5296# environment to override if done explicitly.
5297if test "$ac_env_BITCODE_CFLAGS_set" = set; then
5298  BITCODE_CFLAGS=$ac_env_BITCODE_CFLAGS_value
5299else
5300  BITCODE_CFLAGS="-O2 $BITCODE_CFLAGS"
5301fi
5302if test "$ac_env_BITCODE_CXXFLAGS_set" = set; then
5303  BITCODE_CXXFLAGS=$ac_env_BITCODE_CXXFLAGS_value
5304else
5305  BITCODE_CXXFLAGS="-O2 $BITCODE_CXXFLAGS"
5306fi
5307
5308# C[XX]FLAGS we determined above will be added back at the end
5309user_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
5310CFLAGS=""
5311user_CXXFLAGS=$CXXFLAGS
5312CXXFLAGS=""
5313user_BITCODE_CFLAGS=$BITCODE_CFLAGS
5314BITCODE_CFLAGS=""
5315user_BITCODE_CXXFLAGS=$BITCODE_CXXFLAGS
5316BITCODE_CXXFLAGS=""
5317
5318# set CFLAGS_UNROLL_LOOPS and CFLAGS_VECTORIZE from the environment, if present
5319if test "$ac_env_CFLAGS_UNROLL_LOOPS_set" = set; then
5320  CFLAGS_UNROLL_LOOPS=$ac_env_CFLAGS_UNROLL_LOOPS_value
5321fi
5322if test "$ac_env_CFLAGS_VECTORIZE_set" = set; then
5323  CFLAGS_VECTORIZE=$ac_env_CFLAGS_VECTORIZE_value
5324fi
5325
5326# Some versions of GCC support some additional useful warning flags.
5327# Check whether they are supported, and add them to CFLAGS if so.
5328# ICC pretends to be GCC but it's lying; it doesn't support these flags,
5329# but has its own.  Also check other compiler-specific flags here.
5330
5331if test "$GCC" = yes -a "$ICC" = no; then
5332  CFLAGS="-Wall -Wmissing-prototypes -Wpointer-arith"
5333  CXXFLAGS="-Wall -Wpointer-arith"
5334  # These work in some but not all gcc versions
5335  save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
5336
5337{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CC} supports -Wdeclaration-after-statement, for CFLAGS" >&5
5338$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CC} supports -Wdeclaration-after-statement, for CFLAGS... " >&6; }
5339if ${pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wdeclaration_after_statement+:} false; then :
5340  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
5341else
5342  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
5343pgac_save_CC=$CC
5344CC=${CC}
5345CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wdeclaration-after-statement"
5346ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
5347ac_c_werror_flag=yes
5348cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
5349/* end confdefs.h.  */

5358_ACEOF
5359if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
5360  pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wdeclaration_after_statement=yes
5361else
5362  pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wdeclaration_after_statement=no
5363fi
5364rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
5365ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag
5366CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
5367CC="$pgac_save_CC"
5368fi
5369{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wdeclaration_after_statement" >&5
5370$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wdeclaration_after_statement" >&6; }
5371if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wdeclaration_after_statement" = x"yes"; then
5372  CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wdeclaration-after-statement"
5373fi
5374
5375
5376  # -Wdeclaration-after-statement isn't applicable for C++.  Specific C files
5377  # disable it, so AC_SUBST the negative form.
5378  PERMIT_DECLARATION_AFTER_STATEMENT=
5379  if test x"$save_CFLAGS" != x"$CFLAGS"; then
5380    PERMIT_DECLARATION_AFTER_STATEMENT=-Wno-declaration-after-statement
5381  fi
5382
5383  # Really don't want VLAs to be used in our dialect of C
5384
5385{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CC} supports -Werror=vla, for CFLAGS" >&5
5386$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CC} supports -Werror=vla, for CFLAGS... " >&6; }
5387if ${pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Werror_vla+:} false; then :
5388  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
5389else
5390  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
5391pgac_save_CC=$CC
5392CC=${CC}
5393CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Werror=vla"
5394ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
5395ac_c_werror_flag=yes
5396cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
5397/* end confdefs.h.  */
5398
5399int
5400main ()
5401{
5402
5403  ;
5404  return 0;
5405}

6338$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CXX} supports -fexcess-precision=standard, for CXXFLAGS... " >&6; }

6465fi
6466
6467
6468  #
6469  # The following tests want to suppress various unhelpful warnings by adding
6470  # -Wno-foo switches.  But gcc won't complain about unrecognized -Wno-foo
6471  # switches, so we have to test for the positive form and if that works,
6472  # add the negative form.  Note that tests of this form typically need to
6473  # be duplicated in the BITCODE_CFLAGS setup stanza below.
6474  #
6475  # Suppress clang's unhelpful unused-command-line-argument warnings.
6476  NOT_THE_CFLAGS=""
6477  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CC} supports -Wunused-command-line-argument, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS" >&5
6478$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CC} supports -Wunused-command-line-argument, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }
6479if ${pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument+:} false; then :
6480  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
6481else
6482  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
6483pgac_save_CC=$CC
6484CC=${CC}
6485CFLAGS="${NOT_THE_CFLAGS} -Wunused-command-line-argument"
6486ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
6487ac_c_werror_flag=yes
6488cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
6489/* end confdefs.h.  */
6490
6491int
6492main ()
6493{
6494
6495  ;
6496  return 0;
6497}
6498_ACEOF
6499if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
6500  pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument=yes
6501else
6502  pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument=no
6503fi
6504rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
6505ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag
6506CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
6507CC="$pgac_save_CC"
6508fi
6509{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument" >&5
6510$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument" >&6; }
6511if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument" = x"yes"; then
6512  NOT_THE_CFLAGS="${NOT_THE_CFLAGS} -Wunused-command-line-argument"
6513fi
6514
6515
6516  if test -n "$NOT_THE_CFLAGS"; then
6517    CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -Wno-unused-command-line-argument"
6518  fi
6519  # Remove clang 12+'s compound-token-split-by-macro, as this causes a lot
6520  # of warnings when building plperl because of usages in the Perl headers.
6521  NOT_THE_CFLAGS=""
6522  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CC} supports -Wcompound-token-split-by-macro, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS" >&5
6523$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CC} supports -Wcompound-token-split-by-macro, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }
6524if ${pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__Wcompound_token_split_by_macro+:} false; then :
6525  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
6526else
6527  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
6528pgac_save_CC=$CC
6529CC=${CC}
6530CFLAGS="${NOT_THE_CFLAGS} -Wcompound-token-split-by-macro"
6531ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
6532ac_c_werror_flag=yes
6533cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
6534/* end confdefs.h.  */
6535
6536int
6537main ()
6538{
6539
6540  ;
6541  return 0;
6542}

6695elif test "$ICC" = yes; then

6697  # division by NAN (NaN == 0), -mp1 fixes it, so add it to the CFLAGS.
6698
6699{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CC} supports -mp1, for CFLAGS" >&5
6700$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CC} supports -mp1, for CFLAGS... " >&6; }

6831$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CXX} supports -fno-strict-aliasing, for CXXFLAGS... " >&6; }

6867ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'

6875$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CXX_cxxflags__fno_strict_aliasing" >&6; }

6881fi
6882
6883# If the compiler knows how to hide symbols, add the switch needed for that to
6884# CFLAGS_SL_MODULE and define HAVE_VISIBILITY_ATTRIBUTE.
6885#
6886# This is done separately from the above because -fvisibility is supported by
6887# quite a few different compilers, making the required repetition bothersome.
6888#
6889# We might need to add a separate test to check if
6890# __attribute__((visibility("hidden"))) is supported, if we encounter a
6891# compiler that supports one of the supported variants of -fvisibility=hidden
6892# but uses a different syntax to mark a symbol as exported.
6893if test "$GCC" = yes -o "$SUN_STUDIO_CC" = yes ; then
6894  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CC} supports -fvisibility=hidden, for CFLAGS_SL_MODULE" >&5
6895$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CC} supports -fvisibility=hidden, for CFLAGS_SL_MODULE... " >&6; }
6896if ${pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__fvisibility_hidden+:} false; then :
6897  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
6898else
6899  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
6900pgac_save_CC=$CC
6901CC=${CC}
6902CFLAGS="${CFLAGS_SL_MODULE} -fvisibility=hidden"
6903ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
6904ac_c_werror_flag=yes
6905cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
6906/* end confdefs.h.  */
6907
6908int
6909main ()
6910{
6911
6912  ;
6913  return 0;
6914}

6920fi

6935$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CXX} supports -fvisibility=hidden, for CXXFLAGS_SL_MODULE... " >&6; }

7047
7048# Determine flags used to emit bitcode for JIT inlining.
7049# 1. We must duplicate any behaviour-changing compiler flags used above,
7050# to keep compatibility with the compiler used for normal Postgres code.
7051# 2. We don't bother to duplicate extra-warnings switches --- seeing a
7052# warning in the main build is enough.
7053# 3. But we must duplicate -Wno-warning flags, else we'll see those anyway.
7054if test "$with_llvm" = yes ; then
7055  CLANGXX="$CLANG -xc++"
7056
7057  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CLANG} supports -fno-strict-aliasing, for BITCODE_CFLAGS" >&5
7058$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -fno-strict-aliasing, for BITCODE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }
7059if ${pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__fno_strict_aliasing+:} false; then :
7060  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7061else
7062  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
7063pgac_save_CC=$CC
7064CC=${CLANG}
7065CFLAGS="${BITCODE_CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
7066ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
7067ac_c_werror_flag=yes
7068cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7069/* end confdefs.h.  */
7070
7071int
7072main ()
7073{
7074
7075  ;
7076  return 0;
7077}
7078_ACEOF
7079if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7080  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__fno_strict_aliasing=yes
7081else

7083fi

7085ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag
7086CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
7087CC="$pgac_save_CC"

7093fi
7094
7095  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CLANGXX} supports -fno-strict-aliasing, for BITCODE_CXXFLAGS" >&5
7096$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANGXX} supports -fno-strict-aliasing, for BITCODE_CXXFLAGS... " >&6; }

7102CXX=${CLANGXX}

7105ac_cxx_werror_flag=yes

7310
7311ac_cxx_werror_flag=$ac_save_cxx_werror_flag
7312CXXFLAGS="$pgac_save_CXXFLAGS"
7313CXX="$pgac_save_CXX"
7314fi
7315{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CLANGXX_cxxflags__fexcess_precision_standard" >&5
7316$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANGXX_cxxflags__fexcess_precision_standard" >&6; }
7317if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_CLANGXX_cxxflags__fexcess_precision_standard" = x"yes"; then
7318  BITCODE_CXXFLAGS="${BITCODE_CXXFLAGS} -fexcess-precision=standard"
7319fi
7320
7321
7322  # Ideally bitcode should perhaps match $CC's use, or not, of outline atomic
7323  # functions, but for now we err on the side of suppressing them in bitcode,
7324  # because we can't assume they're available at runtime.  This affects aarch64
7325  # builds using the basic armv8-a ISA without LSE support.
7326  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CLANG} supports -mno-outline-atomics, for BITCODE_CFLAGS" >&5
7327$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -mno-outline-atomics, for BITCODE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }
7328if ${pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics+:} false; then :
7329  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7330else
7331  pgac_save_CXXFLAGS=$CXXFLAGS
7332pgac_save_CXX=$CXX
7333CXX=${CLANG}
7334CXXFLAGS="${BITCODE_CFLAGS} -mno-outline-atomics"
7335ac_save_cxx_werror_flag=$ac_cxx_werror_flag
7336ac_cxx_werror_flag=yes
7337ac_ext=cpp
7338ac_cpp='$CXXCPP $CPPFLAGS'
7339ac_compile='$CXX -c $CXXFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
7340ac_link='$CXX -o conftest$ac_exeext $CXXFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
7341ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_cxx_compiler_gnu
7342
7343cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7344/* end confdefs.h.  */
7345
7346int
7347main ()
7348{
7349
7350  ;
7351  return 0;
7352}
7353_ACEOF
7354if ac_fn_cxx_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7355  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics=yes
7356else
7357  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics=no
7358fi
7359rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext

7361ac_cpp='$CPP $CPPFLAGS'
7362ac_compile='$CC -c $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
7363ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
7364ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_c_compiler_gnu
7365
7366ac_cxx_werror_flag=$ac_save_cxx_werror_flag
7367CXXFLAGS="$pgac_save_CXXFLAGS"
7368CXX="$pgac_save_CXX"
7369fi
7370{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics" >&5
7371$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics" >&6; }
7372if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics" = x"yes"; then
7373  BITCODE_CFLAGS="${BITCODE_CFLAGS} -mno-outline-atomics"
7374fi
7375
7376  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CLANG} supports -mno-outline-atomics, for BITCODE_CXXFLAGS" >&5
7377$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -mno-outline-atomics, for BITCODE_CXXFLAGS... " >&6; }
7378if ${pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics+:} false; then :
7379  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7380else
7381  pgac_save_CXXFLAGS=$CXXFLAGS
7382pgac_save_CXX=$CXX
7383CXX=${CLANG}
7384CXXFLAGS="${BITCODE_CXXFLAGS} -mno-outline-atomics"
7385ac_save_cxx_werror_flag=$ac_cxx_werror_flag
7386ac_cxx_werror_flag=yes
7387ac_ext=cpp
7388ac_cpp='$CXXCPP $CPPFLAGS'
7389ac_compile='$CXX -c $CXXFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
7390ac_link='$CXX -o conftest$ac_exeext $CXXFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
7391ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_cxx_compiler_gnu
7392
7393cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7394/* end confdefs.h.  */

7396int
7397main ()
7398{
7399
7400  ;
7401  return 0;
7402}
7403_ACEOF
7404if ac_fn_cxx_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7405  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics=yes
7406else

7413ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'

7415
7416ac_cxx_werror_flag=$ac_save_cxx_werror_flag

7419fi
7420{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics" >&5
7421$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cxxflags__mno_outline_atomics" >&6; }

7423  BITCODE_CXXFLAGS="${BITCODE_CXXFLAGS} -mno-outline-atomics"

7426
7427  NOT_THE_CFLAGS=""

7429$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -Wunused-command-line-argument, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }

7456ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag

7461$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wunused_command_line_argument" >&6; }

7471$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -Wcompound-token-split-by-macro, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }

7488  ;

7494else

7498ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag

7501fi
7502{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wcompound_token_split_by_macro" >&5
7503$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wcompound_token_split_by_macro" >&6; }

7506fi
7507
7508  if test -n "$NOT_THE_CFLAGS"; then
7509    BITCODE_CFLAGS="$BITCODE_CFLAGS -Wno-compound-token-split-by-macro"
7510  fi
7511  NOT_THE_CFLAGS=""
7512  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CLANG} supports -Wformat-truncation, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS" >&5
7513$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -Wformat-truncation, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }
7514if ${pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wformat_truncation+:} false; then :
7515  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7516else
7517  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS

7523cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7524/* end confdefs.h.  */
7525
7526int
7527main ()
7528{
7529
7530  ;
7531  return 0;
7532}
7533_ACEOF
7534if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7535  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wformat_truncation=yes
7536else
7537  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wformat_truncation=no
7538fi
7539rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
7540ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag
7541CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
7542CC="$pgac_save_CC"
7543fi
7544{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wformat_truncation" >&5
7545$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wformat_truncation" >&6; }
7546if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wformat_truncation" = x"yes"; then
7547  NOT_THE_CFLAGS="${NOT_THE_CFLAGS} -Wformat-truncation"
7548fi
7549
7550  if test -n "$NOT_THE_CFLAGS"; then
7551    BITCODE_CFLAGS="$BITCODE_CFLAGS -Wno-format-truncation"
7552  fi
7553  NOT_THE_CFLAGS=""
7554  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ${CLANG} supports -Wstringop-truncation, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS" >&5
7555$as_echo_n "checking whether ${CLANG} supports -Wstringop-truncation, for NOT_THE_CFLAGS... " >&6; }
7556if ${pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wstringop_truncation+:} false; then :
7557  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7558else
7559  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
7560pgac_save_CC=$CC
7561CC=${CLANG}
7562CFLAGS="${NOT_THE_CFLAGS} -Wstringop-truncation"
7563ac_save_c_werror_flag=$ac_c_werror_flag
7564ac_c_werror_flag=yes
7565cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7566/* end confdefs.h.  */
7567
7568int
7569main ()
7570{
7571
7572  ;
7573  return 0;
7574}
7575_ACEOF
7576if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7577  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wstringop_truncation=yes
7578else
7579  pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wstringop_truncation=no
7580fi
7581rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
7582ac_c_werror_flag=$ac_save_c_werror_flag
7583CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
7584CC="$pgac_save_CC"
7585fi
7586{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wstringop_truncation" >&5
7587$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wstringop_truncation" >&6; }
7588if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_CLANG_cflags__Wstringop_truncation" = x"yes"; then
7589  NOT_THE_CFLAGS="${NOT_THE_CFLAGS} -Wstringop-truncation"
7590fi
7591
7592  if test -n "$NOT_THE_CFLAGS"; then
7593    BITCODE_CFLAGS="$BITCODE_CFLAGS -Wno-stringop-truncation"
7594  fi
7595fi
7596
7597# supply -g if --enable-debug
7598if test "$enable_debug" = yes && test "$ac_cv_prog_cc_g" = yes; then
7599  CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -g"
7600fi
7601
7602if test "$enable_debug" = yes && test "$ac_cv_prog_cxx_g" = yes; then
7603  CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -g"
7604fi
7605
7606# enable code coverage if --enable-coverage
7607if test "$enable_coverage" = yes; then
7608  if test "$GCC" = yes; then
7609    CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage"
7610    CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -fprofile-arcs -ftest-coverage"
7611  else
7612    as_fn_error $? "--enable-coverage is supported only when using GCC" "$LINENO" 5
7613  fi
7614fi
7615
7616# enable profiling if --enable-profiling
7617if test "$enable_profiling" = yes && test "$ac_cv_prog_cc_g" = yes; then
7618  if test "$GCC" = yes; then
7619
7620$as_echo "#define PROFILE_PID_DIR 1" >>confdefs.h
7621
7622    CFLAGS="$CFLAGS -pg $PLATFORM_PROFILE_FLAGS"
7623    CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS -pg $PLATFORM_PROFILE_FLAGS"
7624  else
7625    as_fn_error $? "--enable-profiling is supported only when using GCC" "$LINENO" 5
7626  fi
7627fi
7628
7629# On Solaris, we need these #defines to get POSIX-conforming versions
7630# of many interfaces (sigwait, getpwuid_r, shmdt, ...).
7631if test "$PORTNAME" = "solaris"; then
7632  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS -D_POSIX_C_SOURCE=200112L -D__EXTENSIONS__ -D_POSIX_PTHREAD_SEMANTICS"
7633fi
7634
7635# We already have this in Makefile.win32, but configure needs it too
7636if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32"; then
7637  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS -I$srcdir/src/include/port/win32"
7638fi
7639
7640# Now that we're done automatically adding stuff to C[XX]FLAGS, put back the
7641# user-specified flags (if any) at the end.  This lets users override
7642# the automatic additions.
7643CFLAGS="$CFLAGS $user_CFLAGS"
7644CXXFLAGS="$CXXFLAGS $user_CXXFLAGS"
7645BITCODE_CFLAGS="$BITCODE_CFLAGS $user_BITCODE_CFLAGS"
7646BITCODE_CXXFLAGS="$BITCODE_CXXFLAGS $user_BITCODE_CXXFLAGS"
7647
7648
7649
7650
7651# The template file must set up CFLAGS_SL; we don't support user override
7652
7653
7654# Check if the compiler still works with the final flag settings
7655# (note, we're not checking that for CXX, which is optional)
7656{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether the C compiler still works" >&5
7657$as_echo_n "checking whether the C compiler still works... " >&6; }
7658cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7659/* end confdefs.h.  */
7660
7661int
7662main ()
7663{
7664return 0;
7665  ;
7666  return 0;
7667}
7668_ACEOF
7669if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
7670  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
7671$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
7672else
7673  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
7674$as_echo "no" >&6; }
7675   as_fn_error $? "cannot proceed" "$LINENO" 5
7676fi
7677rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
7678    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
7679
7680# Defend against gcc -ffast-math
7681if test "$GCC" = yes; then
7682cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7683/* end confdefs.h.  */
7684
7685int
7686main ()
7687{
7688#ifdef __FAST_MATH__
7689choke me
7690#endif
7691  ;
7692  return 0;
7693}
7694_ACEOF
7695if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7696
7697else
7698  as_fn_error $? "do not put -ffast-math in CFLAGS" "$LINENO" 5
7699fi
7700rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
7701fi
7702
7703# Defend against clang being used on x86-32 without SSE2 enabled.  As current
7704# versions of clang do not understand -fexcess-precision=standard, the use of
7705# x87 floating point operations leads to problems like isinf possibly returning
7706# false for a value that is infinite when converted from the 80bit register to
7707# the 8byte memory representation.
7708#
7709# Only perform the test if the compiler doesn't understand
7710# -fexcess-precision=standard, that way a potentially fixed compiler will work
7711# automatically.
7712if test "$pgac_cv_prog_CC_cflags__fexcess_precision_standard" = no; then
7713cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7714/* end confdefs.h.  */
7715
7716int
7717main ()
7718{
7719
7720#if defined(__clang__) && defined(__i386__) && !defined(__SSE2_MATH__)
7721choke me
7722#endif
7723
7724  ;
7725  return 0;
7726}
7727_ACEOF
7728if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
7729
7730else
7731  as_fn_error $? "Compiling PostgreSQL with clang, on 32bit x86, requires SSE2 support. Use -msse2 or use gcc." "$LINENO" 5
7732fi
7733rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
7734fi
7735
7736ac_ext=c
7737ac_cpp='$CPP $CPPFLAGS'
7738ac_compile='$CC -c $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
7739ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
7740ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_c_compiler_gnu
7741{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking how to run the C preprocessor" >&5
7742$as_echo_n "checking how to run the C preprocessor... " >&6; }
7743# On Suns, sometimes $CPP names a directory.
7744if test -n "$CPP" && test -d "$CPP"; then
7745  CPP=
7746fi
7747if test -z "$CPP"; then
7748  if ${ac_cv_prog_CPP+:} false; then :
7749  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7750else
7751      # Double quotes because CPP needs to be expanded
7752    for CPP in "$CC -E" "$CC -E -traditional-cpp" "/lib/cpp"
7753    do
7754      ac_preproc_ok=false
7755for ac_c_preproc_warn_flag in '' yes
7756do
7757  # Use a header file that comes with gcc, so configuring glibc
7758  # with a fresh cross-compiler works.
7759  # Prefer <limits.h> to <assert.h> if __STDC__ is defined, since
7760  # <limits.h> exists even on freestanding compilers.
7761  # On the NeXT, cc -E runs the code through the compiler's parser,
7762  # not just through cpp. "Syntax error" is here to catch this case.
7763  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7764/* end confdefs.h.  */
7765#ifdef __STDC__
7766# include <limits.h>
7767#else
7768# include <assert.h>
7769#endif
7770		     Syntax error
7771_ACEOF
7772if ac_fn_c_try_cpp "$LINENO"; then :
7773
7774else
7775  # Broken: fails on valid input.
7776continue
7777fi
7778rm -f conftest.err conftest.i conftest.$ac_ext
7779
7780  # OK, works on sane cases.  Now check whether nonexistent headers
7781  # can be detected and how.
7782  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7783/* end confdefs.h.  */
7784#include <ac_nonexistent.h>
7785_ACEOF
7786if ac_fn_c_try_cpp "$LINENO"; then :
7787  # Broken: success on invalid input.
7788continue
7789else
7790  # Passes both tests.
7791ac_preproc_ok=:
7792break
7793fi
7794rm -f conftest.err conftest.i conftest.$ac_ext
7795
7796done
7797# Because of `break', _AC_PREPROC_IFELSE's cleaning code was skipped.
7798rm -f conftest.i conftest.err conftest.$ac_ext
7799if $ac_preproc_ok; then :
7800  break
7801fi
7802
7803    done
7804    ac_cv_prog_CPP=$CPP
7805
7806fi
7807  CPP=$ac_cv_prog_CPP
7808else
7809  ac_cv_prog_CPP=$CPP
7810fi
7811{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $CPP" >&5
7812$as_echo "$CPP" >&6; }
7813ac_preproc_ok=false
7814for ac_c_preproc_warn_flag in '' yes
7815do
7816  # Use a header file that comes with gcc, so configuring glibc
7817  # with a fresh cross-compiler works.
7818  # Prefer <limits.h> to <assert.h> if __STDC__ is defined, since
7819  # <limits.h> exists even on freestanding compilers.
7820  # On the NeXT, cc -E runs the code through the compiler's parser,
7821  # not just through cpp. "Syntax error" is here to catch this case.
7822  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7823/* end confdefs.h.  */
7824#ifdef __STDC__
7825# include <limits.h>
7826#else
7827# include <assert.h>
7828#endif
7829		     Syntax error
7830_ACEOF
7831if ac_fn_c_try_cpp "$LINENO"; then :
7832
7833else
7834  # Broken: fails on valid input.
7835continue
7836fi
7837rm -f conftest.err conftest.i conftest.$ac_ext
7838
7839  # OK, works on sane cases.  Now check whether nonexistent headers
7840  # can be detected and how.
7841  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
7842/* end confdefs.h.  */
7843#include <ac_nonexistent.h>
7844_ACEOF
7845if ac_fn_c_try_cpp "$LINENO"; then :
7846  # Broken: success on invalid input.
7847continue
7848else
7849  # Passes both tests.
7850ac_preproc_ok=:
7851break
7852fi
7853rm -f conftest.err conftest.i conftest.$ac_ext
7854
7855done
7856# Because of `break', _AC_PREPROC_IFELSE's cleaning code was skipped.
7857rm -f conftest.i conftest.err conftest.$ac_ext
7858if $ac_preproc_ok; then :
7859
7860else
7861  { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
7862$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
7863as_fn_error $? "C preprocessor \"$CPP\" fails sanity check
7864See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
7865fi
7866
7867ac_ext=c
7868ac_cpp='$CPP $CPPFLAGS'
7869ac_compile='$CC -c $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
7870ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
7871ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_c_compiler_gnu
7872
7873
7874
7875
7876#
7877# Set up TAS assembly code if needed; the template file has now had its
7878# chance to request this.
7879#
7880ac_config_links="$ac_config_links src/backend/port/tas.s:src/backend/port/tas/${tas_file}"
7881
7882
7883if test "$need_tas" = yes ; then
7884  TAS=tas.o
7885else
7886  TAS=""
7887fi
7888
7889
7890
7891cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
7892#define DLSUFFIX "$DLSUFFIX"
7893_ACEOF
7894
7895
7896#
7897# Set up pkg_config in case we need it below
7898#
7899
7900
7901
7902
7903
7904
7905
7906if test "x$ac_cv_env_PKG_CONFIG_set" != "xset"; then
7907	if test -n "$ac_tool_prefix"; then
7908  # Extract the first word of "${ac_tool_prefix}pkg-config", so it can be a program name with args.
7909set dummy ${ac_tool_prefix}pkg-config; ac_word=$2
7910{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
7911$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
7912if ${ac_cv_path_PKG_CONFIG+:} false; then :
7913  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7914else
7915  case $PKG_CONFIG in
7916  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
7917  ac_cv_path_PKG_CONFIG="$PKG_CONFIG" # Let the user override the test with a path.
7918  ;;
7919  *)
7920  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
7921for as_dir in $PATH
7922do
7923  IFS=$as_save_IFS
7924  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
7925    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
7926  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
7927    ac_cv_path_PKG_CONFIG="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
7928    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
7929    break 2
7930  fi
7931done
7932  done
7933IFS=$as_save_IFS
7934
7935  ;;
7936esac
7937fi
7938PKG_CONFIG=$ac_cv_path_PKG_CONFIG
7939if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
7940  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PKG_CONFIG" >&5
7941$as_echo "$PKG_CONFIG" >&6; }
7942else
7943  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
7944$as_echo "no" >&6; }
7945fi
7946
7947
7948fi
7949if test -z "$ac_cv_path_PKG_CONFIG"; then
7950  ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG=$PKG_CONFIG
7951  # Extract the first word of "pkg-config", so it can be a program name with args.
7952set dummy pkg-config; ac_word=$2
7953{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
7954$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
7955if ${ac_cv_path_ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG+:} false; then :
7956  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
7957else
7958  case $ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG in
7959  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
7960  ac_cv_path_ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG="$ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG" # Let the user override the test with a path.
7961  ;;
7962  *)
7963  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
7964for as_dir in $PATH
7965do
7966  IFS=$as_save_IFS
7967  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
7968    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
7969  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
7970    ac_cv_path_ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
7971    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
7972    break 2
7973  fi
7974done
7975  done
7976IFS=$as_save_IFS
7977
7978  ;;
7979esac
7980fi
7981ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG=$ac_cv_path_ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG
7982if test -n "$ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG"; then
7983  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG" >&5
7984$as_echo "$ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG" >&6; }
7985else
7986  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
7987$as_echo "no" >&6; }
7988fi
7989
7990  if test "x$ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG" = x; then
7991    PKG_CONFIG=""
7992  else
7993    case $cross_compiling:$ac_tool_warned in
7994yes:)
7995{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&5
7996$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&2;}
7997ac_tool_warned=yes ;;
7998esac
7999    PKG_CONFIG=$ac_pt_PKG_CONFIG
8000  fi
8001else
8002  PKG_CONFIG="$ac_cv_path_PKG_CONFIG"
8003fi
8004
8005fi
8006if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8007	_pkg_min_version=0.9.0
8008	{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking pkg-config is at least version $_pkg_min_version" >&5
8009$as_echo_n "checking pkg-config is at least version $_pkg_min_version... " >&6; }
8010	if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version $_pkg_min_version; then
8011		{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
8012$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
8013	else
8014		{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
8015$as_echo "no" >&6; }
8016		PKG_CONFIG=""
8017	fi
8018fi
8019
8020#
8021# Automatic dependency tracking
8022#
8023
8024
8025# Check whether --enable-depend was given.
8026if test "${enable_depend+set}" = set; then :
8027  enableval=$enable_depend;
8028  case $enableval in
8029    yes)
8030      autodepend=yes
8031      ;;
8032    no)
8033      :
8034      ;;
8035    *)
8036      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --enable-depend option" "$LINENO" 5
8037      ;;
8038  esac
8039
8040else
8041  enable_depend=no
8042
8043fi
8044
8045
8046
8047
8048
8049#
8050# Enable assert checks
8051#
8052
8053
8054# Check whether --enable-cassert was given.
8055if test "${enable_cassert+set}" = set; then :
8056  enableval=$enable_cassert;
8057  case $enableval in
8058    yes)
8059
8060$as_echo "#define USE_ASSERT_CHECKING 1" >>confdefs.h
8061
8062      ;;
8063    no)
8064      :
8065      ;;
8066    *)
8067      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --enable-cassert option" "$LINENO" 5
8068      ;;
8069  esac
8070
8071else
8072  enable_cassert=no
8073
8074fi
8075
8076
8077
8078
8079#
8080# Include directories
8081#
8082ac_save_IFS=$IFS
8083IFS="${IFS}${PATH_SEPARATOR}"
8084# SRCH_INC comes from the template file
8085for dir in $with_includes $SRCH_INC; do
8086  if test -d "$dir"; then
8087    INCLUDES="$INCLUDES -I$dir"
8088  else
8089    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: *** Include directory $dir does not exist." >&5
8090$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: *** Include directory $dir does not exist." >&2;}
8091  fi
8092done
8093IFS=$ac_save_IFS
8094
8095
8096#
8097# Library directories
8098#
8099ac_save_IFS=$IFS
8100IFS="${IFS}${PATH_SEPARATOR}"
8101# LIBRARY_DIRS comes from command line, SRCH_LIB from template file.
8102for dir in $LIBRARY_DIRS $SRCH_LIB; do
8103  if test -d "$dir"; then
8104    LIBDIRS="$LIBDIRS -L$dir"
8105  else
8106    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: *** Library directory $dir does not exist." >&5
8107$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: *** Library directory $dir does not exist." >&2;}
8108  fi
8109done
8110IFS=$ac_save_IFS
8111
8112#
8113# ICU
8114#
8115{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with ICU support" >&5
8116$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with ICU support... " >&6; }
8117
8118
8119
8120# Check whether --with-icu was given.
8121if test "${with_icu+set}" = set; then :
8122  withval=$with_icu;
8123  case $withval in
8124    yes)
8125
8126$as_echo "#define USE_ICU 1" >>confdefs.h
8127
8128      ;;
8129    no)
8130      :
8131      ;;
8132    *)
8133      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-icu option" "$LINENO" 5
8134      ;;
8135  esac
8136
8137else
8138  with_icu=yes
8139
8140$as_echo "#define USE_ICU 1" >>confdefs.h
8141
8142fi
8143
8144
8145{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_icu" >&5
8146$as_echo "$with_icu" >&6; }
8147
8148
8149if test "$with_icu" = yes; then
8150
8151pkg_failed=no
8152{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for icu-uc icu-i18n" >&5
8153$as_echo_n "checking for icu-uc icu-i18n... " >&6; }
8154
8155if test -n "$ICU_CFLAGS"; then
8156    pkg_cv_ICU_CFLAGS="$ICU_CFLAGS"
8157 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8158    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
8159    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"icu-uc icu-i18n\""; } >&5
8160  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "icu-uc icu-i18n") 2>&5
8161  ac_status=$?
8162  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
8163  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
8164  pkg_cv_ICU_CFLAGS=`$PKG_CONFIG --cflags "icu-uc icu-i18n" 2>/dev/null`
8165		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
8166else
8167  pkg_failed=yes
8168fi
8169 else
8170    pkg_failed=untried
8171fi
8172if test -n "$ICU_LIBS"; then
8173    pkg_cv_ICU_LIBS="$ICU_LIBS"
8174 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8175    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
8176    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"icu-uc icu-i18n\""; } >&5
8177  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "icu-uc icu-i18n") 2>&5
8178  ac_status=$?
8179  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
8180  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
8181  pkg_cv_ICU_LIBS=`$PKG_CONFIG --libs "icu-uc icu-i18n" 2>/dev/null`
8182		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
8183else
8184  pkg_failed=yes
8185fi
8186 else
8187    pkg_failed=untried
8188fi
8189
8190
8191
8192if test $pkg_failed = yes; then
8193        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
8194$as_echo "no" >&6; }
8195
8196if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version 0.20; then
8197        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes
8198else
8199        _pkg_short_errors_supported=no
8200fi
8201        if test $_pkg_short_errors_supported = yes; then
8202	        ICU_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --short-errors --print-errors --cflags --libs "icu-uc icu-i18n" 2>&1`
8203        else
8204	        ICU_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --print-errors --cflags --libs "icu-uc icu-i18n" 2>&1`
8205        fi
8206	# Put the nasty error message in config.log where it belongs
8207	echo "$ICU_PKG_ERRORS" >&5
8208
8209	as_fn_error $? "Package requirements (icu-uc icu-i18n) were not met:
8210
8211$ICU_PKG_ERRORS
8212
8213Consider adjusting the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable if you
8214installed software in a non-standard prefix.
8215
8216Alternatively, you may set the environment variables ICU_CFLAGS
8217and ICU_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
8218See the pkg-config man page for more details." "$LINENO" 5
8219elif test $pkg_failed = untried; then
8220        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
8221$as_echo "no" >&6; }
8222	{ { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
8223$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
8224as_fn_error $? "The pkg-config script could not be found or is too old.  Make sure it
8225is in your PATH or set the PKG_CONFIG environment variable to the full
8226path to pkg-config.
8227
8228Alternatively, you may set the environment variables ICU_CFLAGS
8229and ICU_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
8230See the pkg-config man page for more details.
8231
8232To get pkg-config, see <http://pkg-config.freedesktop.org/>.
8233See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
8234else
8235	ICU_CFLAGS=$pkg_cv_ICU_CFLAGS
8236	ICU_LIBS=$pkg_cv_ICU_LIBS
8237        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
8238$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
8239
8240fi
8241fi
8242
8243#
8244# Optionally build Tcl modules (PL/Tcl)
8245#
8246{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with Tcl" >&5
8247$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with Tcl... " >&6; }
8248
8249
8250
8251# Check whether --with-tcl was given.
8252if test "${with_tcl+set}" = set; then :
8253  withval=$with_tcl;
8254  case $withval in
8255    yes)
8256      :
8257      ;;
8258    no)
8259      :
8260      ;;
8261    *)
8262      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-tcl option" "$LINENO" 5
8263      ;;
8264  esac
8265
8266else
8267  with_tcl=no
8268
8269fi
8270
8271
8272{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_tcl" >&5
8273$as_echo "$with_tcl" >&6; }
8274
8275
8276# We see if the path to the Tcl/Tk configuration scripts is specified.
8277# This will override the use of tclsh to find the paths to search.
8278
8279
8280
8281
8282# Check whether --with-tclconfig was given.
8283if test "${with_tclconfig+set}" = set; then :
8284  withval=$with_tclconfig;
8285  case $withval in
8286    yes)
8287      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-tclconfig option" "$LINENO" 5
8288      ;;
8289    no)
8290      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-tclconfig option" "$LINENO" 5
8291      ;;
8292    *)
8293
8294      ;;
8295  esac
8296
8297fi
8298
8299
8300
8301#
8302# Optionally build Perl modules (PL/Perl)
8303#
8304{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build Perl modules" >&5
8305$as_echo_n "checking whether to build Perl modules... " >&6; }
8306
8307
8308
8309# Check whether --with-perl was given.
8310if test "${with_perl+set}" = set; then :
8311  withval=$with_perl;
8312  case $withval in
8313    yes)
8314      :
8315      ;;
8316    no)
8317      :
8318      ;;
8319    *)
8320      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-perl option" "$LINENO" 5
8321      ;;
8322  esac
8323
8324else
8325  with_perl=no
8326
8327fi
8328
8329
8330{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_perl" >&5
8331$as_echo "$with_perl" >&6; }
8332
8333
8334#
8335# Optionally build Python modules (PL/Python)
8336#
8337{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build Python modules" >&5
8338$as_echo_n "checking whether to build Python modules... " >&6; }
8339
8340
8341
8342# Check whether --with-python was given.
8343if test "${with_python+set}" = set; then :
8344  withval=$with_python;
8345  case $withval in
8346    yes)
8347      :
8348      ;;
8349    no)
8350      :
8351      ;;
8352    *)
8353      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-python option" "$LINENO" 5
8354      ;;
8355  esac
8356
8357else
8358  with_python=no
8359
8360fi
8361
8362
8363{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_python" >&5
8364$as_echo "$with_python" >&6; }
8365
8366
8367#
8368# GSSAPI
8369#
8370{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with GSSAPI support" >&5
8371$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with GSSAPI support... " >&6; }
8372
8373
8374
8375# Check whether --with-gssapi was given.
8376if test "${with_gssapi+set}" = set; then :
8377  withval=$with_gssapi;
8378  case $withval in
8379    yes)
8380
8381
8382$as_echo "#define ENABLE_GSS 1" >>confdefs.h
8383
8384  krb_srvtab="FILE:\$(sysconfdir)/krb5.keytab"
8385
8386      ;;
8387    no)
8388      :
8389      ;;
8390    *)
8391      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-gssapi option" "$LINENO" 5
8392      ;;
8393  esac
8394
8395else
8396  with_gssapi=no
8397
8398fi
8399
8400
8401{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_gssapi" >&5
8402$as_echo "$with_gssapi" >&6; }
8403
8404
8405
8406
8407
8408
8409#
8410# Kerberos configuration parameters
8411#
8412
8413
8414
8415# Check whether --with-krb-srvnam was given.
8416if test "${with_krb_srvnam+set}" = set; then :
8417  withval=$with_krb_srvnam;
8418  case $withval in
8419    yes)
8420      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-krb-srvnam option" "$LINENO" 5
8421      ;;
8422    no)
8423      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-krb-srvnam option" "$LINENO" 5
8424      ;;
8425    *)
8426
8427      ;;
8428  esac
8429
8430else

8432fi

8434
8435
8436

8438#define PG_KRB_SRVNAM "$with_krb_srvnam"
8439_ACEOF
8440
8441
8442
8443#
8444# PAM
8445#
8446{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with PAM support" >&5
8447$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with PAM support... " >&6; }
8448
8449
8450
8451# Check whether --with-pam was given.
8452if test "${with_pam+set}" = set; then :
8453  withval=$with_pam;
8454  case $withval in
8455    yes)
8456
8457$as_echo "#define USE_PAM 1" >>confdefs.h
8458
8459      ;;
8460    no)
8461      :
8462      ;;
8463    *)

8465      ;;
8466  esac
8467
8468else
8469  with_pam=no
8470
8471fi
8472
8473
8474{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_pam" >&5

8476
8477
8478#
8479# BSD AUTH
8480#
8481{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with BSD Authentication support" >&5
8482$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with BSD Authentication support... " >&6; }
8483
8484
8485
8486# Check whether --with-bsd-auth was given.
8487if test "${with_bsd_auth+set}" = set; then :
8488  withval=$with_bsd_auth;
8489  case $withval in
8490    yes)
8491
8492$as_echo "#define USE_BSD_AUTH 1" >>confdefs.h
8493
8494      ;;
8495    no)
8496      :
8497      ;;
8498    *)
8499      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-bsd-auth option" "$LINENO" 5
8500      ;;
8501  esac
8502
8503else
8504  with_bsd_auth=no
8505
8506fi
8507
8508
8509{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_bsd_auth" >&5
8510$as_echo "$with_bsd_auth" >&6; }

8531      :

8535      ;;

8631$as_echo "#define USE_SYSTEMD 1" >>confdefs.h

8661  case $withval in
8662    yes)
8663      :
8664      ;;

8666      :
8667      ;;
8668    *)
8669      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-readline option" "$LINENO" 5
8670      ;;
8671  esac
8672
8673else
8674  with_readline=yes
8675
8676fi
8677
8678
8679# readline on MinGW has problems with backslashes in psql and other bugs.
8680# This is particularly a problem with non-US code pages.
8681# Therefore disable its use until we understand the cause. 2004-07-20
8682if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32"; then
8683  if test "$with_readline" = yes; then
8684    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: *** Readline does not work on MinGW --- disabling" >&5
8685$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: *** Readline does not work on MinGW --- disabling" >&2;}
8686    with_readline=no
8687  fi
8688fi

8720#

8722#
8723{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with liburing support" >&5
8724$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with liburing support... " >&6; }
8725
8726
8727
8728# Check whether --with-liburing was given.
8729if test "${with_liburing+set}" = set; then :
8730  withval=$with_liburing;
8731  case $withval in
8732    yes)
8733
8734$as_echo "#define USE_LIBURING 1" >>confdefs.h
8735
8736      ;;
8737    no)
8738      :
8739      ;;
8740    *)
8741      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-liburing option" "$LINENO" 5
8742      ;;
8743  esac
8744
8745else
8746  with_liburing=no
8747
8748fi
8749
8750
8751{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_liburing" >&5
8752$as_echo "$with_liburing" >&6; }
8753
8754
8755if test "$with_liburing" = yes; then
8756
8757pkg_failed=no
8758{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for liburing" >&5
8759$as_echo_n "checking for liburing... " >&6; }
8760
8761if test -n "$LIBURING_CFLAGS"; then
8762    pkg_cv_LIBURING_CFLAGS="$LIBURING_CFLAGS"
8763 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8764    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
8765    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"liburing\""; } >&5
8766  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "liburing") 2>&5
8767  ac_status=$?
8768  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
8769  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
8770  pkg_cv_LIBURING_CFLAGS=`$PKG_CONFIG --cflags "liburing" 2>/dev/null`
8771		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
8772else
8773  pkg_failed=yes
8774fi
8775 else
8776    pkg_failed=untried
8777fi
8778if test -n "$LIBURING_LIBS"; then
8779    pkg_cv_LIBURING_LIBS="$LIBURING_LIBS"
8780 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8781    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
8782    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"liburing\""; } >&5
8783  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "liburing") 2>&5
8784  ac_status=$?
8785  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
8786  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
8787  pkg_cv_LIBURING_LIBS=`$PKG_CONFIG --libs "liburing" 2>/dev/null`
8788		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
8789else
8790  pkg_failed=yes
8791fi
8792 else
8793    pkg_failed=untried
8794fi
8795
8796
8797
8798if test $pkg_failed = yes; then
8799        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
8800$as_echo "no" >&6; }
8801
8802if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version 0.20; then
8803        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes
8804else
8805        _pkg_short_errors_supported=no
8806fi
8807        if test $_pkg_short_errors_supported = yes; then
8808	        LIBURING_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --short-errors --print-errors --cflags --libs "liburing" 2>&1`
8809        else
8810	        LIBURING_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --print-errors --cflags --libs "liburing" 2>&1`
8811        fi
8812	# Put the nasty error message in config.log where it belongs
8813	echo "$LIBURING_PKG_ERRORS" >&5
8814
8815	as_fn_error $? "Package requirements (liburing) were not met:
8816
8817$LIBURING_PKG_ERRORS
8818
8819Consider adjusting the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable if you
8820installed software in a non-standard prefix.
8821
8822Alternatively, you may set the environment variables LIBURING_CFLAGS
8823and LIBURING_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
8824See the pkg-config man page for more details." "$LINENO" 5
8825elif test $pkg_failed = untried; then
8826        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
8827$as_echo "no" >&6; }
8828	{ { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
8829$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
8830as_fn_error $? "The pkg-config script could not be found or is too old.  Make sure it
8831is in your PATH or set the PKG_CONFIG environment variable to the full
8832path to pkg-config.
8833
8834Alternatively, you may set the environment variables LIBURING_CFLAGS
8835and LIBURING_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
8836See the pkg-config man page for more details.
8837
8838To get pkg-config, see <http://pkg-config.freedesktop.org/>.
8839See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
8840else
8841	LIBURING_CFLAGS=$pkg_cv_LIBURING_CFLAGS
8842	LIBURING_LIBS=$pkg_cv_LIBURING_LIBS
8843        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
8844$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
8845
8846fi
8847fi
8848
8849#
8850# UUID library
8851#
8852# There are at least three UUID libraries in common use: the FreeBSD/NetBSD
8853# library, the e2fsprogs libuuid (now part of util-linux-ng), and the OSSP
8854# UUID library.  More than one of these might be present on a given platform,
8855# so we make the user say which one she wants.
8856#
8857
8858
8859
8860# Check whether --with-uuid was given.
8861if test "${with_uuid+set}" = set; then :
8862  withval=$with_uuid;
8863  case $withval in
8864    yes)
8865      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-uuid option" "$LINENO" 5
8866      ;;
8867    no)
8868      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-uuid option" "$LINENO" 5
8869      ;;
8870    *)
8871
8872      ;;
8873  esac
8874
8875fi
8876
8877
8878if test x"$with_uuid" = x"" ; then
8879  with_uuid=no
8880fi
8881
8882
8883
8884# Check whether --with-ossp-uuid was given.
8885if test "${with_ossp_uuid+set}" = set; then :
8886  withval=$with_ossp_uuid;
8887  case $withval in
8888    yes)
8889      :
8890      ;;
8891    no)
8892      :
8893      ;;
8894    *)
8895      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-ossp-uuid option" "$LINENO" 5
8896      ;;
8897  esac
8898
8899else
8900  with_ossp_uuid=no
8901
8902fi
8903
8904
8905if test "$with_ossp_uuid" = yes ; then
8906  with_uuid=ossp
8907fi
8908
8909if test "$with_uuid" != no ; then
8910  if test "$with_uuid" = bsd ; then
8911
8912$as_echo "#define HAVE_UUID_BSD 1" >>confdefs.h
8913
8914  elif test "$with_uuid" = e2fs ; then
8915
8916$as_echo "#define HAVE_UUID_E2FS 1" >>confdefs.h
8917
8918  elif test "$with_uuid" = ossp ; then
8919
8920$as_echo "#define HAVE_UUID_OSSP 1" >>confdefs.h
8921
8922  else
8923    as_fn_error $? "--with-uuid must specify one of bsd, e2fs, or ossp" "$LINENO" 5
8924  fi
8925fi
8926
8927
8928
8929#
8930# libcurl
8931#
8932{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with libcurl support" >&5
8933$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with libcurl support... " >&6; }
8934
8935
8936
8937# Check whether --with-libcurl was given.
8938if test "${with_libcurl+set}" = set; then :
8939  withval=$with_libcurl;
8940  case $withval in
8941    yes)
8942
8943$as_echo "#define USE_LIBCURL 1" >>confdefs.h
8944
8945      ;;
8946    no)
8947      :
8948      ;;
8949    *)
8950      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-libcurl option" "$LINENO" 5
8951      ;;
8952  esac
8953
8954else
8955  with_libcurl=no
8956
8957fi
8958
8959
8960{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_libcurl" >&5
8961$as_echo "$with_libcurl" >&6; }
8962
8963
8964if test "$with_libcurl" = yes ; then
8965  # Check for libcurl 7.61.0 or higher (corresponding to RHEL8 and the ability
8966  # to explicitly set TLS 1.3 ciphersuites).
8967
8968pkg_failed=no
8969{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for libcurl >= 7.61.0" >&5
8970$as_echo_n "checking for libcurl >= 7.61.0... " >&6; }
8971
8972if test -n "$LIBCURL_CFLAGS"; then
8973    pkg_cv_LIBCURL_CFLAGS="$LIBCURL_CFLAGS"
8974 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8975    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
8976    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"libcurl >= 7.61.0\""; } >&5
8977  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "libcurl >= 7.61.0") 2>&5
8978  ac_status=$?
8979  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
8980  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
8981  pkg_cv_LIBCURL_CFLAGS=`$PKG_CONFIG --cflags "libcurl >= 7.61.0" 2>/dev/null`
8982		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
8983else
8984  pkg_failed=yes
8985fi
8986 else
8987    pkg_failed=untried
8988fi
8989if test -n "$LIBCURL_LIBS"; then
8990    pkg_cv_LIBCURL_LIBS="$LIBCURL_LIBS"
8991 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
8992    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
8993    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"libcurl >= 7.61.0\""; } >&5
8994  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "libcurl >= 7.61.0") 2>&5
8995  ac_status=$?
8996  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
8997  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
8998  pkg_cv_LIBCURL_LIBS=`$PKG_CONFIG --libs "libcurl >= 7.61.0" 2>/dev/null`
8999		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
9000else
9001  pkg_failed=yes
9002fi
9003 else
9004    pkg_failed=untried
9005fi
9006
9007
9008
9009if test $pkg_failed = yes; then
9010        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9011$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9012
9013if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version 0.20; then
9014        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes
9015else
9016        _pkg_short_errors_supported=no
9017fi
9018        if test $_pkg_short_errors_supported = yes; then
9019	        LIBCURL_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --short-errors --print-errors --cflags --libs "libcurl >= 7.61.0" 2>&1`
9020        else
9021	        LIBCURL_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --print-errors --cflags --libs "libcurl >= 7.61.0" 2>&1`
9022        fi
9023	# Put the nasty error message in config.log where it belongs
9024	echo "$LIBCURL_PKG_ERRORS" >&5
9025
9026	as_fn_error $? "Package requirements (libcurl >= 7.61.0) were not met:
9027
9028$LIBCURL_PKG_ERRORS
9029
9030Consider adjusting the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable if you
9031installed software in a non-standard prefix.
9032
9033Alternatively, you may set the environment variables LIBCURL_CFLAGS
9034and LIBCURL_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
9035See the pkg-config man page for more details." "$LINENO" 5
9036elif test $pkg_failed = untried; then
9037        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9038$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9039	{ { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
9040$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
9041as_fn_error $? "The pkg-config script could not be found or is too old.  Make sure it
9042is in your PATH or set the PKG_CONFIG environment variable to the full
9043path to pkg-config.
9044
9045Alternatively, you may set the environment variables LIBCURL_CFLAGS
9046and LIBCURL_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
9047See the pkg-config man page for more details.
9048
9049To get pkg-config, see <http://pkg-config.freedesktop.org/>.
9050See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
9051else
9052	LIBCURL_CFLAGS=$pkg_cv_LIBCURL_CFLAGS
9053	LIBCURL_LIBS=$pkg_cv_LIBCURL_LIBS
9054        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
9055$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
9056
9057fi
9058
9059  # Curl's flags are kept separate from the standard CPPFLAGS/LDFLAGS. We use
9060  # them only for libpq-oauth.
9061  LIBCURL_CPPFLAGS=
9062  LIBCURL_LDFLAGS=
9063
9064  # We only care about -I, -D, and -L switches. Note that -lcurl will be added
9065  # to LIBCURL_LDLIBS by PGAC_CHECK_LIBCURL, below.
9066  for pgac_option in $LIBCURL_CFLAGS; do
9067    case $pgac_option in
9068      -I*|-D*) LIBCURL_CPPFLAGS="$LIBCURL_CPPFLAGS $pgac_option";;
9069    esac
9070  done
9071  for pgac_option in $LIBCURL_LIBS; do
9072    case $pgac_option in
9073      -L*) LIBCURL_LDFLAGS="$LIBCURL_LDFLAGS $pgac_option";;
9074    esac
9075  done
9076
9077
9078
9079
9080  # OAuth requires python for testing
9081  if test "$with_python" != yes; then
9082    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: *** OAuth support tests require --with-python to run" >&5
9083$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: *** OAuth support tests require --with-python to run" >&2;}
9084  fi
9085fi
9086
9087
9088#
9089# libnuma
9090#
9091{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether to build with libnuma support" >&5
9092$as_echo_n "checking whether to build with libnuma support... " >&6; }
9093
9094
9095
9096# Check whether --with-libnuma was given.
9097if test "${with_libnuma+set}" = set; then :
9098  withval=$with_libnuma;
9099  case $withval in
9100    yes)
9101
9102$as_echo "#define USE_LIBNUMA 1" >>confdefs.h
9103
9104      ;;
9105    no)
9106      :
9107      ;;
9108    *)
9109      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-libnuma option" "$LINENO" 5
9110      ;;
9111  esac
9112
9113else
9114  with_libnuma=no
9115
9116fi
9117
9118
9119{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $with_libnuma" >&5
9120$as_echo "$with_libnuma" >&6; }
9121
9122
9123if test "$with_libnuma" = yes ; then
9124  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for numa_available in -lnuma" >&5
9125$as_echo_n "checking for numa_available in -lnuma... " >&6; }
9126if ${ac_cv_lib_numa_numa_available+:} false; then :
9127  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
9128else
9129  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
9130LIBS="-lnuma  $LIBS"
9131cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
9132/* end confdefs.h.  */
9133
9134/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
9135   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
9136   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
9137#ifdef __cplusplus
9138extern "C"
9139#endif
9140char numa_available ();
9141int
9142main ()
9143{
9144return numa_available ();
9145  ;
9146  return 0;
9147}
9148_ACEOF
9149if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
9150  ac_cv_lib_numa_numa_available=yes
9151else
9152  ac_cv_lib_numa_numa_available=no
9153fi
9154rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
9155    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
9156LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
9157fi
9158{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_numa_numa_available" >&5
9159$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_numa_numa_available" >&6; }
9160if test "x$ac_cv_lib_numa_numa_available" = xyes; then :
9161  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
9162#define HAVE_LIBNUMA 1
9163_ACEOF
9164
9165  LIBS="-lnuma $LIBS"
9166
9167else
9168  as_fn_error $? "library 'libnuma' is required for NUMA support" "$LINENO" 5
9169fi
9170
9171
9172pkg_failed=no
9173{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for numa" >&5
9174$as_echo_n "checking for numa... " >&6; }
9175
9176if test -n "$LIBNUMA_CFLAGS"; then
9177    pkg_cv_LIBNUMA_CFLAGS="$LIBNUMA_CFLAGS"
9178 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
9179    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
9180    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"numa\""; } >&5
9181  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "numa") 2>&5
9182  ac_status=$?
9183  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
9184  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
9185  pkg_cv_LIBNUMA_CFLAGS=`$PKG_CONFIG --cflags "numa" 2>/dev/null`
9186		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
9187else
9188  pkg_failed=yes
9189fi
9190 else
9191    pkg_failed=untried
9192fi
9193if test -n "$LIBNUMA_LIBS"; then
9194    pkg_cv_LIBNUMA_LIBS="$LIBNUMA_LIBS"
9195 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
9196    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
9197    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"numa\""; } >&5
9198  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "numa") 2>&5
9199  ac_status=$?
9200  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
9201  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
9202  pkg_cv_LIBNUMA_LIBS=`$PKG_CONFIG --libs "numa" 2>/dev/null`
9203		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
9204else
9205  pkg_failed=yes
9206fi
9207 else
9208    pkg_failed=untried
9209fi
9210
9211
9212
9213if test $pkg_failed = yes; then
9214        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9215$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9216
9217if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version 0.20; then
9218        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes
9219else
9220        _pkg_short_errors_supported=no
9221fi
9222        if test $_pkg_short_errors_supported = yes; then
9223	        LIBNUMA_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --short-errors --print-errors --cflags --libs "numa" 2>&1`
9224        else
9225	        LIBNUMA_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --print-errors --cflags --libs "numa" 2>&1`
9226        fi
9227	# Put the nasty error message in config.log where it belongs
9228	echo "$LIBNUMA_PKG_ERRORS" >&5
9229
9230	as_fn_error $? "Package requirements (numa) were not met:
9231
9232$LIBNUMA_PKG_ERRORS
9233
9234Consider adjusting the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable if you
9235installed software in a non-standard prefix.
9236
9237Alternatively, you may set the environment variables LIBNUMA_CFLAGS
9238and LIBNUMA_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
9239See the pkg-config man page for more details." "$LINENO" 5
9240elif test $pkg_failed = untried; then
9241        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9242$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9243	{ { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
9244$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
9245as_fn_error $? "The pkg-config script could not be found or is too old.  Make sure it
9246is in your PATH or set the PKG_CONFIG environment variable to the full
9247path to pkg-config.
9248
9249Alternatively, you may set the environment variables LIBNUMA_CFLAGS
9250and LIBNUMA_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
9251See the pkg-config man page for more details.
9252
9253To get pkg-config, see <http://pkg-config.freedesktop.org/>.
9254See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
9255else
9256	LIBNUMA_CFLAGS=$pkg_cv_LIBNUMA_CFLAGS
9257	LIBNUMA_LIBS=$pkg_cv_LIBNUMA_LIBS
9258        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
9259$as_echo "yes" >&6; }

9275  case $withval in
9276    yes)

9279
9280      ;;

9282      :
9283      ;;

9292fi

9294

9296$as_echo "$with_libxml" >&6; }
9297
9298
9299if test "$with_libxml" = yes ; then
9300  # Check pkg-config, then xml2-config.  But for backwards compatibility,
9301  # setting XML2_CONFIG overrides pkg-config.
9302    have_libxml2_pkg_config=no
9303  if test -z "$XML2_CONFIG" -a -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
9304
9305pkg_failed=no
9306{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23" >&5
9307$as_echo_n "checking for libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23... " >&6; }
9308
9309if test -n "$XML2_CFLAGS"; then
9310    pkg_cv_XML2_CFLAGS="$XML2_CFLAGS"
9311 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
9312    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
9313    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23\""; } >&5
9314  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23") 2>&5
9315  ac_status=$?
9316  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
9317  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
9318  pkg_cv_XML2_CFLAGS=`$PKG_CONFIG --cflags "libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23" 2>/dev/null`
9319		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
9320else
9321  pkg_failed=yes
9322fi
9323 else
9324    pkg_failed=untried
9325fi
9326if test -n "$XML2_LIBS"; then
9327    pkg_cv_XML2_LIBS="$XML2_LIBS"
9328 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
9329    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
9330    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23\""; } >&5
9331  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23") 2>&5
9332  ac_status=$?
9333  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
9334  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
9335  pkg_cv_XML2_LIBS=`$PKG_CONFIG --libs "libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23" 2>/dev/null`
9336		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
9337else
9338  pkg_failed=yes
9339fi
9340 else
9341    pkg_failed=untried
9342fi
9343
9344
9345
9346if test $pkg_failed = yes; then
9347        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9348$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9349
9350if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version 0.20; then
9351        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes
9352else
9353        _pkg_short_errors_supported=no
9354fi
9355        if test $_pkg_short_errors_supported = yes; then
9356	        XML2_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --short-errors --print-errors --cflags --libs "libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23" 2>&1`
9357        else
9358	        XML2_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --print-errors --cflags --libs "libxml-2.0 >= 2.6.23" 2>&1`
9359        fi
9360	# Put the nasty error message in config.log where it belongs
9361	echo "$XML2_PKG_ERRORS" >&5
9362
9363	# do nothing
9364elif test $pkg_failed = untried; then
9365        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9366$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9367	# do nothing
9368else
9369	XML2_CFLAGS=$pkg_cv_XML2_CFLAGS
9370	XML2_LIBS=$pkg_cv_XML2_LIBS
9371        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
9372$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
9373	have_libxml2_pkg_config=yes
9374fi
9375  fi
9376  if test "$have_libxml2_pkg_config" = no ; then
9377    if test -z "$XML2_CONFIG"; then
9378  for ac_prog in xml2-config
9379do
9380  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
9381set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
9382{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
9383$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
9384if ${ac_cv_path_XML2_CONFIG+:} false; then :
9385  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
9386else
9387  case $XML2_CONFIG in
9388  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
9389  ac_cv_path_XML2_CONFIG="$XML2_CONFIG" # Let the user override the test with a path.
9390  ;;
9391  *)
9392  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
9393for as_dir in $PATH
9394do
9395  IFS=$as_save_IFS
9396  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
9397    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
9398  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
9399    ac_cv_path_XML2_CONFIG="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
9400    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
9401    break 2
9402  fi
9403done
9404  done
9405IFS=$as_save_IFS
9406
9407  ;;
9408esac
9409fi
9410XML2_CONFIG=$ac_cv_path_XML2_CONFIG
9411if test -n "$XML2_CONFIG"; then
9412  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XML2_CONFIG" >&5
9413$as_echo "$XML2_CONFIG" >&6; }
9414else
9415  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9416$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9417fi
9418
9419
9420  test -n "$XML2_CONFIG" && break
9421done
9422
9423else
9424  # Report the value of XML2_CONFIG in configure's output in all cases.
9425  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for XML2_CONFIG" >&5
9426$as_echo_n "checking for XML2_CONFIG... " >&6; }
9427  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XML2_CONFIG" >&5
9428$as_echo "$XML2_CONFIG" >&6; }
9429fi
9430
9431    if test -n "$XML2_CONFIG"; then
9432      XML2_CFLAGS=`$XML2_CONFIG --cflags`
9433      XML2_LIBS=`$XML2_CONFIG --libs`
9434    fi
9435  fi
9436  # Note the user could also set XML2_CFLAGS/XML2_LIBS directly
9437  for pgac_option in $XML2_CFLAGS; do
9438    case $pgac_option in
9439      -I*|-D*) INCLUDES="$INCLUDES $pgac_option";;
9440    esac
9441  done
9442  for pgac_option in $XML2_LIBS; do
9443    case $pgac_option in
9444      -L*) LIBDIRS="$LIBDIRS $pgac_option";;
9445    esac
9446  done
9447fi
9448

9621        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes

9624fi

9627        else

9641and LZ4_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.

9648as_fn_error $? "The pkg-config script could not be found or is too old.  Make sure it

9650path to pkg-config.

9653and LZ4_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.

9656To get pkg-config, see <http://pkg-config.freedesktop.org/>.

9739 elif test -n "$PKG_CONFIG"; then
9740    if test -n "$PKG_CONFIG" && \
9741    { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors \"libzstd >= 1.4.0\""; } >&5
9742  ($PKG_CONFIG --exists --print-errors "libzstd >= 1.4.0") 2>&5
9743  ac_status=$?
9744  $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: \$? = $ac_status" >&5
9745  test $ac_status = 0; }; then
9746  pkg_cv_ZSTD_LIBS=`$PKG_CONFIG --libs "libzstd >= 1.4.0" 2>/dev/null`
9747		      test "x$?" != "x0" && pkg_failed=yes
9748else
9749  pkg_failed=yes
9750fi
9751 else
9752    pkg_failed=untried
9753fi
9754
9755
9756
9757if test $pkg_failed = yes; then
9758        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9759$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9760
9761if $PKG_CONFIG --atleast-pkgconfig-version 0.20; then
9762        _pkg_short_errors_supported=yes
9763else
9764        _pkg_short_errors_supported=no
9765fi
9766        if test $_pkg_short_errors_supported = yes; then
9767	        ZSTD_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --short-errors --print-errors --cflags --libs "libzstd >= 1.4.0" 2>&1`
9768        else
9769	        ZSTD_PKG_ERRORS=`$PKG_CONFIG --print-errors --cflags --libs "libzstd >= 1.4.0" 2>&1`
9770        fi
9771	# Put the nasty error message in config.log where it belongs
9772	echo "$ZSTD_PKG_ERRORS" >&5
9773
9774	as_fn_error $? "Package requirements (libzstd >= 1.4.0) were not met:
9775
9776$ZSTD_PKG_ERRORS
9777
9778Consider adjusting the PKG_CONFIG_PATH environment variable if you
9779installed software in a non-standard prefix.
9780
9781Alternatively, you may set the environment variables ZSTD_CFLAGS
9782and ZSTD_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
9783See the pkg-config man page for more details." "$LINENO" 5
9784elif test $pkg_failed = untried; then
9785        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9786$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9787	{ { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
9788$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
9789as_fn_error $? "The pkg-config script could not be found or is too old.  Make sure it
9790is in your PATH or set the PKG_CONFIG environment variable to the full
9791path to pkg-config.
9792
9793Alternatively, you may set the environment variables ZSTD_CFLAGS
9794and ZSTD_LIBS to avoid the need to call pkg-config.
9795See the pkg-config man page for more details.
9796
9797To get pkg-config, see <http://pkg-config.freedesktop.org/>.
9798See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
9799else
9800	ZSTD_CFLAGS=$pkg_cv_ZSTD_CFLAGS
9801	ZSTD_LIBS=$pkg_cv_ZSTD_LIBS
9802        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
9803$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
9804
9805fi
9806  # We only care about -I, -D, and -L switches;
9807  # note that -lzstd will be added by AC_CHECK_LIB below.
9808  for pgac_option in $ZSTD_CFLAGS; do
9809    case $pgac_option in
9810      -I*|-D*) INCLUDES="$INCLUDES $pgac_option";;
9811    esac
9812  done
9813  for pgac_option in $ZSTD_LIBS; do
9814    case $pgac_option in
9815      -L*) LIBDIRS="$LIBDIRS $pgac_option";;
9816    esac
9817  done
9818fi
9819#
9820# Assignments
9821#
9822
9823CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $INCLUDES"
9824LDFLAGS="$LDFLAGS $LIBDIRS"
9825
9826
9827
9828
9829
9830  if test -n "$ac_tool_prefix"; then
9831  # Extract the first word of "${ac_tool_prefix}strip", so it can be a program name with args.
9832set dummy ${ac_tool_prefix}strip; ac_word=$2
9833{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
9834$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
9835if ${ac_cv_prog_STRIP+:} false; then :
9836  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
9837else
9838  if test -n "$STRIP"; then
9839  ac_cv_prog_STRIP="$STRIP" # Let the user override the test.
9840else
9841as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
9842for as_dir in $PATH
9843do
9844  IFS=$as_save_IFS
9845  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
9846    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
9847  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
9848    ac_cv_prog_STRIP="${ac_tool_prefix}strip"
9849    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
9850    break 2
9851  fi
9852done
9853  done
9854IFS=$as_save_IFS
9855
9856fi
9857fi
9858STRIP=$ac_cv_prog_STRIP
9859if test -n "$STRIP"; then
9860  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $STRIP" >&5
9861$as_echo "$STRIP" >&6; }
9862else
9863  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9864$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9865fi
9866
9867
9868fi
9869if test -z "$ac_cv_prog_STRIP"; then
9870  ac_ct_STRIP=$STRIP
9871  # Extract the first word of "strip", so it can be a program name with args.
9872set dummy strip; ac_word=$2
9873{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
9874$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
9875if ${ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_STRIP+:} false; then :
9876  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
9877else
9878  if test -n "$ac_ct_STRIP"; then
9879  ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_STRIP="$ac_ct_STRIP" # Let the user override the test.
9880else
9881as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
9882for as_dir in $PATH
9883do
9884  IFS=$as_save_IFS
9885  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
9886    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do

9888    ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_STRIP="strip"
9889    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
9890    break 2
9891  fi
9892done
9893  done
9894IFS=$as_save_IFS
9895
9896fi
9897fi
9898ac_ct_STRIP=$ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_STRIP
9899if test -n "$ac_ct_STRIP"; then
9900  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_ct_STRIP" >&5
9901$as_echo "$ac_ct_STRIP" >&6; }
9902else
9903  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9904$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9905fi
9906
9907  if test "x$ac_ct_STRIP" = x; then
9908    STRIP=":"
9909  else
9910    case $cross_compiling:$ac_tool_warned in
9911yes:)
9912{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&5
9913$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&2;}
9914ac_tool_warned=yes ;;
9915esac
9916    STRIP=$ac_ct_STRIP
9917  fi
9918else
9919  STRIP="$ac_cv_prog_STRIP"
9920fi
9921
9922
9923  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether it is possible to strip libraries" >&5
9924$as_echo_n "checking whether it is possible to strip libraries... " >&6; }
9925  if test x"$STRIP" != x"" && "$STRIP" -V 2>&1 | grep "GNU strip" >/dev/null; then
9926    STRIP_STATIC_LIB="$STRIP --strip-unneeded"
9927    STRIP_SHARED_LIB="$STRIP --strip-unneeded"
9928    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
9929$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
9930  else
9931    case $host_os in
9932      darwin*)
9933        STRIP="$STRIP -x"
9934        STRIP_STATIC_LIB=$STRIP
9935        STRIP_SHARED_LIB=$STRIP
9936        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
9937$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
9938        ;;
9939      *)
9940        STRIP_STATIC_LIB=:
9941        STRIP_SHARED_LIB=:
9942        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9943$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9944        ;;
9945    esac
9946  fi
9947
9948
9949
9950if test -n "$ac_tool_prefix"; then
9951  # Extract the first word of "${ac_tool_prefix}ar", so it can be a program name with args.
9952set dummy ${ac_tool_prefix}ar; ac_word=$2
9953{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
9954$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }

9956  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
9957else

9962for as_dir in $PATH

9965  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
9966    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do

9968    ac_cv_prog_AR="${ac_tool_prefix}ar"
9969    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
9970    break 2
9971  fi
9972done
9973  done
9974IFS=$as_save_IFS
9975
9976fi
9977fi
9978AR=$ac_cv_prog_AR

9980  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $AR" >&5
9981$as_echo "$AR" >&6; }
9982else
9983  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
9984$as_echo "no" >&6; }
9985fi
9986
9987
9988fi
9989if test -z "$ac_cv_prog_AR"; then
9990  ac_ct_AR=$AR
9991  # Extract the first word of "ar", so it can be a program name with args.
9992set dummy ar; ac_word=$2
9993{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
9994$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
9995if ${ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_AR+:} false; then :
9996  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
9997else
9998  if test -n "$ac_ct_AR"; then
9999  ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_AR="$ac_ct_AR" # Let the user override the test.
10000else
10001as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10002for as_dir in $PATH
10003do
10004  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10005  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10006    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10007  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10008    ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_AR="ar"
10009    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5

10024$as_echo "no" >&6; }
