10028    AR="ar"
10029  else
10030    case $cross_compiling:$ac_tool_warned in
10031yes:)
10032{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&5
10033$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&2;}
10034ac_tool_warned=yes ;;
10035esac
10036    AR=$ac_ct_AR
10037  fi
10038else
10039  AR="$ac_cv_prog_AR"
10040fi
10041
10042if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32"; then
10043  if test -n "$ac_tool_prefix"; then
10044  # Extract the first word of "${ac_tool_prefix}windres", so it can be a program name with args.
10045set dummy ${ac_tool_prefix}windres; ac_word=$2
10046{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10047$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10048if ${ac_cv_prog_WINDRES+:} false; then :
10049  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10050else
10051  if test -n "$WINDRES"; then
10052  ac_cv_prog_WINDRES="$WINDRES" # Let the user override the test.
10053else
10054as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10055for as_dir in $PATH
10056do
10057  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10058  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10059    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10060  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10061    ac_cv_prog_WINDRES="${ac_tool_prefix}windres"
10062    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10063    break 2
10064  fi
10065done
10066  done
10067IFS=$as_save_IFS
10068
10069fi
10070fi
10071WINDRES=$ac_cv_prog_WINDRES
10072if test -n "$WINDRES"; then
10073  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $WINDRES" >&5
10074$as_echo "$WINDRES" >&6; }
10075else
10076  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10077$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10078fi
10079
10080
10081fi
10082if test -z "$ac_cv_prog_WINDRES"; then
10083  ac_ct_WINDRES=$WINDRES
10084  # Extract the first word of "windres", so it can be a program name with args.
10085set dummy windres; ac_word=$2
10086{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10087$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10088if ${ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_WINDRES+:} false; then :
10089  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10090else
10091  if test -n "$ac_ct_WINDRES"; then
10092  ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_WINDRES="$ac_ct_WINDRES" # Let the user override the test.
10093else
10094as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10095for as_dir in $PATH
10096do
10097  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10098  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10099    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10100  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10101    ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_WINDRES="windres"
10102    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10103    break 2
10104  fi
10105done
10106  done
10107IFS=$as_save_IFS
10108
10109fi
10110fi
10111ac_ct_WINDRES=$ac_cv_prog_ac_ct_WINDRES
10112if test -n "$ac_ct_WINDRES"; then
10113  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_ct_WINDRES" >&5
10114$as_echo "$ac_ct_WINDRES" >&6; }
10115else
10116  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10117$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10118fi
10119
10120  if test "x$ac_ct_WINDRES" = x; then
10121    WINDRES="windres"
10122  else
10123    case $cross_compiling:$ac_tool_warned in
10124yes:)
10125{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&5
10126$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: using cross tools not prefixed with host triplet" >&2;}
10127ac_tool_warned=yes ;;
10128esac
10129    WINDRES=$ac_ct_WINDRES
10130  fi
10131else
10132  WINDRES="$ac_cv_prog_WINDRES"
10133fi
10134
10135fi
10136
10137# Find a good install program.  We prefer a C program (faster),
10138# so one script is as good as another.  But avoid the broken or
10139# incompatible versions:
10140# SysV /etc/install, /usr/sbin/install
10141# SunOS /usr/etc/install
10142# IRIX /sbin/install
10143# AIX /bin/install
10144# AmigaOS /C/install, which installs bootblocks on floppy discs
10145# AIX 4 /usr/bin/installbsd, which doesn't work without a -g flag
10146# AFS /usr/afsws/bin/install, which mishandles nonexistent args
10147# SVR4 /usr/ucb/install, which tries to use the nonexistent group "staff"
10148# OS/2's system install, which has a completely different semantic
10149# ./install, which can be erroneously created by make from ./install.sh.
10150# Reject install programs that cannot install multiple files.
10151{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for a BSD-compatible install" >&5
10152$as_echo_n "checking for a BSD-compatible install... " >&6; }
10153if test -z "$INSTALL"; then
10154if ${ac_cv_path_install+:} false; then :
10155  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10156else
10157  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10158for as_dir in $PATH
10159do
10160  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10161  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10162    # Account for people who put trailing slashes in PATH elements.
10163case $as_dir/ in #((
10164  ./ | .// | /[cC]/* | \
10165  /etc/* | /usr/sbin/* | /usr/etc/* | /sbin/* | /usr/afsws/bin/* | \
10166  ?:[\\/]os2[\\/]install[\\/]* | ?:[\\/]OS2[\\/]INSTALL[\\/]* | \
10167  /usr/ucb/* ) ;;
10168  *)
10169    # OSF1 and SCO ODT 3.0 have their own names for install.
10170    # Don't use installbsd from OSF since it installs stuff as root
10171    # by default.
10172    for ac_prog in ginstall scoinst install; do
10173      for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10174	if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext"; then
10175	  if test $ac_prog = install &&
10176	    grep dspmsg "$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
10177	    # AIX install.  It has an incompatible calling convention.
10178	    :
10179	  elif test $ac_prog = install &&
10180	    grep pwplus "$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
10181	    # program-specific install script used by HP pwplus--don't use.
10182	    :
10183	  else
10184	    rm -rf conftest.one conftest.two conftest.dir
10185	    echo one > conftest.one
10186	    echo two > conftest.two
10187	    mkdir conftest.dir
10188	    if "$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext" -c conftest.one conftest.two "`pwd`/conftest.dir" &&
10189	      test -s conftest.one && test -s conftest.two &&
10190	      test -s conftest.dir/conftest.one &&
10191	      test -s conftest.dir/conftest.two
10192	    then
10193	      ac_cv_path_install="$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext -c"
10194	      break 3
10195	    fi
10196	  fi
10197	fi
10198      done
10199    done
10200    ;;
10201esac
10202
10203  done
10204IFS=$as_save_IFS
10205
10206rm -rf conftest.one conftest.two conftest.dir
10207
10208fi
10209  if test "${ac_cv_path_install+set}" = set; then
10210    INSTALL=$ac_cv_path_install
10211  else
10212    # As a last resort, use the slow shell script.  Don't cache a
10213    # value for INSTALL within a source directory, because that will
10214    # break other packages using the cache if that directory is
10215    # removed, or if the value is a relative name.
10216    INSTALL=$ac_install_sh
10217  fi
10218fi
10219{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $INSTALL" >&5
10220$as_echo "$INSTALL" >&6; }
10221
10222# Use test -z because SunOS4 sh mishandles braces in ${var-val}.
10223# It thinks the first close brace ends the variable substitution.
10224test -z "$INSTALL_PROGRAM" && INSTALL_PROGRAM='${INSTALL}'
10225
10226test -z "$INSTALL_SCRIPT" && INSTALL_SCRIPT='${INSTALL}'
10227
10228test -z "$INSTALL_DATA" && INSTALL_DATA='${INSTALL} -m 644'
10229
10230# When Autoconf chooses install-sh as install program it tries to generate
10231# a relative path to it in each makefile where it substitutes it. This clashes
10232# with our Makefile.global concept. This workaround helps.
10233case $INSTALL in
10234  *install-sh*) install_bin='';;
10235  *) install_bin=$INSTALL;;
10236esac
10237
10238
10239if test -z "$TAR"; then
10240  for ac_prog in tar
10241do
10242  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
10243set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
10244{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10245$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10246if ${ac_cv_path_TAR+:} false; then :
10247  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10248else
10249  case $TAR in
10250  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
10251  ac_cv_path_TAR="$TAR" # Let the user override the test with a path.
10252  ;;
10253  *)
10254  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10255for as_dir in $PATH
10256do
10257  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10258  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10259    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10260  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10261    ac_cv_path_TAR="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
10262    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10263    break 2
10264  fi
10265done
10266  done
10267IFS=$as_save_IFS
10268
10269  ;;
10270esac
10271fi
10272TAR=$ac_cv_path_TAR
10273if test -n "$TAR"; then
10274  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $TAR" >&5
10275$as_echo "$TAR" >&6; }
10276else
10277  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10278$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10279fi
10280
10281
10282  test -n "$TAR" && break
10283done
10284
10285else
10286  # Report the value of TAR in configure's output in all cases.
10287  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for TAR" >&5
10288$as_echo_n "checking for TAR... " >&6; }
10289  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $TAR" >&5
10290$as_echo "$TAR" >&6; }
10291fi
10292
10293{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether ln -s works" >&5
10294$as_echo_n "checking whether ln -s works... " >&6; }
10295LN_S=$as_ln_s
10296if test "$LN_S" = "ln -s"; then
10297  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
10298$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
10299else
10300  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no, using $LN_S" >&5
10301$as_echo "no, using $LN_S" >&6; }
10302fi
10303
10304{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for a thread-safe mkdir -p" >&5
10305$as_echo_n "checking for a thread-safe mkdir -p... " >&6; }
10306if test -z "$MKDIR_P"; then
10307  if ${ac_cv_path_mkdir+:} false; then :
10308  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10309else
10310  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10311for as_dir in $PATH$PATH_SEPARATOR/opt/sfw/bin
10312do
10313  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10314  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10315    for ac_prog in mkdir gmkdir; do
10316	 for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10317	   as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext" || continue
10318	   case `"$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext" --version 2>&1` in #(
10319	     'mkdir (GNU coreutils) '* | \
10320	     'mkdir (coreutils) '* | \
10321	     'mkdir (fileutils) '4.1*)
10322	       ac_cv_path_mkdir=$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext
10323	       break 3;;
10324	   esac
10325	 done
10326       done
10327  done
10328IFS=$as_save_IFS
10329
10330fi
10331
10332  test -d ./--version && rmdir ./--version
10333  if test "${ac_cv_path_mkdir+set}" = set; then
10334    MKDIR_P="$ac_cv_path_mkdir -p"
10335  else
10336    # As a last resort, use the slow shell script.  Don't cache a
10337    # value for MKDIR_P within a source directory, because that will
10338    # break other packages using the cache if that directory is
10339    # removed, or if the value is a relative name.
10340    MKDIR_P="$ac_install_sh -d"
10341  fi
10342fi
10343{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $MKDIR_P" >&5
10344$as_echo "$MKDIR_P" >&6; }
10345
10346# When Autoconf chooses install-sh as mkdir -p program it tries to generate
10347# a relative path to it in each makefile where it substitutes it. This clashes
10348# with our Makefile.global concept. This workaround helps.
10349case $MKDIR_P in
10350  *install-sh*) MKDIR_P='\${SHELL} \${top_srcdir}/config/install-sh -c -d';;
10351esac
10352
10353if test -z "$BISON"; then
10354  for ac_prog in bison
10355do
10356  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
10357set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
10358{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10359$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10360if ${ac_cv_path_BISON+:} false; then :
10361  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10362else
10363  case $BISON in
10364  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
10365  ac_cv_path_BISON="$BISON" # Let the user override the test with a path.
10366  ;;
10367  *)
10368  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10369for as_dir in $PATH
10370do
10371  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10372  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10373    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10374  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10375    ac_cv_path_BISON="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
10376    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10377    break 2
10378  fi
10379done
10380  done
10381IFS=$as_save_IFS
10382
10383  ;;
10384esac
10385fi
10386BISON=$ac_cv_path_BISON
10387if test -n "$BISON"; then
10388  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $BISON" >&5
10389$as_echo "$BISON" >&6; }
10390else
10391  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10392$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10393fi
10394
10395
10396  test -n "$BISON" && break
10397done
10398
10399else
10400  # Report the value of BISON in configure's output in all cases.
10401  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for BISON" >&5
10402$as_echo_n "checking for BISON... " >&6; }
10403  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $BISON" >&5
10404$as_echo "$BISON" >&6; }
10405fi
10406
10407
10408if test "$BISON"; then
10409  pgac_bison_version=`$BISON --version 2>/dev/null | sed q`
10410  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using $pgac_bison_version" >&5
10411$as_echo "$as_me: using $pgac_bison_version" >&6;}
10412  if echo "$pgac_bison_version" | $AWK '{ if ($4 < 2.3) exit 0; else exit 1;}'
10413  then
10414    as_fn_error $? "
10415*** The installed version of Bison, $BISON, is too old to use with PostgreSQL.
10416*** Bison version 2.3 or later is required, but this is $pgac_bison_version." "$LINENO" 5
10417  fi
10418  # Bison >=3.0 issues warnings about %name-prefix="base_yy", instead
10419  # of the now preferred %name-prefix "base_yy", but the latter
10420  # doesn't work with Bison 2.3 or less.  So for now we silence the
10421  # deprecation warnings.
10422  if echo "$pgac_bison_version" | $AWK '{ if ($4 >= 3) exit 0; else exit 1;}'
10423  then
10424    BISONFLAGS="$BISONFLAGS -Wno-deprecated"
10425  fi
10426fi
10427
10428if test -z "$BISON"; then
10429  as_fn_error $? "bison not found" "$LINENO" 5
10430fi
10431
10432
10433if test -z "$FLEX"; then
10434  for ac_prog in flex
10435do
10436  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
10437set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
10438{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10439$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10440if ${ac_cv_path_FLEX+:} false; then :
10441  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10442else
10443  case $FLEX in
10444  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
10445  ac_cv_path_FLEX="$FLEX" # Let the user override the test with a path.
10446  ;;
10447  *)
10448  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10449for as_dir in $PATH
10450do
10451  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10452  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10453    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10454  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10455    ac_cv_path_FLEX="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
10456    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10457    break 2
10458  fi
10459done
10460  done
10461IFS=$as_save_IFS
10462
10463  ;;
10464esac
10465fi
10466FLEX=$ac_cv_path_FLEX
10467if test -n "$FLEX"; then
10468  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $FLEX" >&5
10469$as_echo "$FLEX" >&6; }
10470else
10471  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10472$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10473fi
10474
10475
10476  test -n "$FLEX" && break
10477done
10478
10479else
10480  # Report the value of FLEX in configure's output in all cases.
10481  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for FLEX" >&5
10482$as_echo_n "checking for FLEX... " >&6; }
10483  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $FLEX" >&5
10484$as_echo "$FLEX" >&6; }
10485fi
10486
10487if test -z "$FLEX"; then
10488  as_fn_error $? "flex not found" "$LINENO" 5
10489fi
10490
10491pgac_flex_version=`$FLEX --version 2>/dev/null`
10492{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using $pgac_flex_version" >&5
10493$as_echo "$as_me: using $pgac_flex_version" >&6;}
10494
10495
10496
10497
10498
10499if test -z "$PERL"; then
10500  for ac_prog in perl
10501do
10502  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
10503set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
10504{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10505$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10506if ${ac_cv_path_PERL+:} false; then :
10507  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10508else
10509  case $PERL in
10510  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
10511  ac_cv_path_PERL="$PERL" # Let the user override the test with a path.
10512  ;;
10513  *)
10514  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10515for as_dir in $PATH
10516do
10517  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10518  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10519    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10520  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10521    ac_cv_path_PERL="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
10522    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10523    break 2
10524  fi
10525done
10526  done
10527IFS=$as_save_IFS
10528
10529  ;;
10530esac
10531fi
10532PERL=$ac_cv_path_PERL
10533if test -n "$PERL"; then
10534  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PERL" >&5
10535$as_echo "$PERL" >&6; }
10536else
10537  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10538$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10539fi
10540
10541
10542  test -n "$PERL" && break
10543done
10544
10545else
10546  # Report the value of PERL in configure's output in all cases.
10547  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for PERL" >&5
10548$as_echo_n "checking for PERL... " >&6; }
10549  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PERL" >&5
10550$as_echo "$PERL" >&6; }
10551fi
10552
10553
10554if test "$PERL"; then
10555  pgac_perl_version=`$PERL -v 2>/dev/null | sed -n 's/This is perl.*v[a-z ]*\([0-9]\.[0-9][0-9.]*\).*$/\1/p'`
10556  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using perl $pgac_perl_version" >&5
10557$as_echo "$as_me: using perl $pgac_perl_version" >&6;}
10558  if echo "$pgac_perl_version" | sed 's/[.a-z_]/ /g' | \
10559    $AWK '{ if ($1 == 5 && ($2 >= 14)) exit 1; else exit 0;}'
10560  then
10561    as_fn_error $? "
10562*** The installed version of Perl, $PERL, is too old to use with PostgreSQL.
10563*** Perl version 5.14 or later is required, but this is $pgac_perl_version." "$LINENO" 5
10564  fi
10565fi
10566
10567if test -z "$PERL"; then
10568  as_fn_error $? "Perl not found" "$LINENO" 5
10569fi
10570
10571if test "$with_perl" = yes; then
10572  if test -z "$PERL"; then
10573    as_fn_error $? "Perl not found" "$LINENO" 5
10574  fi
10575
10576{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for Perl archlibexp" >&5
10577$as_echo_n "checking for Perl archlibexp... " >&6; }
10578perl_archlibexp=`$PERL -MConfig -e 'print $Config{archlibexp}'`
10579test "$PORTNAME" = "win32" && perl_archlibexp=`echo $perl_archlibexp | sed 's,\\\\,/,g'`
10580{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $perl_archlibexp" >&5
10581$as_echo "$perl_archlibexp" >&6; }
10582{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for Perl privlibexp" >&5
10583$as_echo_n "checking for Perl privlibexp... " >&6; }
10584perl_privlibexp=`$PERL -MConfig -e 'print $Config{privlibexp}'`
10585test "$PORTNAME" = "win32" && perl_privlibexp=`echo $perl_privlibexp | sed 's,\\\\,/,g'`
10586{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $perl_privlibexp" >&5
10587$as_echo "$perl_privlibexp" >&6; }
10588{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for Perl useshrplib" >&5
10589$as_echo_n "checking for Perl useshrplib... " >&6; }
10590perl_useshrplib=`$PERL -MConfig -e 'print $Config{useshrplib}'`
10591test "$PORTNAME" = "win32" && perl_useshrplib=`echo $perl_useshrplib | sed 's,\\\\,/,g'`
10592{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $perl_useshrplib" >&5
10593$as_echo "$perl_useshrplib" >&6; }
10594  if test "$perl_useshrplib" != yes && test "$perl_useshrplib" != true; then
10595    as_fn_error $? "cannot build PL/Perl because libperl is not a shared library
10596You might have to rebuild your Perl installation.  Refer to the
10597documentation for details.  Use --without-perl to disable building
10598PL/Perl." "$LINENO" 5
10599  fi
10600  # On most platforms, archlibexp is also where the Perl include files live ...
10601  perl_includespec="-I$perl_archlibexp/CORE"
10602  # ... but on newer macOS versions, we must use -iwithsysroot to look
10603  # under $PG_SYSROOT
10604  if test \! -f "$perl_archlibexp/CORE/perl.h" ; then
10605    if test -f "$PG_SYSROOT$perl_archlibexp/CORE/perl.h" ; then
10606      perl_includespec="-iwithsysroot $perl_archlibexp/CORE"
10607    fi
10608  fi
10609
10610{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for CFLAGS recommended by Perl" >&5
10611$as_echo_n "checking for CFLAGS recommended by Perl... " >&6; }
10612perl_ccflags=`$PERL -MConfig -e 'print $Config{ccflags}'`
10613{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $perl_ccflags" >&5
10614$as_echo "$perl_ccflags" >&6; }
10615{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for CFLAGS to compile embedded Perl" >&5
10616$as_echo_n "checking for CFLAGS to compile embedded Perl... " >&6; }
10617perl_embed_ccflags=`$PERL -MConfig -e 'foreach $f (split(" ",$Config{ccflags})) {print $f, " " if ($f =~ /^-D[^_]/ || $f =~ /^-D_USE_32BIT_TIME_T/)}'`
10618{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $perl_embed_ccflags" >&5
10619$as_echo "$perl_embed_ccflags" >&6; }
10620
10621
10622{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for flags to link embedded Perl" >&5
10623$as_echo_n "checking for flags to link embedded Perl... " >&6; }
10624if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32" ; then
10625	perl_lib=`basename $perl_archlibexp/CORE/perl[5-9]*.lib .lib`
10626	if test -e "$perl_archlibexp/CORE/$perl_lib.lib"; then
10627		perl_embed_ldflags="-L$perl_archlibexp/CORE -l$perl_lib"
10628	else
10629		perl_lib=`basename $perl_archlibexp/CORE/libperl[5-9]*.a .a | sed 's/^lib//'`
10630		if test -e "$perl_archlibexp/CORE/lib$perl_lib.a"; then
10631			perl_embed_ldflags="-L$perl_archlibexp/CORE -l$perl_lib"
10632		fi
10633	fi
10634else
10635	pgac_tmp1=`$PERL -MExtUtils::Embed -e ldopts`
10636	pgac_tmp2=`$PERL -MConfig -e 'print "$Config{ccdlflags} $Config{ldflags}"'`
10637	perl_embed_ldflags=`echo X"$pgac_tmp1" | sed -e "s/^X//" -e "s%$pgac_tmp2%%"`
10638fi
10639if test -z "$perl_embed_ldflags" ; then
10640	{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10641$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10642	as_fn_error $? "could not determine flags for linking embedded Perl.
10643This probably means that ExtUtils::Embed or ExtUtils::MakeMaker is not
10644installed." "$LINENO" 5
10645else
10646	{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $perl_embed_ldflags" >&5
10647$as_echo "$perl_embed_ldflags" >&6; }
10648fi
10649
10650fi
10651
10652if test "$with_python" = yes; then
10653  if test -z "$PYTHON"; then
10654  for ac_prog in python3 python
10655do
10656  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
10657set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
10658{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10659$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10660if ${ac_cv_path_PYTHON+:} false; then :
10661  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10662else
10663  case $PYTHON in
10664  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
10665  ac_cv_path_PYTHON="$PYTHON" # Let the user override the test with a path.
10666  ;;
10667  *)
10668  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10669for as_dir in $PATH
10670do
10671  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10672  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10673    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10674  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10675    ac_cv_path_PYTHON="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
10676    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10677    break 2
10678  fi
10679done
10680  done
10681IFS=$as_save_IFS
10682
10683  ;;
10684esac
10685fi
10686PYTHON=$ac_cv_path_PYTHON
10687if test -n "$PYTHON"; then
10688  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PYTHON" >&5
10689$as_echo "$PYTHON" >&6; }
10690else
10691  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10692$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10693fi
10694
10695
10696  test -n "$PYTHON" && break
10697done
10698
10699else
10700  # Report the value of PYTHON in configure's output in all cases.
10701  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for PYTHON" >&5
10702$as_echo_n "checking for PYTHON... " >&6; }
10703  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PYTHON" >&5
10704$as_echo "$PYTHON" >&6; }
10705fi
10706
10707if test x"$PYTHON" = x""; then
10708  as_fn_error $? "Python not found" "$LINENO" 5
10709fi
10710
10711
10712python_fullversion=`${PYTHON} -c "import sys; print(sys.version)" | sed q`
10713{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using python $python_fullversion" >&5
10714$as_echo "$as_me: using python $python_fullversion" >&6;}
10715# python_fullversion is typically n.n.n plus some trailing junk
10716python_majorversion=`echo "$python_fullversion" | sed 's/^\([0-9]*\).*/\1/'`
10717python_minorversion=`echo "$python_fullversion" | sed 's/^[0-9]*\.\([0-9]*\).*/\1/'`
10718python_version=`echo "$python_fullversion" | sed 's/^\([0-9]*\.[0-9]*\).*/\1/'`
10719# Reject unsupported Python versions as soon as practical.
10720if test "$python_majorversion" -lt 3; then
10721  as_fn_error $? "Python version $python_version is too old (version 3 or later is required)" "$LINENO" 5
10722fi
10723
10724{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for Python sysconfig module" >&5
10725$as_echo_n "checking for Python sysconfig module... " >&6; }
10726if "${PYTHON}" -c 'import sysconfig' 2>&5
10727then
10728    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
10729$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
10730else
10731    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10732$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10733    as_fn_error $? "sysconfig module not found" "$LINENO" 5
10734fi
10735
10736{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking Python configuration directory" >&5
10737$as_echo_n "checking Python configuration directory... " >&6; }
10738python_configdir=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print(' '.join(filter(None,sysconfig.get_config_vars('LIBPL'))))"`
10739{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $python_configdir" >&5
10740$as_echo "$python_configdir" >&6; }
10741
10742{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking Python include directory" >&5
10743$as_echo_n "checking Python include directory... " >&6; }
10744python_includespec=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print('-I' + sysconfig.get_config_var('INCLUDEPY'))"`
10745if test "$PORTNAME" = win32 ; then
10746    python_includespec=`echo $python_includespec | sed 's,[\],/,g'`
10747fi
10748{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $python_includespec" >&5
10749$as_echo "$python_includespec" >&6; }
10750
10751
10752
10753{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking how to link an embedded Python application" >&5
10754$as_echo_n "checking how to link an embedded Python application... " >&6; }
10755
10756python_libdir=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print(' '.join(filter(None,sysconfig.get_config_vars('LIBDIR'))))"`
10757python_ldlibrary=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print(' '.join(filter(None,sysconfig.get_config_vars('LDLIBRARY'))))"`
10758
10759# If LDLIBRARY exists and has a shlib extension, use it verbatim.
10760ldlibrary=`echo "${python_ldlibrary}" | sed -e 's/\.so$//' -e 's/\.dll$//' -e 's/\.dylib$//' -e 's/\.sl$//'`
10761if test -e "${python_libdir}/${python_ldlibrary}" -a x"${python_ldlibrary}" != x"${ldlibrary}"
10762then
10763	ldlibrary=`echo "${ldlibrary}" | sed "s/^lib//"`
10764	found_shlib=1
10765else
10766	# Otherwise, guess the base name of the shlib.
10767	# LDVERSION was added in Python 3.2, before that use VERSION,
10768	# or failing that, $python_version from _PGAC_CHECK_PYTHON_DIRS.
10769	python_ldversion=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print(' '.join(filter(None,sysconfig.get_config_vars('LDVERSION'))))"`
10770	if test x"${python_ldversion}" != x""; then
10771		ldlibrary="python${python_ldversion}"
10772	else
10773		python_version_var=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print(' '.join(filter(None,sysconfig.get_config_vars('VERSION'))))"`
10774		if test x"${python_version_var}" != x""; then
10775			ldlibrary="python${python_version_var}"
10776		else
10777			ldlibrary="python${python_version}"
10778		fi
10779	fi
10780	# Search for a likely-looking file.
10781	found_shlib=0
10782	for d in "${python_libdir}" "${python_configdir}" /usr/lib64 /usr/lib
10783	do
10784		if test -e "$d/lib${ldlibrary}${DLSUFFIX}"; then
10785			python_libdir="$d"
10786			found_shlib=1
10787			break 2
10788		fi
10789	done
10790	# Some platforms (OpenBSD) require us to accept a bare versioned shlib
10791	# (".so.n.n") as well. However, check this only after failing to find
10792	# ".so" anywhere, because yet other platforms (Debian) put the .so
10793	# symlink in a different directory from the underlying versioned shlib.
10794	if test "$found_shlib" != 1; then
10795		for d in "${python_libdir}" "${python_configdir}" /usr/lib64 /usr/lib
10796		do
10797			for f in "$d/lib${ldlibrary}.so."* ; do
10798				if test -e "$f"; then
10799					python_libdir="$d"
10800					found_shlib=1
10801					break 2
10802				fi
10803			done
10804		done
10805	fi
10806	# As usual, Windows has its own ideas.  Possible default library
10807	# locations include c:/Windows/System32 and (for Cygwin) /usr/bin,
10808	# and the "lib" prefix might not be there.
10809	if test "$found_shlib" != 1 -a \( "$PORTNAME" = win32 -o "$PORTNAME" = cygwin \); then
10810		for d in "${python_libdir}" "${python_configdir}" c:/Windows/System32 /usr/bin
10811		do
10812			for f in "$d/lib${ldlibrary}.dll" "$d/${ldlibrary}.dll" ; do
10813				if test -e "$f"; then
10814					python_libdir="$d"
10815					found_shlib=1
10816					break 2
10817				fi
10818			done
10819		done
10820	fi
10821fi
10822if test "$found_shlib" != 1; then
10823	as_fn_error $? "could not find shared library for Python
10824You might have to rebuild your Python installation.  Refer to the
10825documentation for details.  Use --without-python to disable building
10826PL/Python." "$LINENO" 5
10827fi
10828python_libspec="-L${python_libdir} -l${ldlibrary}"
10829
10830python_additional_libs=`${PYTHON} -c "import sysconfig; print(' '.join(filter(None,sysconfig.get_config_vars('LIBS','LIBC','LIBM','BASEMODLIBS'))))"`
10831
10832{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: ${python_libspec} ${python_additional_libs}" >&5
10833$as_echo "${python_libspec} ${python_additional_libs}" >&6; }
10834
10835
10836
10837fi
10838
10839if test x"$cross_compiling" = x"yes" && test -z "$with_system_tzdata"; then
10840  if test -z "$ZIC"; then
10841  for ac_prog in zic
10842do
10843  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
10844set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
10845{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
10846$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
10847if ${ac_cv_path_ZIC+:} false; then :
10848  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10849else
10850  case $ZIC in
10851  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
10852  ac_cv_path_ZIC="$ZIC" # Let the user override the test with a path.
10853  ;;
10854  *)
10855  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10856for as_dir in $PATH
10857do
10858  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10859  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10860    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10861  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
10862    ac_cv_path_ZIC="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
10863    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
10864    break 2
10865  fi
10866done
10867  done
10868IFS=$as_save_IFS
10869
10870  ;;
10871esac
10872fi
10873ZIC=$ac_cv_path_ZIC
10874if test -n "$ZIC"; then
10875  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ZIC" >&5
10876$as_echo "$ZIC" >&6; }
10877else
10878  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
10879$as_echo "no" >&6; }
10880fi
10881
10882
10883  test -n "$ZIC" && break
10884done
10885
10886else
10887  # Report the value of ZIC in configure's output in all cases.
10888  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for ZIC" >&5
10889$as_echo_n "checking for ZIC... " >&6; }
10890  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ZIC" >&5
10891$as_echo "$ZIC" >&6; }
10892fi
10893
10894  if test -z "$ZIC"; then
10895    as_fn_error $? "
10896When cross-compiling, either use the option --with-system-tzdata to use
10897existing time-zone data, or set the environment variable ZIC to a zic
10898program to use during the build." "$LINENO" 5
10899  fi
10900fi
10901
10902#
10903# Pthreads
10904#
10905# For each platform, we need to know about any special compile and link
10906# libraries, and whether the normal C function names are thread-safe.
10907# WIN32 doesn't need the pthread tests;  it always uses threads
10908#
10909# These tests are run before the library-tests, because linking with the
10910# other libraries can pull in the pthread functions as a side-effect.  We
10911# want to use the -pthread or similar flags directly, and not rely on
10912# the side-effects of linking with some other library.
10913
10914{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for a sed that does not truncate output" >&5
10915$as_echo_n "checking for a sed that does not truncate output... " >&6; }
10916if ${ac_cv_path_SED+:} false; then :
10917  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10918else
10919            ac_script=s/aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa/bbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbbb/
10920     for ac_i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7; do
10921       ac_script="$ac_script$as_nl$ac_script"
10922     done
10923     echo "$ac_script" 2>/dev/null | sed 99q >conftest.sed
10924     { ac_script=; unset ac_script;}
10925     if test -z "$SED"; then
10926  ac_path_SED_found=false
10927  # Loop through the user's path and test for each of PROGNAME-LIST
10928  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10929for as_dir in $PATH
10930do
10931  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10932  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10933    for ac_prog in sed gsed; do
10934    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10935      ac_path_SED="$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext"
10936      as_fn_executable_p "$ac_path_SED" || continue
10937# Check for GNU ac_path_SED and select it if it is found.
10938  # Check for GNU $ac_path_SED
10939case `"$ac_path_SED" --version 2>&1` in
10940*GNU*)
10941  ac_cv_path_SED="$ac_path_SED" ac_path_SED_found=:;;
10942*)
10943  ac_count=0
10944  $as_echo_n 0123456789 >"conftest.in"
10945  while :
10946  do
10947    cat "conftest.in" "conftest.in" >"conftest.tmp"
10948    mv "conftest.tmp" "conftest.in"
10949    cp "conftest.in" "conftest.nl"
10950    $as_echo '' >> "conftest.nl"
10951    "$ac_path_SED" -f conftest.sed < "conftest.nl" >"conftest.out" 2>/dev/null || break
10952    diff "conftest.out" "conftest.nl" >/dev/null 2>&1 || break
10953    as_fn_arith $ac_count + 1 && ac_count=$as_val
10954    if test $ac_count -gt ${ac_path_SED_max-0}; then
10955      # Best one so far, save it but keep looking for a better one
10956      ac_cv_path_SED="$ac_path_SED"
10957      ac_path_SED_max=$ac_count
10958    fi
10959    # 10*(2^10) chars as input seems more than enough
10960    test $ac_count -gt 10 && break
10961  done
10962  rm -f conftest.in conftest.tmp conftest.nl conftest.out;;
10963esac
10964
10965      $ac_path_SED_found && break 3
10966    done
10967  done
10968  done
10969IFS=$as_save_IFS
10970  if test -z "$ac_cv_path_SED"; then
10971    as_fn_error $? "no acceptable sed could be found in \$PATH" "$LINENO" 5
10972  fi
10973else
10974  ac_cv_path_SED=$SED
10975fi
10976
10977fi
10978{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_path_SED" >&5
10979$as_echo "$ac_cv_path_SED" >&6; }
10980 SED="$ac_cv_path_SED"
10981  rm -f conftest.sed
10982
10983
10984{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for grep that handles long lines and -e" >&5
10985$as_echo_n "checking for grep that handles long lines and -e... " >&6; }
10986if ${ac_cv_path_GREP+:} false; then :
10987  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
10988else
10989  if test -z "$GREP"; then
10990  ac_path_GREP_found=false
10991  # Loop through the user's path and test for each of PROGNAME-LIST
10992  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
10993for as_dir in $PATH$PATH_SEPARATOR/usr/xpg4/bin
10994do
10995  IFS=$as_save_IFS
10996  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
10997    for ac_prog in grep ggrep; do
10998    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
10999      ac_path_GREP="$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext"
11000      as_fn_executable_p "$ac_path_GREP" || continue
11001# Check for GNU ac_path_GREP and select it if it is found.
11002  # Check for GNU $ac_path_GREP
11003case `"$ac_path_GREP" --version 2>&1` in
11004*GNU*)
11005  ac_cv_path_GREP="$ac_path_GREP" ac_path_GREP_found=:;;
11006*)
11007  ac_count=0
11008  $as_echo_n 0123456789 >"conftest.in"
11009  while :
11010  do
11011    cat "conftest.in" "conftest.in" >"conftest.tmp"
11012    mv "conftest.tmp" "conftest.in"
11013    cp "conftest.in" "conftest.nl"
11014    $as_echo 'GREP' >> "conftest.nl"
11015    "$ac_path_GREP" -e 'GREP$' -e '-(cannot match)-' < "conftest.nl" >"conftest.out" 2>/dev/null || break
11016    diff "conftest.out" "conftest.nl" >/dev/null 2>&1 || break
11017    as_fn_arith $ac_count + 1 && ac_count=$as_val
11018    if test $ac_count -gt ${ac_path_GREP_max-0}; then
11019      # Best one so far, save it but keep looking for a better one
11020      ac_cv_path_GREP="$ac_path_GREP"
11021      ac_path_GREP_max=$ac_count
11022    fi
11023    # 10*(2^10) chars as input seems more than enough
11024    test $ac_count -gt 10 && break
11025  done
11026  rm -f conftest.in conftest.tmp conftest.nl conftest.out;;
11027esac
11028
11029      $ac_path_GREP_found && break 3
11030    done
11031  done
11032  done
11033IFS=$as_save_IFS
11034  if test -z "$ac_cv_path_GREP"; then
11035    as_fn_error $? "no acceptable grep could be found in $PATH$PATH_SEPARATOR/usr/xpg4/bin" "$LINENO" 5
11036  fi
11037else
11038  ac_cv_path_GREP=$GREP
11039fi
11040
11041fi
11042{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_path_GREP" >&5
11043$as_echo "$ac_cv_path_GREP" >&6; }
11044 GREP="$ac_cv_path_GREP"
11045
11046
11047{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for egrep" >&5
11048$as_echo_n "checking for egrep... " >&6; }
11049if ${ac_cv_path_EGREP+:} false; then :
11050  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11051else
11052  if echo a | $GREP -E '(a|b)' >/dev/null 2>&1
11053   then ac_cv_path_EGREP="$GREP -E"
11054   else
11055     if test -z "$EGREP"; then
11056  ac_path_EGREP_found=false
11057  # Loop through the user's path and test for each of PROGNAME-LIST
11058  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
11059for as_dir in $PATH$PATH_SEPARATOR/usr/xpg4/bin
11060do
11061  IFS=$as_save_IFS
11062  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
11063    for ac_prog in egrep; do
11064    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
11065      ac_path_EGREP="$as_dir/$ac_prog$ac_exec_ext"
11066      as_fn_executable_p "$ac_path_EGREP" || continue
11067# Check for GNU ac_path_EGREP and select it if it is found.
11068  # Check for GNU $ac_path_EGREP
11069case `"$ac_path_EGREP" --version 2>&1` in
11070*GNU*)
11071  ac_cv_path_EGREP="$ac_path_EGREP" ac_path_EGREP_found=:;;
11072*)
11073  ac_count=0
11074  $as_echo_n 0123456789 >"conftest.in"
11075  while :
11076  do
11077    cat "conftest.in" "conftest.in" >"conftest.tmp"
11078    mv "conftest.tmp" "conftest.in"
11079    cp "conftest.in" "conftest.nl"
11080    $as_echo 'EGREP' >> "conftest.nl"
11081    "$ac_path_EGREP" 'EGREP$' < "conftest.nl" >"conftest.out" 2>/dev/null || break
11082    diff "conftest.out" "conftest.nl" >/dev/null 2>&1 || break
11083    as_fn_arith $ac_count + 1 && ac_count=$as_val
11084    if test $ac_count -gt ${ac_path_EGREP_max-0}; then
11085      # Best one so far, save it but keep looking for a better one
11086      ac_cv_path_EGREP="$ac_path_EGREP"
11087      ac_path_EGREP_max=$ac_count
11088    fi
11089    # 10*(2^10) chars as input seems more than enough
11090    test $ac_count -gt 10 && break
11091  done
11092  rm -f conftest.in conftest.tmp conftest.nl conftest.out;;
11093esac
11094
11095      $ac_path_EGREP_found && break 3
11096    done
11097  done
11098  done
11099IFS=$as_save_IFS
11100  if test -z "$ac_cv_path_EGREP"; then
11101    as_fn_error $? "no acceptable egrep could be found in $PATH$PATH_SEPARATOR/usr/xpg4/bin" "$LINENO" 5
11102  fi
11103else
11104  ac_cv_path_EGREP=$EGREP
11105fi
11106
11107   fi
11108fi
11109{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_path_EGREP" >&5
11110$as_echo "$ac_cv_path_EGREP" >&6; }
11111 EGREP="$ac_cv_path_EGREP"
11112
11113
11114{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for ANSI C header files" >&5
11115$as_echo_n "checking for ANSI C header files... " >&6; }
11116if ${ac_cv_header_stdc+:} false; then :
11117  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11118else
11119  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11120/* end confdefs.h.  */
11121#include <stdlib.h>
11122#include <stdarg.h>
11123#include <string.h>
11124#include <float.h>
11125
11126int
11127main ()
11128{
11129
11130  ;
11131  return 0;
11132}
11133_ACEOF
11134if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
11135  ac_cv_header_stdc=yes
11136else
11137  ac_cv_header_stdc=no
11138fi
11139rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
11140
11141if test $ac_cv_header_stdc = yes; then
11142  # SunOS 4.x string.h does not declare mem*, contrary to ANSI.
11143  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11144/* end confdefs.h.  */
11145#include <string.h>
11146
11147_ACEOF
11148if (eval "$ac_cpp conftest.$ac_ext") 2>&5 |
11149  $EGREP "memchr" >/dev/null 2>&1; then :
11150
11151else
11152  ac_cv_header_stdc=no
11153fi
11154rm -f conftest*
11155
11156fi
11157
11158if test $ac_cv_header_stdc = yes; then
11159  # ISC 2.0.2 stdlib.h does not declare free, contrary to ANSI.
11160  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11161/* end confdefs.h.  */
11162#include <stdlib.h>
11163
11164_ACEOF
11165if (eval "$ac_cpp conftest.$ac_ext") 2>&5 |
11166  $EGREP "free" >/dev/null 2>&1; then :
11167
11168else
11169  ac_cv_header_stdc=no
11170fi
11171rm -f conftest*
11172
11173fi
11174
11175if test $ac_cv_header_stdc = yes; then
11176  # /bin/cc in Irix-4.0.5 gets non-ANSI ctype macros unless using -ansi.
11177  if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
11178  :
11179else
11180  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11181/* end confdefs.h.  */
11182#include <ctype.h>
11183#include <stdlib.h>
11184#if ((' ' & 0x0FF) == 0x020)
11185# define ISLOWER(c) ('a' <= (c) && (c) <= 'z')
11186# define TOUPPER(c) (ISLOWER(c) ? 'A' + ((c) - 'a') : (c))
11187#else
11188# define ISLOWER(c) \
11189		   (('a' <= (c) && (c) <= 'i') \
11190		     || ('j' <= (c) && (c) <= 'r') \
11191		     || ('s' <= (c) && (c) <= 'z'))
11192# define TOUPPER(c) (ISLOWER(c) ? ((c) | 0x40) : (c))
11193#endif
11194
11195#define XOR(e, f) (((e) && !(f)) || (!(e) && (f)))
11196int
11197main ()
11198{
11199  int i;
11200  for (i = 0; i < 256; i++)
11201    if (XOR (islower (i), ISLOWER (i))
11202	|| toupper (i) != TOUPPER (i))
11203      return 2;
11204  return 0;
11205}
11206_ACEOF
11207if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
11208
11209else
11210  ac_cv_header_stdc=no
11211fi
11212rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
11213  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
11214fi
11215
11216fi
11217fi
11218{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_header_stdc" >&5
11219$as_echo "$ac_cv_header_stdc" >&6; }
11220if test $ac_cv_header_stdc = yes; then
11221
11222$as_echo "#define STDC_HEADERS 1" >>confdefs.h
11223
11224fi
11225
11226# On IRIX 5.3, sys/types and inttypes.h are conflicting.
11227for ac_header in sys/types.h sys/stat.h stdlib.h string.h memory.h strings.h \
11228		  inttypes.h stdint.h unistd.h
11229do :
11230  as_ac_Header=`$as_echo "ac_cv_header_$ac_header" | $as_tr_sh`
11231ac_fn_c_check_header_compile "$LINENO" "$ac_header" "$as_ac_Header" "$ac_includes_default
11232"
11233if eval test \"x\$"$as_ac_Header"\" = x"yes"; then :
11234  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
11235#define `$as_echo "HAVE_$ac_header" | $as_tr_cpp` 1
11236_ACEOF
11237
11238fi
11239
11240done
11241
11242
11243if test "$PORTNAME" != "win32"; then :
11244   # then
11245
11246
11247
11248
11249ac_ext=c
11250ac_cpp='$CPP $CPPFLAGS'
11251ac_compile='$CC -c $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
11252ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
11253ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_c_compiler_gnu
11254
11255ax_pthread_ok=no
11256
11257# We used to check for pthread.h first, but this fails if pthread.h
11258# requires special compiler flags (e.g. on Tru64 or Sequent).
11259# It gets checked for in the link test anyway.
11260
11261# First of all, check if the user has set any of the PTHREAD_LIBS,
11262# etcetera environment variables, and if threads linking works using
11263# them:
11264if test "x$PTHREAD_CFLAGS$PTHREAD_LIBS" != "x"; then
11265        ax_pthread_save_CC="$CC"
11266        ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS="$CFLAGS"
11267        ax_pthread_save_LIBS="$LIBS"
11268        if test "x$PTHREAD_CC" != "x"; then :
11269  CC="$PTHREAD_CC"
11270fi
11271        CFLAGS="$CFLAGS $PTHREAD_CFLAGS"
11272        LIBS="$PTHREAD_LIBS $LIBS"
11273        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for pthread_join using $CC $PTHREAD_CFLAGS $PTHREAD_LIBS" >&5
11274$as_echo_n "checking for pthread_join using $CC $PTHREAD_CFLAGS $PTHREAD_LIBS... " >&6; }
11275        cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11276/* end confdefs.h.  */
11277
11278/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
11279   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
11280   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
11281#ifdef __cplusplus
11282extern "C"
11283#endif
11284char pthread_join ();
11285int
11286main ()
11287{
11288return pthread_join ();
11289  ;
11290  return 0;
11291}
11292_ACEOF
11293if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
11294  ax_pthread_ok=yes
11295fi
11296rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
11297    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
11298        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_pthread_ok" >&5
11299$as_echo "$ax_pthread_ok" >&6; }
11300        if test "x$ax_pthread_ok" = "xno"; then
11301                PTHREAD_LIBS=""
11302                PTHREAD_CFLAGS=""
11303        fi
11304        CC="$ax_pthread_save_CC"
11305        CFLAGS="$ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS"
11306        LIBS="$ax_pthread_save_LIBS"
11307fi
11308
11309# We must check for the threads library under a number of different
11310# names; the ordering is very important because some systems
11311# (e.g. DEC) have both -lpthread and -lpthreads, where one of the
11312# libraries is broken (non-POSIX).
11313
11314# Create a list of thread flags to try.  Items starting with a "-" are
11315# C compiler flags, and other items are library names, except for "none"
11316# which indicates that we try without any flags at all, and "pthread-config"
11317# which is a program returning the flags for the Pth emulation library.
11318
11319ax_pthread_flags="pthreads none -Kthread -pthread -pthreads -mthreads pthread --thread-safe -mt pthread-config"
11320
11321# The ordering *is* (sometimes) important.  Some notes on the
11322# individual items follow:
11323
11324# pthreads: AIX (must check this before -lpthread)
11325# none: in case threads are in libc; should be tried before -Kthread and
11326#       other compiler flags to prevent continual compiler warnings
11327# -Kthread: Sequent (threads in libc, but -Kthread needed for pthread.h)
11328# -pthread: Linux/gcc (kernel threads), BSD/gcc (userland threads), Tru64
11329#           (Note: HP C rejects this with "bad form for `-t' option")
11330# -pthreads: Solaris/gcc (Note: HP C also rejects)
11331# -mt: Sun Workshop C (may only link SunOS threads [-lthread], but it
11332#      doesn't hurt to check since this sometimes defines pthreads and
11333#      -D_REENTRANT too), HP C (must be checked before -lpthread, which
11334#      is present but should not be used directly; and before -mthreads,
11335#      because the compiler interprets this as "-mt" + "-hreads")
11336# -mthreads: Mingw32/gcc, Lynx/gcc
11337# pthread: Linux, etcetera
11338# --thread-safe: KAI C++
11339# pthread-config: use pthread-config program (for GNU Pth library)
11340
11341case $host_os in
11342
11343        freebsd*)
11344
11345        # -kthread: FreeBSD kernel threads (preferred to -pthread since SMP-able)
11346        # lthread: LinuxThreads port on FreeBSD (also preferred to -pthread)
11347
11348        ax_pthread_flags="-kthread lthread $ax_pthread_flags"
11349        ;;
11350
11351        hpux*)
11352
11353        # From the cc(1) man page: "[-mt] Sets various -D flags to enable
11354        # multi-threading and also sets -lpthread."
11355
11356        ax_pthread_flags="-mt -pthread pthread $ax_pthread_flags"
11357        ;;
11358
11359        openedition*)
11360
11361        # IBM z/OS requires a feature-test macro to be defined in order to
11362        # enable POSIX threads at all, so give the user a hint if this is
11363        # not set. (We don't define these ourselves, as they can affect
11364        # other portions of the system API in unpredictable ways.)
11365
11366        cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11367/* end confdefs.h.  */
11368
11369#            if !defined(_OPEN_THREADS) && !defined(_UNIX03_THREADS)
11370             AX_PTHREAD_ZOS_MISSING
11371#            endif
11372
11373_ACEOF
11374if (eval "$ac_cpp conftest.$ac_ext") 2>&5 |
11375  $EGREP "AX_PTHREAD_ZOS_MISSING" >/dev/null 2>&1; then :
11376  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: IBM z/OS requires -D_OPEN_THREADS or -D_UNIX03_THREADS to enable pthreads support." >&5
11377$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: IBM z/OS requires -D_OPEN_THREADS or -D_UNIX03_THREADS to enable pthreads support." >&2;}
11378fi
11379rm -f conftest*
11380
11381        ;;
11382
11383        solaris*)
11384
11385        # On Solaris (at least, for some versions), libc contains stubbed
11386        # (non-functional) versions of the pthreads routines, so link-based
11387        # tests will erroneously succeed. (N.B.: The stubs are missing
11388        # pthread_cleanup_push, or rather a function called by this macro,
11389        # so we could check for that, but who knows whether they'll stub
11390        # that too in a future libc.)  So we'll check first for the
11391        # standard Solaris way of linking pthreads (-mt -lpthread).
11392
11393        ax_pthread_flags="-mt,pthread pthread $ax_pthread_flags"
11394        ;;
11395esac
11396
11397# GCC generally uses -pthread, or -pthreads on some platforms (e.g. SPARC)
11398
11399if test "x$GCC" = "xyes"; then :
11400  ax_pthread_flags="-pthread -pthreads $ax_pthread_flags"
11401fi
11402
11403# The presence of a feature test macro requesting re-entrant function
11404# definitions is, on some systems, a strong hint that pthreads support is
11405# correctly enabled
11406
11407case $host_os in
11408        darwin* | hpux* | linux* | osf* | solaris*)
11409        ax_pthread_check_macro="_REENTRANT"
11410        ;;
11411
11412        aix*)
11413        ax_pthread_check_macro="_THREAD_SAFE"
11414        ;;
11415
11416        *)
11417        ax_pthread_check_macro="--"
11418        ;;
11419esac
11420if test "x$ax_pthread_check_macro" = "x--"; then :
11421  ax_pthread_check_cond=0
11422else
11423  ax_pthread_check_cond="!defined($ax_pthread_check_macro)"
11424fi
11425
11426# Are we compiling with Clang?
11427
11428{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether $CC is Clang" >&5
11429$as_echo_n "checking whether $CC is Clang... " >&6; }
11430if ${ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG+:} false; then :
11431  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11432else
11433  ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG=no
11434     # Note that Autoconf sets GCC=yes for Clang as well as GCC
11435     if test "x$GCC" = "xyes"; then
11436        cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11437/* end confdefs.h.  */
11438/* Note: Clang 2.7 lacks __clang_[a-z]+__ */
11439#            if defined(__clang__) && defined(__llvm__)
11440             AX_PTHREAD_CC_IS_CLANG
11441#            endif
11442
11443_ACEOF
11444if (eval "$ac_cpp conftest.$ac_ext") 2>&5 |
11445  $EGREP "AX_PTHREAD_CC_IS_CLANG" >/dev/null 2>&1; then :
11446  ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG=yes
11447fi
11448rm -f conftest*
11449
11450     fi
11451
11452fi
11453{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG" >&5
11454$as_echo "$ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG" >&6; }
11455ax_pthread_clang="$ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG"
11456
11457ax_pthread_clang_warning=no
11458
11459# Clang needs special handling, because older versions handle the -pthread
11460# option in a rather... idiosyncratic way
11461
11462if test "x$ax_pthread_clang" = "xyes"; then
11463
11464        # Clang takes -pthread; it has never supported any other flag
11465
11466        # (Note 1: This will need to be revisited if a system that Clang
11467        # supports has POSIX threads in a separate library.  This tends not
11468        # to be the way of modern systems, but it's conceivable.)
11469
11470        # (Note 2: On some systems, notably Darwin, -pthread is not needed
11471        # to get POSIX threads support; the API is always present and
11472        # active.  We could reasonably leave PTHREAD_CFLAGS empty.  But
11473        # -pthread does define _REENTRANT, and while the Darwin headers
11474        # ignore this macro, third-party headers might not.)
11475
11476        PTHREAD_CFLAGS="-pthread"
11477        PTHREAD_LIBS=
11478
11479        ax_pthread_ok=yes
11480
11481        # However, older versions of Clang make a point of warning the user
11482        # that, in an invocation where only linking and no compilation is
11483        # taking place, the -pthread option has no effect ("argument unused
11484        # during compilation").  They expect -pthread to be passed in only
11485        # when source code is being compiled.
11486        #
11487        # Problem is, this is at odds with the way Automake and most other
11488        # C build frameworks function, which is that the same flags used in
11489        # compilation (CFLAGS) are also used in linking.  Many systems
11490        # supported by AX_PTHREAD require exactly this for POSIX threads
11491        # support, and in fact it is often not straightforward to specify a
11492        # flag that is used only in the compilation phase and not in
11493        # linking.  Such a scenario is extremely rare in practice.
11494        #
11495        # Even though use of the -pthread flag in linking would only print
11496        # a warning, this can be a nuisance for well-run software projects
11497        # that build with -Werror.  So if the active version of Clang has
11498        # this misfeature, we search for an option to squash it.
11499
11500        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether Clang needs flag to prevent \"argument unused\" warning when linking with -pthread" >&5
11501$as_echo_n "checking whether Clang needs flag to prevent \"argument unused\" warning when linking with -pthread... " >&6; }
11502if ${ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG+:} false; then :
11503  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11504else
11505  ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG=unknown
11506             # Create an alternate version of $ac_link that compiles and
11507             # links in two steps (.c -> .o, .o -> exe) instead of one
11508             # (.c -> exe), because the warning occurs only in the second
11509             # step
11510             ax_pthread_save_ac_link="$ac_link"
11511             ax_pthread_sed='s/conftest\.\$ac_ext/conftest.$ac_objext/g'
11512             ax_pthread_link_step=`$as_echo "$ac_link" | sed "$ax_pthread_sed"`
11513             ax_pthread_2step_ac_link="($ac_compile) && (echo ==== >&5) && ($ax_pthread_link_step)"
11514             ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS="$CFLAGS"
11515             for ax_pthread_try in '' -Qunused-arguments -Wno-unused-command-line-argument unknown; do
11516                if test "x$ax_pthread_try" = "xunknown"; then :
11517  break
11518fi
11519                CFLAGS="-Werror -Wunknown-warning-option $ax_pthread_try -pthread $ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS"
11520                ac_link="$ax_pthread_save_ac_link"
11521                cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11522/* end confdefs.h.  */
11523int main(void){return 0;}
11524_ACEOF
11525if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
11526  ac_link="$ax_pthread_2step_ac_link"
11527                     cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11528/* end confdefs.h.  */
11529int main(void){return 0;}
11530_ACEOF
11531if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
11532  break
11533fi
11534rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
11535    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
11536
11537fi
11538rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
11539    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
11540             done
11541             ac_link="$ax_pthread_save_ac_link"
11542             CFLAGS="$ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS"
11543             if test "x$ax_pthread_try" = "x"; then :
11544  ax_pthread_try=no
11545fi
11546             ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG="$ax_pthread_try"
11547
11548fi
11549{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG" >&5
11550$as_echo "$ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG" >&6; }
11551
11552        case "$ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG" in
11553                no | unknown) ;;
11554                *) PTHREAD_CFLAGS="$ax_cv_PTHREAD_CLANG_NO_WARN_FLAG $PTHREAD_CFLAGS" ;;
11555        esac
11556
11557fi # $ax_pthread_clang = yes
11558
11559if test "x$ax_pthread_ok" = "xno"; then
11560for ax_pthread_try_flag in $ax_pthread_flags; do
11561
11562        case $ax_pthread_try_flag in
11563                none)
11564                { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether pthreads work without any flags" >&5
11565$as_echo_n "checking whether pthreads work without any flags... " >&6; }
11566                ;;
11567
11568                -mt,pthread)
11569                { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether pthreads work with -mt -lpthread" >&5
11570$as_echo_n "checking whether pthreads work with -mt -lpthread... " >&6; }
11571                PTHREAD_CFLAGS="-mt"
11572                PTHREAD_LIBS="-lpthread"
11573                ;;
11574
11575                -*)
11576                { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether pthreads work with $ax_pthread_try_flag" >&5
11577$as_echo_n "checking whether pthreads work with $ax_pthread_try_flag... " >&6; }
11578                PTHREAD_CFLAGS="$ax_pthread_try_flag"
11579                ;;
11580
11581                pthread-config)
11582                # Extract the first word of "pthread-config", so it can be a program name with args.
11583set dummy pthread-config; ac_word=$2
11584{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
11585$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
11586if ${ac_cv_prog_ax_pthread_config+:} false; then :
11587  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11588else
11589  if test -n "$ax_pthread_config"; then
11590  ac_cv_prog_ax_pthread_config="$ax_pthread_config" # Let the user override the test.
11591else
11592as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
11593for as_dir in $PATH
11594do
11595  IFS=$as_save_IFS
11596  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
11597    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
11598  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
11599    ac_cv_prog_ax_pthread_config="yes"
11600    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
11601    break 2
11602  fi
11603done
11604  done
11605IFS=$as_save_IFS
11606
11607  test -z "$ac_cv_prog_ax_pthread_config" && ac_cv_prog_ax_pthread_config="no"
11608fi
11609fi
11610ax_pthread_config=$ac_cv_prog_ax_pthread_config
11611if test -n "$ax_pthread_config"; then
11612  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_pthread_config" >&5
11613$as_echo "$ax_pthread_config" >&6; }
11614else
11615  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
11616$as_echo "no" >&6; }
11617fi
11618
11619
11620                if test "x$ax_pthread_config" = "xno"; then :
11621  continue
11622fi
11623                PTHREAD_CFLAGS="`pthread-config --cflags`"
11624                PTHREAD_LIBS="`pthread-config --ldflags` `pthread-config --libs`"
11625                ;;
11626
11627                *)
11628                { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for the pthreads library -l$ax_pthread_try_flag" >&5
11629$as_echo_n "checking for the pthreads library -l$ax_pthread_try_flag... " >&6; }
11630                PTHREAD_LIBS="-l$ax_pthread_try_flag"
11631                ;;
11632        esac
11633
11634        ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS="$CFLAGS"
11635        ax_pthread_save_LIBS="$LIBS"
11636        CFLAGS="$CFLAGS $PTHREAD_CFLAGS"
11637        LIBS="$PTHREAD_LIBS $LIBS"
11638
11639        # Check for various functions.  We must include pthread.h,
11640        # since some functions may be macros.  (On the Sequent, we
11641        # need a special flag -Kthread to make this header compile.)
11642        # We check for pthread_join because it is in -lpthread on IRIX
11643        # while pthread_create is in libc.  We check for pthread_attr_init
11644        # due to DEC craziness with -lpthreads.  We check for
11645        # pthread_cleanup_push because it is one of the few pthread
11646        # functions on Solaris that doesn't have a non-functional libc stub.
11647        # We try pthread_create on general principles.
11648
11649        cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11650/* end confdefs.h.  */
11651#include <pthread.h>
11652#                       if $ax_pthread_check_cond
11653#                        error "$ax_pthread_check_macro must be defined"
11654#                       endif
11655                        static void routine(void *a) { a = 0; }
11656                        static void *start_routine(void *a) { return a; }
11657int
11658main ()
11659{
11660pthread_t th; pthread_attr_t attr;
11661                        pthread_create(&th, 0, start_routine, 0);
11662                        pthread_join(th, 0);
11663                        pthread_attr_init(&attr);
11664                        pthread_cleanup_push(routine, 0);
11665                        pthread_cleanup_pop(0) /* ; */
11666  ;
11667  return 0;
11668}
11669_ACEOF
11670if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
11671  ax_pthread_ok=yes
11672fi
11673rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
11674    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
11675
11676        CFLAGS="$ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS"
11677        LIBS="$ax_pthread_save_LIBS"
11678
11679        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_pthread_ok" >&5
11680$as_echo "$ax_pthread_ok" >&6; }
11681        if test "x$ax_pthread_ok" = "xyes"; then :
11682  break
11683fi
11684
11685        PTHREAD_LIBS=""
11686        PTHREAD_CFLAGS=""
11687done
11688fi
11689
11690# Various other checks:
11691if test "x$ax_pthread_ok" = "xyes"; then
11692        ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS="$CFLAGS"
11693        ax_pthread_save_LIBS="$LIBS"
11694        CFLAGS="$CFLAGS $PTHREAD_CFLAGS"
11695        LIBS="$PTHREAD_LIBS $LIBS"
11696
11697        # Detect AIX lossage: JOINABLE attribute is called UNDETACHED.
11698        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for joinable pthread attribute" >&5
11699$as_echo_n "checking for joinable pthread attribute... " >&6; }
11700if ${ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR+:} false; then :
11701  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11702else
11703  ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR=unknown
11704             for ax_pthread_attr in PTHREAD_CREATE_JOINABLE PTHREAD_CREATE_UNDETACHED; do
11705                 cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11706/* end confdefs.h.  */
11707#include <pthread.h>
11708int
11709main ()
11710{
11711int attr = $ax_pthread_attr; return attr /* ; */
11712  ;
11713  return 0;
11714}
11715_ACEOF
11716if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
11717  ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR=$ax_pthread_attr; break
11718fi
11719rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
11720    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
11721             done
11722
11723fi
11724{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR" >&5
11725$as_echo "$ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR" >&6; }
11726        if test "x$ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR" != "xunknown" && \
11727               test "x$ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR" != "xPTHREAD_CREATE_JOINABLE" && \
11728               test "x$ax_pthread_joinable_attr_defined" != "xyes"; then :
11729
11730cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
11731#define PTHREAD_CREATE_JOINABLE $ax_cv_PTHREAD_JOINABLE_ATTR
11732_ACEOF
11733
11734               ax_pthread_joinable_attr_defined=yes
11735
11736fi
11737
11738        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether more special flags are required for pthreads" >&5
11739$as_echo_n "checking whether more special flags are required for pthreads... " >&6; }
11740if ${ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS+:} false; then :
11741  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11742else
11743  ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS=no
11744             case $host_os in
11745             solaris*)
11746             ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS="-D_POSIX_PTHREAD_SEMANTICS"
11747             ;;
11748             esac
11749
11750fi
11751{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS" >&5
11752$as_echo "$ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS" >&6; }
11753        if test "x$ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS" != "xno" && \
11754               test "x$ax_pthread_special_flags_added" != "xyes"; then :
11755  PTHREAD_CFLAGS="$ax_cv_PTHREAD_SPECIAL_FLAGS $PTHREAD_CFLAGS"
11756               ax_pthread_special_flags_added=yes
11757fi
11758
11759        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT" >&5
11760$as_echo_n "checking for PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT... " >&6; }
11761if ${ax_cv_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT+:} false; then :
11762  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11763else
11764  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11765/* end confdefs.h.  */
11766#include <pthread.h>
11767int
11768main ()
11769{
11770int i = PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT;
11771  ;
11772  return 0;
11773}
11774_ACEOF
11775if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
11776  ax_cv_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT=yes
11777else
11778  ax_cv_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT=no
11779fi
11780rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
11781    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
11782
11783fi
11784{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ax_cv_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT" >&5
11785$as_echo "$ax_cv_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT" >&6; }
11786        if test "x$ax_cv_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT" = "xyes" && \
11787               test "x$ax_pthread_prio_inherit_defined" != "xyes"; then :
11788
11789$as_echo "#define HAVE_PTHREAD_PRIO_INHERIT 1" >>confdefs.h
11790
11791               ax_pthread_prio_inherit_defined=yes
11792
11793fi
11794
11795        CFLAGS="$ax_pthread_save_CFLAGS"
11796        LIBS="$ax_pthread_save_LIBS"
11797
11798        # More AIX lossage: compile with *_r variant
11799        if test "x$GCC" != "xyes"; then
11800            case $host_os in
11801                aix*)
11802                case "x/$CC" in #(
11803  x*/c89|x*/c89_128|x*/c99|x*/c99_128|x*/cc|x*/cc128|x*/xlc|x*/xlc_v6|x*/xlc128|x*/xlc128_v6) :
11804    #handle absolute path differently from PATH based program lookup
11805                     case "x$CC" in #(
11806  x/*) :
11807    if as_fn_executable_p ${CC}_r; then :
11808  PTHREAD_CC="${CC}_r"
11809fi ;; #(
11810  *) :
11811    for ac_prog in ${CC}_r
11812do
11813  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
11814set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
11815{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
11816$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
11817if ${ac_cv_prog_PTHREAD_CC+:} false; then :
11818  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11819else
11820  if test -n "$PTHREAD_CC"; then
11821  ac_cv_prog_PTHREAD_CC="$PTHREAD_CC" # Let the user override the test.
11822else
11823as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
11824for as_dir in $PATH
11825do
11826  IFS=$as_save_IFS
11827  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
11828    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
11829  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
11830    ac_cv_prog_PTHREAD_CC="$ac_prog"
11831    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
11832    break 2
11833  fi
11834done
11835  done
11836IFS=$as_save_IFS
11837
11838fi
11839fi
11840PTHREAD_CC=$ac_cv_prog_PTHREAD_CC
11841if test -n "$PTHREAD_CC"; then
11842  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PTHREAD_CC" >&5
11843$as_echo "$PTHREAD_CC" >&6; }
11844else
11845  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
11846$as_echo "no" >&6; }
11847fi
11848
11849
11850  test -n "$PTHREAD_CC" && break
11851done
11852test -n "$PTHREAD_CC" || PTHREAD_CC="$CC"
11853 ;;
11854esac ;; #(
11855  *) :
11856     ;;
11857esac
11858                ;;
11859            esac
11860        fi
11861fi
11862
11863test -n "$PTHREAD_CC" || PTHREAD_CC="$CC"
11864
11865
11866
11867
11868
11869# Finally, execute ACTION-IF-FOUND/ACTION-IF-NOT-FOUND:
11870if test "x$ax_pthread_ok" = "xyes"; then
11871
11872$as_echo "#define HAVE_PTHREAD 1" >>confdefs.h
11873
11874        :
11875else
11876        ax_pthread_ok=no
11877
11878fi
11879ac_ext=c
11880ac_cpp='$CPP $CPPFLAGS'
11881ac_compile='$CC -c $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext >&5'
11882ac_link='$CC -o conftest$ac_exeext $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS conftest.$ac_ext $LIBS >&5'
11883ac_compiler_gnu=$ac_cv_c_compiler_gnu
11884
11885	# set thread flags
11886
11887# Some platforms use these, so just define them.  They can't hurt if they
11888# are not supported.
11889PTHREAD_CFLAGS="$PTHREAD_CFLAGS -D_REENTRANT -D_THREAD_SAFE"
11890
11891# Check for *_r functions
11892_CFLAGS="$CFLAGS"
11893_LIBS="$LIBS"
11894CFLAGS="$CFLAGS $PTHREAD_CFLAGS"
11895LIBS="$LIBS $PTHREAD_LIBS"
11896
11897ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "pthread.h" "ac_cv_header_pthread_h" "$ac_includes_default"
11898if test "x$ac_cv_header_pthread_h" = xyes; then :
11899
11900else
11901  as_fn_error $? "
11902pthread.h not found" "$LINENO" 5
11903fi
11904
11905
11906
11907for ac_func in strerror_r
11908do :
11909  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "strerror_r" "ac_cv_func_strerror_r"
11910if test "x$ac_cv_func_strerror_r" = xyes; then :
11911  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
11912#define HAVE_STRERROR_R 1
11913_ACEOF
11914
11915fi
11916done
11917
11918
11919# Do test here with the proper thread flags
11920{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether strerror_r returns int" >&5
11921$as_echo_n "checking whether strerror_r returns int... " >&6; }
11922if ${pgac_cv_func_strerror_r_int+:} false; then :
11923  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11924else
11925  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11926/* end confdefs.h.  */
11927#include <string.h>
11928int
11929main ()
11930{
11931char buf[100];
11932  switch (strerror_r(1, buf, sizeof(buf)))
11933  { case 0: break; default: break; }
11934
11935  ;
11936  return 0;
11937}
11938_ACEOF
11939if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
11940  pgac_cv_func_strerror_r_int=yes
11941else
11942  pgac_cv_func_strerror_r_int=no
11943fi
11944rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
11945fi
11946{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_func_strerror_r_int" >&5
11947$as_echo "$pgac_cv_func_strerror_r_int" >&6; }
11948if test x"$pgac_cv_func_strerror_r_int" = xyes ; then
11949
11950$as_echo "#define STRERROR_R_INT 1" >>confdefs.h
11951
11952fi
11953
11954
11955CFLAGS="$_CFLAGS"
11956LIBS="$_LIBS"
11957
11958
11959else
11960   # else
11961# do not use values from template file
11962PTHREAD_CFLAGS=
11963PTHREAD_LIBS=
11964
11965fi # fi
11966
11967
11968
11969
11970
11971##
11972## Libraries
11973##
11974## Most libraries are included only if they demonstrably provide a function
11975## we need, but libm is an exception: always include it, because there are
11976## too many compilers that play cute optimization games that will break
11977## probes for standard functions such as pow().
11978##
11979
11980{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for main in -lm" >&5
11981$as_echo_n "checking for main in -lm... " >&6; }
11982if ${ac_cv_lib_m_main+:} false; then :
11983  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
11984else
11985  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
11986LIBS="-lm  $LIBS"
11987cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
11988/* end confdefs.h.  */
11989
11990
11991int
11992main ()
11993{
11994return main ();
11995  ;
11996  return 0;
11997}
11998_ACEOF
11999if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12000  ac_cv_lib_m_main=yes
12001else
12002  ac_cv_lib_m_main=no
12003fi
12004rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12005    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
12006LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
12007fi
12008{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_m_main" >&5
12009$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_m_main" >&6; }
12010if test "x$ac_cv_lib_m_main" = xyes; then :
12011  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
12012#define HAVE_LIBM 1
12013_ACEOF
12014
12015  LIBS="-lm $LIBS"
12016
12017fi
12018
12019{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing setproctitle" >&5
12020$as_echo_n "checking for library containing setproctitle... " >&6; }
12021if ${ac_cv_search_setproctitle+:} false; then :
12022  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12023else
12024  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12025cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12026/* end confdefs.h.  */
12027
12028/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12029   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12030   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12031#ifdef __cplusplus
12032extern "C"
12033#endif
12034char setproctitle ();
12035int
12036main ()
12037{
12038return setproctitle ();
12039  ;
12040  return 0;
12041}
12042_ACEOF
12043for ac_lib in '' util; do
12044  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
12045    ac_res="none required"
12046  else
12047    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
12048    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
12049  fi
12050  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12051  ac_cv_search_setproctitle=$ac_res
12052fi
12053rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12054    conftest$ac_exeext
12055  if ${ac_cv_search_setproctitle+:} false; then :
12056  break
12057fi
12058done
12059if ${ac_cv_search_setproctitle+:} false; then :
12060
12061else
12062  ac_cv_search_setproctitle=no
12063fi
12064rm conftest.$ac_ext
12065LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
12066fi
12067{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_setproctitle" >&5
12068$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_setproctitle" >&6; }
12069ac_res=$ac_cv_search_setproctitle
12070if test "$ac_res" != no; then :
12071  test "$ac_res" = "none required" || LIBS="$ac_res $LIBS"
12072
12073fi
12074
12075# gcc/clang's sanitizer helper library provides dlopen but not dlsym, thus
12076# when enabling asan the dlopen check doesn't notice that -ldl is actually
12077# required. Just checking for dlsym() ought to suffice.
12078{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing dlsym" >&5
12079$as_echo_n "checking for library containing dlsym... " >&6; }
12080if ${ac_cv_search_dlsym+:} false; then :
12081  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12082else
12083  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12084cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12085/* end confdefs.h.  */
12086
12087/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12088   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12089   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12090#ifdef __cplusplus
12091extern "C"
12092#endif
12093char dlsym ();
12094int
12095main ()
12096{
12097return dlsym ();
12098  ;
12099  return 0;
12100}
12101_ACEOF
12102for ac_lib in '' dl; do
12103  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
12104    ac_res="none required"
12105  else

12108  fi

12111fi
12112rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12113    conftest$ac_exeext
12114  if ${ac_cv_search_dlsym+:} false; then :
12115  break
12116fi
12117done
12118if ${ac_cv_search_dlsym+:} false; then :
12119
12120else
12121  ac_cv_search_dlsym=no
12122fi
12123rm conftest.$ac_ext
12124LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
12125fi
12126{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_dlsym" >&5
12127$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_dlsym" >&6; }

12406{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_clock_gettime" >&5
12407$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_clock_gettime" >&6; }
12408ac_res=$ac_cv_search_clock_gettime
12409if test "$ac_res" != no; then :
12410  test "$ac_res" = "none required" || LIBS="$ac_res $LIBS"
12411
12412fi
12413
12414# Cygwin:
12415{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing shmget" >&5
12416$as_echo_n "checking for library containing shmget... " >&6; }
12417if ${ac_cv_search_shmget+:} false; then :
12418  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12419else
12420  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12421cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12422/* end confdefs.h.  */
12423
12424/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12425   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12426   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12427#ifdef __cplusplus
12428extern "C"
12429#endif
12430char shmget ();
12431int
12432main ()
12433{
12434return shmget ();
12435  ;
12436  return 0;
12437}
12438_ACEOF
12439for ac_lib in '' cygipc; do
12440  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
12441    ac_res="none required"
12442  else
12443    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
12444    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
12445  fi
12446  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12447  ac_cv_search_shmget=$ac_res
12448fi
12449rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12450    conftest$ac_exeext
12451  if ${ac_cv_search_shmget+:} false; then :
12452  break
12453fi
12454done
12455if ${ac_cv_search_shmget+:} false; then :
12456
12457else
12458  ac_cv_search_shmget=no
12459fi
12460rm conftest.$ac_ext
12461LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
12462fi
12463{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_shmget" >&5
12464$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_shmget" >&6; }
12465ac_res=$ac_cv_search_shmget

12473$as_echo_n "checking for library containing backtrace_symbols... " >&6; }
