12476else
12477  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12478cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12479/* end confdefs.h.  */
12480
12481/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12482   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12483   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12484#ifdef __cplusplus
12485extern "C"
12486#endif
12487char backtrace_symbols ();
12488int
12489main ()
12490{
12491return backtrace_symbols ();
12492  ;
12493  return 0;
12494}
12495_ACEOF
12496for ac_lib in '' execinfo; do
12497  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
12498    ac_res="none required"
12499  else
12500    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
12501    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
12502  fi
12503  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12504  ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols=$ac_res
12505fi
12506rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12507    conftest$ac_exeext
12508  if ${ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols+:} false; then :
12509  break
12510fi
12511done
12512if ${ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols+:} false; then :
12513
12514else
12515  ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols=no
12516fi
12517rm conftest.$ac_ext
12518LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
12519fi
12520{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols" >&5
12521$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols" >&6; }
12522ac_res=$ac_cv_search_backtrace_symbols

12528
12529{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing pthread_barrier_wait" >&5
12530$as_echo_n "checking for library containing pthread_barrier_wait... " >&6; }

12532  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12533else

12535cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12536/* end confdefs.h.  */
12537
12538/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12539   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12540   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12541#ifdef __cplusplus
12542extern "C"
12543#endif
12544char pthread_barrier_wait ();
12545int
12546main ()
12547{
12548return pthread_barrier_wait ();
12549  ;
12550  return 0;
12551}
12552_ACEOF
12553for ac_lib in '' pthread; do
12554  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
12555    ac_res="none required"
12556  else
12557    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
12558    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"

12583fi
12584
12585
12586if test "$with_readline" = yes; then
12587
12588
12589{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing readline" >&5
12590$as_echo_n "checking for library containing readline... " >&6; }
12591if ${pgac_cv_check_readline+:} false; then :
12592  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12593else
12594  pgac_cv_check_readline=no
12595pgac_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12596if test x"$with_libedit_preferred" != x"yes"
12597then	READLINE_ORDER="-lreadline -ledit"
12598else	READLINE_ORDER="-ledit -lreadline"
12599fi
12600for pgac_rllib in $READLINE_ORDER ; do
12601  for pgac_lib in "" " -ltermcap" " -lncurses" " -lcurses" ; do
12602    LIBS="${pgac_rllib}${pgac_lib} $pgac_save_LIBS"
12603    cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12604/* end confdefs.h.  */
12605
12606/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12607   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12608   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12609#ifdef __cplusplus
12610extern "C"
12611#endif
12612char readline ();
12613int
12614main ()
12615{
12616return readline ();
12617  ;
12618  return 0;
12619}
12620_ACEOF
12621if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12622
12623      # Older NetBSD and OpenBSD have a broken linker that does not
12624      # recognize dependent libraries; assume curses is needed if we didn't
12625      # find any dependency.
12626      case $host_os in
12627        netbsd* | openbsd*)
12628          if test x"$pgac_lib" = x"" ; then
12629            pgac_lib=" -lcurses"
12630          fi ;;
12631      esac
12632
12633      pgac_cv_check_readline="${pgac_rllib}${pgac_lib}"
12634      break
12635
12636fi
12637rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12638    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
12639  done
12640  if test "$pgac_cv_check_readline" != no ; then
12641    break
12642  fi
12643done
12644LIBS=$pgac_save_LIBS
12645
12646fi
12647{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_check_readline" >&5
12648$as_echo "$pgac_cv_check_readline" >&6; }
12649if test "$pgac_cv_check_readline" != no ; then
12650  LIBS="$pgac_cv_check_readline $LIBS"
12651
12652$as_echo "#define HAVE_LIBREADLINE 1" >>confdefs.h
12653
12654fi
12655
12656
12657  if test x"$pgac_cv_check_readline" = x"no"; then
12658    as_fn_error $? "readline library not found
12659If you have readline already installed, see config.log for details on the
12660failure.  It is possible the compiler isn't looking in the proper directory.
12661Use --without-readline to disable readline support." "$LINENO" 5
12662  fi
12663fi
12664
12665if test "$with_zlib" = yes; then
12666  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for inflate in -lz" >&5
12667$as_echo_n "checking for inflate in -lz... " >&6; }
12668if ${ac_cv_lib_z_inflate+:} false; then :
12669  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12670else
12671  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12672LIBS="-lz  $LIBS"
12673cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12674/* end confdefs.h.  */
12675
12676/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12677   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12678   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12679#ifdef __cplusplus
12680extern "C"
12681#endif
12682char inflate ();
12683int
12684main ()
12685{
12686return inflate ();
12687  ;
12688  return 0;
12689}
12690_ACEOF
12691if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12692  ac_cv_lib_z_inflate=yes
12693else
12694  ac_cv_lib_z_inflate=no
12695fi
12696rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12697    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
12698LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
12699fi
12700{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_z_inflate" >&5
12701$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_z_inflate" >&6; }
12702if test "x$ac_cv_lib_z_inflate" = xyes; then :
12703  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
12704#define HAVE_LIBZ 1
12705_ACEOF
12706
12707  LIBS="-lz $LIBS"
12708
12709else
12710  as_fn_error $? "zlib library not found
12711If you have zlib already installed, see config.log for details on the
12712failure.  It is possible the compiler isn't looking in the proper directory.
12713Use --without-zlib to disable zlib support." "$LINENO" 5
12714fi
12715
12716fi
12717
12718if test "$with_libcurl" = yes ; then
12719
12720  # libcurl compiler/linker flags are kept separate from the global flags, so
12721  # they have to be added back temporarily for the following tests.
12722  pgac_save_CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS
12723  pgac_save_LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS
12724  pgac_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12725
12726  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $LIBCURL_CPPFLAGS"
12727  LDFLAGS="$LDFLAGS $LIBCURL_LDFLAGS"
12728
12729  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "curl/curl.h" "ac_cv_header_curl_curl_h" "$ac_includes_default"
12730if test "x$ac_cv_header_curl_curl_h" = xyes; then :
12731
12732else
12733  as_fn_error $? "header file <curl/curl.h> is required for --with-libcurl" "$LINENO" 5
12734fi
12735
12736
12737
12738  # LIBCURL_LDLIBS is determined here. Like the compiler flags, it should not
12739  # pollute the global LIBS setting.
12740  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for curl_multi_init in -lcurl" >&5
12741$as_echo_n "checking for curl_multi_init in -lcurl... " >&6; }
12742if ${ac_cv_lib_curl_curl_multi_init+:} false; then :
12743  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12744else
12745  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12746LIBS="-lcurl  $LIBS"
12747cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12748/* end confdefs.h.  */
12749
12750/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12751   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12752   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12753#ifdef __cplusplus
12754extern "C"
12755#endif
12756char curl_multi_init ();
12757int
12758main ()
12759{
12760return curl_multi_init ();
12761  ;
12762  return 0;
12763}
12764_ACEOF
12765if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12766  ac_cv_lib_curl_curl_multi_init=yes
12767else
12768  ac_cv_lib_curl_curl_multi_init=no
12769fi
12770rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12771    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
12772LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
12773fi
12774{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_curl_curl_multi_init" >&5
12775$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_curl_curl_multi_init" >&6; }
12776if test "x$ac_cv_lib_curl_curl_multi_init" = xyes; then :
12777
12778
12779$as_echo "#define HAVE_LIBCURL 1" >>confdefs.h
12780
12781				 LIBCURL_LDLIBS=-lcurl
12782
12783
12784else
12785  as_fn_error $? "library 'curl' does not provide curl_multi_init" "$LINENO" 5
12786fi
12787
12788
12789  LIBS="$LIBCURL_LDLIBS $LIBS"
12790
12791  # Check to see whether the current platform supports threadsafe Curl
12792  # initialization.
12793  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for curl_global_init thread safety" >&5
12794$as_echo_n "checking for curl_global_init thread safety... " >&6; }
12795if ${pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init+:} false; then :
12796  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12797else
12798  if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
12799  pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init=unknown
12800else
12801  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12802/* end confdefs.h.  */
12803
12804#include <curl/curl.h>
12805
12806int
12807main ()
12808{
12809
12810    curl_version_info_data *info;
12811
12812    if (curl_global_init(CURL_GLOBAL_ALL))
12813        return -1;
12814
12815    info = curl_version_info(CURLVERSION_NOW);
12816#ifdef CURL_VERSION_THREADSAFE
12817    if (info->features & CURL_VERSION_THREADSAFE)
12818        return 0;
12819#endif
12820
12821    return 1;
12822
12823  ;
12824  return 0;
12825}
12826_ACEOF
12827if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
12828  pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init=yes
12829else
12830  pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init=no
12831fi
12832rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
12833  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
12834fi
12835
12836fi
12837{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init" >&5
12838$as_echo "$pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init" >&6; }
12839  if test x"$pgac_cv__libcurl_threadsafe_init" = xyes ; then
12840
12841$as_echo "#define HAVE_THREADSAFE_CURL_GLOBAL_INIT 1" >>confdefs.h
12842
12843  fi
12844
12845  # Fail if a thread-friendly DNS resolver isn't built.
12846  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for curl support for asynchronous DNS" >&5
12847$as_echo_n "checking for curl support for asynchronous DNS... " >&6; }
12848if ${pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns+:} false; then :
12849  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12850else
12851  if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
12852  pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns=unknown
12853else
12854  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12855/* end confdefs.h.  */
12856
12857#include <curl/curl.h>
12858
12859int
12860main ()
12861{
12862
12863    curl_version_info_data *info;
12864
12865    if (curl_global_init(CURL_GLOBAL_ALL))
12866        return -1;
12867
12868    info = curl_version_info(CURLVERSION_NOW);
12869    return (info->features & CURL_VERSION_ASYNCHDNS) ? 0 : 1;
12870
12871  ;
12872  return 0;
12873}
12874_ACEOF
12875if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
12876  pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns=yes
12877else
12878  pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns=no
12879fi
12880rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
12881  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
12882fi
12883
12884fi
12885{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns" >&5
12886$as_echo "$pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns" >&6; }
12887  if test x"$pgac_cv__libcurl_async_dns" = xno ; then
12888    as_fn_error $? "
12889*** The installed version of libcurl does not support asynchronous DNS
12890*** lookups. Rebuild libcurl with the AsynchDNS feature enabled in order
12891*** to use it with libpq." "$LINENO" 5
12892  fi
12893
12894  CPPFLAGS=$pgac_save_CPPFLAGS
12895  LDFLAGS=$pgac_save_LDFLAGS
12896  LIBS=$pgac_save_LIBS
12897
12898fi
12899
12900if test "$with_gssapi" = yes ; then
12901  if test "$PORTNAME" != "win32"; then
12902    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing gss_store_cred_into" >&5
12903$as_echo_n "checking for library containing gss_store_cred_into... " >&6; }
12904if ${ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into+:} false; then :
12905  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
12906else
12907  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
12908cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
12909/* end confdefs.h.  */
12910
12911/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
12912   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
12913   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
12914#ifdef __cplusplus
12915extern "C"
12916#endif
12917char gss_store_cred_into ();
12918int
12919main ()
12920{
12921return gss_store_cred_into ();
12922  ;
12923  return 0;
12924}
12925_ACEOF
12926for ac_lib in '' gssapi_krb5 gss 'gssapi -lkrb5 -lcrypto'; do
12927  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
12928    ac_res="none required"
12929  else
12930    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
12931    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
12932  fi
12933  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
12934  ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into=$ac_res
12935fi
12936rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
12937    conftest$ac_exeext
12938  if ${ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into+:} false; then :
12939  break
12940fi
12941done
12942if ${ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into+:} false; then :
12943
12944else
12945  ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into=no
12946fi
12947rm conftest.$ac_ext
12948LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
12949fi
12950{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into" >&5
12951$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into" >&6; }
12952ac_res=$ac_cv_search_gss_store_cred_into
12953if test "$ac_res" != no; then :
12954  test "$ac_res" = "none required" || LIBS="$ac_res $LIBS"
12955
12956else
12957  as_fn_error $? "could not find function 'gss_store_cred_into' required for GSSAPI" "$LINENO" 5
12958fi
12959
12960  else
12961    LIBS="$LIBS -lgssapi32"
12962  fi
12963fi
12964
12965#
12966# SSL Library
12967#
12968# There is currently only one supported SSL/TLS library: OpenSSL.
12969#
12970
12971
12972
12973# Check whether --with-ssl was given.
12974if test "${with_ssl+set}" = set; then :
12975  withval=$with_ssl;
12976  case $withval in
12977    yes)
12978      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-ssl option" "$LINENO" 5
12979      ;;
12980    no)
12981      as_fn_error $? "argument required for --with-ssl option" "$LINENO" 5
12982      ;;
12983    *)
12984
12985      ;;
12986  esac
12987
12988fi
12989
12990
12991if test x"$with_ssl" = x"" ; then
12992  with_ssl=no
12993fi
12994
12995
12996
12997# Check whether --with-openssl was given.
12998if test "${with_openssl+set}" = set; then :
12999  withval=$with_openssl;
13000  case $withval in
13001    yes)
13002      :
13003      ;;
13004    no)
13005      :
13006      ;;
13007    *)
13008      as_fn_error $? "no argument expected for --with-openssl option" "$LINENO" 5
13009      ;;
13010  esac
13011
13012else
13013  with_openssl=no
13014
13015fi
13016
13017
13018if test "$with_openssl" = yes ; then
13019  with_ssl=openssl
13020fi
13021
13022if test "$with_ssl" = openssl ; then
13023    # Minimum required OpenSSL version is 1.1.1
13024
13025$as_echo "#define OPENSSL_API_COMPAT 0x10101000L" >>confdefs.h
13026

13121  # Functions introduced in OpenSSL 1.1.1.

13123do :
13124  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites" "ac_cv_func_SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites"
13125if test "x$ac_cv_func_SSL_CTX_set_ciphersuites" = xyes; then :
13126  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13127#define HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_CIPHERSUITES 1
13128_ACEOF
13129
13130else
13131  as_fn_error $? "OpenSSL version >= 1.1.1 is required for SSL support" "$LINENO" 5
13132fi
13133done
13134
13135  # Function introduced in OpenSSL 1.0.2, not in LibreSSL.
13136  for ac_func in SSL_CTX_set_cert_cb
13137do :
13138  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "SSL_CTX_set_cert_cb" "ac_cv_func_SSL_CTX_set_cert_cb"
13139if test "x$ac_cv_func_SSL_CTX_set_cert_cb" = xyes; then :
13140  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13141#define HAVE_SSL_CTX_SET_CERT_CB 1
13142_ACEOF
13143
13144fi
13145done
13146
13147  # Function introduced in OpenSSL 1.1.1, not in LibreSSL.
13148  for ac_func in X509_get_signature_info SSL_CTX_set_num_tickets SSL_CTX_set_keylog_callback
13149do :
13150  as_ac_var=`$as_echo "ac_cv_func_$ac_func" | $as_tr_sh`
13151ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "$ac_func" "$as_ac_var"
13152if eval test \"x\$"$as_ac_var"\" = x"yes"; then :
13153  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13154#define `$as_echo "HAVE_$ac_func" | $as_tr_cpp` 1
13155_ACEOF
13156
13157fi
13158done
13159
13160
13161$as_echo "#define USE_OPENSSL 1" >>confdefs.h
13162

13164  as_fn_error $? "--with-ssl must specify openssl" "$LINENO" 5

13453extern "C"

13459return ldap_bind ();
13460  ;

13473{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_ldap_ldap_bind" >&5

13476  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13477#define HAVE_LIBLDAP 1
13478_ACEOF
13479
13480  LIBS="-lldap $LIBS"
13481
13482else

13484fi
13485
13486    LDAP_LIBS_BE="-lldap $EXTRA_LDAP_LIBS"
13487    # This test is carried out against libldap.
13488    for ac_func in ldap_initialize
13489do :
13490  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "ldap_initialize" "ac_cv_func_ldap_initialize"
13491if test "x$ac_cv_func_ldap_initialize" = xyes; then :
13492  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13493#define HAVE_LDAP_INITIALIZE 1
13494_ACEOF
13495
13496fi
13497done
13498
13499    # The separate ldap_r library only exists in OpenLDAP < 2.5, and if we
13500    # have 2.5 or later, we shouldn't even probe for ldap_r (we might find a
13501    # library from a separate OpenLDAP installation).  The most reliable
13502    # way to check that is to check for a function introduced in 2.5.
13503    ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "ldap_verify_credentials" "ac_cv_func_ldap_verify_credentials"
13504if test "x$ac_cv_func_ldap_verify_credentials" = xyes; then :
13505  thread_safe_libldap=yes
13506else
13507  thread_safe_libldap=no
13508fi
13509
13510    if test "$thread_safe_libldap" = no; then
13511      # Use ldap_r for FE if available, else assume ldap is thread-safe.
13512      # On some platforms ldap_r fails to link without PTHREAD_LIBS.
13513      LIBS="$_LIBS"
13514      { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for ldap_bind in -lldap_r" >&5
13515$as_echo_n "checking for ldap_bind in -lldap_r... " >&6; }
13516if ${ac_cv_lib_ldap_r_ldap_bind+:} false; then :
13517  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
13518else
13519  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
13520LIBS="-lldap_r $PTHREAD_CFLAGS $PTHREAD_LIBS $EXTRA_LDAP_LIBS $LIBS"
13521cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
13522/* end confdefs.h.  */
13523
13524/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
13525   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
13526   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */

13528extern "C"
13529#endif

13531int
13532main ()
13533{
13534return ldap_bind ();
13535  ;
13536  return 0;
13537}

13588  ac_cv_lib_wldap32_ldap_bind=no
13589fi
13590rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
13591    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
13592LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
13593fi
13594{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_wldap32_ldap_bind" >&5
13595$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_wldap32_ldap_bind" >&6; }
13596if test "x$ac_cv_lib_wldap32_ldap_bind" = xyes; then :
13597  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13598#define HAVE_LIBWLDAP32 1
13599_ACEOF
13600
13601  LIBS="-lwldap32 $LIBS"
13602
13603else
13604  as_fn_error $? "library 'wldap32' is required for LDAP" "$LINENO" 5
13605fi
13606
13607    LDAP_LIBS_FE="-lwldap32"
13608    LDAP_LIBS_BE="-lwldap32"
13609  fi
13610  LIBS="$_LIBS"
13611fi
13612
13613
13614
13615# for contrib/sepgsql
13616if test "$with_selinux" = yes; then
13617  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for security_compute_create_name in -lselinux" >&5
13618$as_echo_n "checking for security_compute_create_name in -lselinux... " >&6; }
13619if ${ac_cv_lib_selinux_security_compute_create_name+:} false; then :
13620  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
13621else
13622  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
13623LIBS="-lselinux  $LIBS"
13624cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
13625/* end confdefs.h.  */
13626
13627/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
13628   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
13629   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
13630#ifdef __cplusplus
13631extern "C"
13632#endif
13633char security_compute_create_name ();
13634int
13635main ()
13636{
13637return security_compute_create_name ();
13638  ;
13639  return 0;
13640}
13641_ACEOF
13642if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
13643  ac_cv_lib_selinux_security_compute_create_name=yes
13644else
13645  ac_cv_lib_selinux_security_compute_create_name=no
13646fi
13647rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
13648    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
13649LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
13650fi
13651{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_selinux_security_compute_create_name" >&5
13652$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_selinux_security_compute_create_name" >&6; }
13653if test "x$ac_cv_lib_selinux_security_compute_create_name" = xyes; then :
13654  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13655#define HAVE_LIBSELINUX 1
13656_ACEOF
13657
13658  LIBS="-lselinux $LIBS"
13659
13660else
13661  as_fn_error $? "library 'libselinux', version 2.1.10 or newer, is required for SELinux support" "$LINENO" 5
13662fi
13663
13664fi
13665
13666# for contrib/uuid-ossp
13667if test "$with_uuid" = bsd ; then
13668  # On BSD, the UUID functions are in libc
13669  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "uuid_to_string" "ac_cv_func_uuid_to_string"
13670if test "x$ac_cv_func_uuid_to_string" = xyes; then :
13671  UUID_LIBS=""
13672else
13673  as_fn_error $? "BSD UUID functions are not present" "$LINENO" 5
13674fi
13675
13676elif test "$with_uuid" = e2fs ; then
13677  # On macOS, the UUID functions are in libc
13678  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "uuid_generate" "ac_cv_func_uuid_generate"
13679if test "x$ac_cv_func_uuid_generate" = xyes; then :
13680  UUID_LIBS=""
13681else
13682  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for uuid_generate in -luuid" >&5
13683$as_echo_n "checking for uuid_generate in -luuid... " >&6; }
13684if ${ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_generate+:} false; then :
13685  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
13686else
13687  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
13688LIBS="-luuid  $LIBS"
13689cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
13690/* end confdefs.h.  */
13691
13692/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
13693   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
13694   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
13695#ifdef __cplusplus
13696extern "C"
13697#endif
13698char uuid_generate ();
13699int
13700main ()
13701{
13702return uuid_generate ();
13703  ;
13704  return 0;
13705}
13706_ACEOF
13707if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
13708  ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_generate=yes
13709else
13710  ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_generate=no
13711fi
13712rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
13713    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
13714LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
13715fi
13716{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_generate" >&5
13717$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_generate" >&6; }
13718if test "x$ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_generate" = xyes; then :
13719  UUID_LIBS="-luuid"
13720else
13721  as_fn_error $? "library 'uuid' is required for E2FS UUID" "$LINENO" 5
13722fi
13723
13724fi
13725
13726elif test "$with_uuid" = ossp ; then
13727  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for uuid_export in -lossp-uuid" >&5
13728$as_echo_n "checking for uuid_export in -lossp-uuid... " >&6; }
13729if ${ac_cv_lib_ossp_uuid_uuid_export+:} false; then :
13730  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
13731else
13732  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
13733LIBS="-lossp-uuid  $LIBS"
13734cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
13735/* end confdefs.h.  */
13736
13737/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
13738   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
13739   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
13740#ifdef __cplusplus
13741extern "C"
13742#endif
13743char uuid_export ();
13744int
13745main ()
13746{
13747return uuid_export ();
13748  ;
13749  return 0;
13750}
13751_ACEOF
13752if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
13753  ac_cv_lib_ossp_uuid_uuid_export=yes
13754else
13755  ac_cv_lib_ossp_uuid_uuid_export=no
13756fi
13757rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
13758    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
13759LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
13760fi
13761{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_ossp_uuid_uuid_export" >&5
13762$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_ossp_uuid_uuid_export" >&6; }
13763if test "x$ac_cv_lib_ossp_uuid_uuid_export" = xyes; then :
13764  UUID_LIBS="-lossp-uuid"
13765else
13766  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for uuid_export in -luuid" >&5
13767$as_echo_n "checking for uuid_export in -luuid... " >&6; }
13768if ${ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_export+:} false; then :
13769  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
13770else
13771  ac_check_lib_save_LIBS=$LIBS
13772LIBS="-luuid  $LIBS"
13773cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
13774/* end confdefs.h.  */
13775
13776/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
13777   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
13778   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
13779#ifdef __cplusplus
13780extern "C"
13781#endif
13782char uuid_export ();
13783int
13784main ()
13785{
13786return uuid_export ();
13787  ;
13788  return 0;
13789}
13790_ACEOF
13791if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
13792  ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_export=yes
13793else
13794  ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_export=no
13795fi
13796rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
13797    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
13798LIBS=$ac_check_lib_save_LIBS
13799fi
13800{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_export" >&5
13801$as_echo "$ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_export" >&6; }
13802if test "x$ac_cv_lib_uuid_uuid_export" = xyes; then :
13803  UUID_LIBS="-luuid"
13804else
13805  as_fn_error $? "library 'ossp-uuid' or 'uuid' is required for OSSP UUID" "$LINENO" 5
13806fi
13807
13808fi
13809
13810fi
13811
13812
13813
13814##
13815## Header files
13816##
13817
13818for ac_header in atomic.h copyfile.h execinfo.h getopt.h ifaddrs.h mbarrier.h sys/epoll.h sys/event.h sys/personality.h sys/prctl.h sys/procctl.h sys/signalfd.h sys/ucred.h termios.h ucred.h xlocale.h
13819do :
13820  as_ac_Header=`$as_echo "ac_cv_header_$ac_header" | $as_tr_sh`
13821ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "$ac_header" "$as_ac_Header" "$ac_includes_default"
13822if eval test \"x\$"$as_ac_Header"\" = x"yes"; then :
13823  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13824#define `$as_echo "HAVE_$ac_header" | $as_tr_cpp` 1
13825_ACEOF
13826
13827fi
13828
13829done
13830
13831
13832if expr x"$pgac_cv_check_readline" : 'x-lreadline' >/dev/null ; then
13833  for ac_header in readline/readline.h
13834do :
13835  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "readline/readline.h" "ac_cv_header_readline_readline_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13836if test "x$ac_cv_header_readline_readline_h" = xyes; then :
13837  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13838#define HAVE_READLINE_READLINE_H 1
13839_ACEOF
13840
13841else
13842  for ac_header in readline.h
13843do :
13844  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "readline.h" "ac_cv_header_readline_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13845if test "x$ac_cv_header_readline_h" = xyes; then :
13846  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13847#define HAVE_READLINE_H 1
13848_ACEOF
13849
13850else
13851  as_fn_error $? "readline header not found
13852If you have readline already installed, see config.log for details on the
13853failure.  It is possible the compiler isn't looking in the proper directory.
13854Use --without-readline to disable readline support." "$LINENO" 5
13855fi
13856
13857done
13858
13859fi
13860
13861done
13862
13863  for ac_header in readline/history.h
13864do :
13865  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "readline/history.h" "ac_cv_header_readline_history_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13866if test "x$ac_cv_header_readline_history_h" = xyes; then :
13867  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13868#define HAVE_READLINE_HISTORY_H 1
13869_ACEOF
13870
13871else
13872  for ac_header in history.h
13873do :
13874  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "history.h" "ac_cv_header_history_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13875if test "x$ac_cv_header_history_h" = xyes; then :
13876  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13877#define HAVE_HISTORY_H 1
13878_ACEOF
13879
13880else
13881  as_fn_error $? "history header not found
13882If you have readline already installed, see config.log for details on the
13883failure.  It is possible the compiler isn't looking in the proper directory.
13884Use --without-readline to disable readline support." "$LINENO" 5
13885fi
13886
13887done
13888
13889fi
13890
13891done
13892
13893fi
13894
13895if expr x"$pgac_cv_check_readline" : 'x-ledit' >/dev/null ; then
13896# Some installations of libedit usurp /usr/include/readline/, which seems
13897# bad practice, since in combined installations readline will have its headers
13898# there.  We might have to resort to AC_EGREP checks to make sure we found
13899# the proper header...
13900  for ac_header in editline/readline.h
13901do :
13902  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "editline/readline.h" "ac_cv_header_editline_readline_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13903if test "x$ac_cv_header_editline_readline_h" = xyes; then :
13904  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13905#define HAVE_EDITLINE_READLINE_H 1
13906_ACEOF
13907
13908else
13909  for ac_header in readline.h
13910do :
13911  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "readline.h" "ac_cv_header_readline_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13912if test "x$ac_cv_header_readline_h" = xyes; then :
13913  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13914#define HAVE_READLINE_H 1
13915_ACEOF
13916
13917else
13918  for ac_header in readline/readline.h
13919do :
13920  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "readline/readline.h" "ac_cv_header_readline_readline_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13921if test "x$ac_cv_header_readline_readline_h" = xyes; then :
13922  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13923#define HAVE_READLINE_READLINE_H 1
13924_ACEOF
13925
13926else
13927  as_fn_error $? "readline header not found
13928If you have libedit already installed, see config.log for details on the
13929failure.  It is possible the compiler isn't looking in the proper directory.
13930Use --without-readline to disable libedit support." "$LINENO" 5
13931fi
13932
13933done
13934
13935fi
13936
13937done
13938
13939fi
13940
13941done
13942
13943# Note: in a libedit installation, history.h is sometimes a dummy, and may
13944# not be there at all.  Hence, don't complain if not found.  We must check
13945# though, since in yet other versions it is an independent header.
13946  for ac_header in editline/history.h
13947do :
13948  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "editline/history.h" "ac_cv_header_editline_history_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13949if test "x$ac_cv_header_editline_history_h" = xyes; then :
13950  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13951#define HAVE_EDITLINE_HISTORY_H 1
13952_ACEOF
13953
13954else
13955  for ac_header in history.h
13956do :
13957  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "history.h" "ac_cv_header_history_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13958if test "x$ac_cv_header_history_h" = xyes; then :
13959  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13960#define HAVE_HISTORY_H 1
13961_ACEOF
13962
13963else
13964  for ac_header in readline/history.h
13965do :
13966  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "readline/history.h" "ac_cv_header_readline_history_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13967if test "x$ac_cv_header_readline_history_h" = xyes; then :
13968  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
13969#define HAVE_READLINE_HISTORY_H 1
13970_ACEOF
13971
13972fi
13973
13974done
13975
13976fi
13977
13978done
13979
13980fi
13981
13982done
13983
13984fi
13985
13986if test "$with_zlib" = yes; then
13987  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "zlib.h" "ac_cv_header_zlib_h" "$ac_includes_default"
13988if test "x$ac_cv_header_zlib_h" = xyes; then :
13989
13990else
13991  as_fn_error $? "zlib header not found
13992If you have zlib already installed, see config.log for details on the
13993failure.  It is possible the compiler isn't looking in the proper directory.
13994Use --without-zlib to disable zlib support." "$LINENO" 5
13995fi
13996
13997
13998fi
13999
14000if test -z "$LZ4"; then
14001  for ac_prog in lz4
14002do
14003  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
14004set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
14005{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
14006$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
14007if ${ac_cv_path_LZ4+:} false; then :
14008  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
14009else
14010  case $LZ4 in
14011  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
14012  ac_cv_path_LZ4="$LZ4" # Let the user override the test with a path.
14013  ;;
14014  *)
14015  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
14016for as_dir in $PATH
14017do
14018  IFS=$as_save_IFS
14019  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
14020    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
14021  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
14022    ac_cv_path_LZ4="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
14023    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
14024    break 2
14025  fi
14026done
14027  done
14028IFS=$as_save_IFS
14029
14030  ;;
14031esac
14032fi
14033LZ4=$ac_cv_path_LZ4
14034if test -n "$LZ4"; then
14035  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $LZ4" >&5
14036$as_echo "$LZ4" >&6; }
14037else
14038  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
14039$as_echo "no" >&6; }
14040fi
14041
14042
14043  test -n "$LZ4" && break
14044done
14045
14046else
14047  # Report the value of LZ4 in configure's output in all cases.
14048  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for LZ4" >&5
14049$as_echo_n "checking for LZ4... " >&6; }
14050  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $LZ4" >&5
14051$as_echo "$LZ4" >&6; }
14052fi
14053
14054if test "$with_lz4" = yes; then
14055  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "lz4.h" "ac_cv_header_lz4_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14056if test "x$ac_cv_header_lz4_h" = xyes; then :
14057
14058else
14059  as_fn_error $? "lz4.h header file is required for LZ4" "$LINENO" 5
14060fi
14061
14062
14063fi
14064
14065if test -z "$ZSTD"; then
14066  for ac_prog in zstd
14067do
14068  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
14069set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
14070{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
14071$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
14072if ${ac_cv_path_ZSTD+:} false; then :
14073  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
14074else
14075  case $ZSTD in
14076  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
14077  ac_cv_path_ZSTD="$ZSTD" # Let the user override the test with a path.
14078  ;;
14079  *)
14080  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
14081for as_dir in $PATH
14082do
14083  IFS=$as_save_IFS
14084  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
14085    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
14086  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
14087    ac_cv_path_ZSTD="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
14088    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
14089    break 2
14090  fi
14091done
14092  done
14093IFS=$as_save_IFS
14094
14095  ;;
14096esac
14097fi
14098ZSTD=$ac_cv_path_ZSTD
14099if test -n "$ZSTD"; then
14100  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ZSTD" >&5
14101$as_echo "$ZSTD" >&6; }
14102else
14103  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
14104$as_echo "no" >&6; }
14105fi
14106
14107
14108  test -n "$ZSTD" && break
14109done
14110
14111else
14112  # Report the value of ZSTD in configure's output in all cases.
14113  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for ZSTD" >&5
14114$as_echo_n "checking for ZSTD... " >&6; }
14115  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ZSTD" >&5
14116$as_echo "$ZSTD" >&6; }
14117fi
14118
14119if test "$with_zstd" = yes; then
14120  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "zstd.h" "ac_cv_header_zstd_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14121if test "x$ac_cv_header_zstd_h" = xyes; then :
14122
14123else
14124  as_fn_error $? "zstd.h header file is required for ZSTD" "$LINENO" 5
14125fi
14126
14127
14128fi
14129
14130if test "$with_gssapi" = yes ; then
14131  for ac_header in gssapi/gssapi.h
14132do :
14133  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "gssapi/gssapi.h" "ac_cv_header_gssapi_gssapi_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14134if test "x$ac_cv_header_gssapi_gssapi_h" = xyes; then :
14135  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
14136#define HAVE_GSSAPI_GSSAPI_H 1
14137_ACEOF
14138
14139else
14140  for ac_header in gssapi.h
14141do :
14142  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "gssapi.h" "ac_cv_header_gssapi_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14143if test "x$ac_cv_header_gssapi_h" = xyes; then :
14144  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
14145#define HAVE_GSSAPI_H 1
14146_ACEOF
14147
14148else
14149  as_fn_error $? "gssapi.h header file is required for GSSAPI" "$LINENO" 5
14150fi
14151
14152done
14153
14154fi
14155
14156done
14157
14158  for ac_header in gssapi/gssapi_ext.h
14159do :
14160  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "gssapi/gssapi_ext.h" "ac_cv_header_gssapi_gssapi_ext_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14161if test "x$ac_cv_header_gssapi_gssapi_ext_h" = xyes; then :
14162  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
14163#define HAVE_GSSAPI_GSSAPI_EXT_H 1
14164_ACEOF
14165
14166else
14167  for ac_header in gssapi_ext.h
14168do :
14169  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "gssapi_ext.h" "ac_cv_header_gssapi_ext_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14170if test "x$ac_cv_header_gssapi_ext_h" = xyes; then :
14171  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
14172#define HAVE_GSSAPI_EXT_H 1
14173_ACEOF
14174
14175else
14176  as_fn_error $? "gssapi_ext.h header file is required for GSSAPI" "$LINENO" 5
14177fi
14178
14179done
14180
14181fi
14182
14183done
14184
14185fi
14186
14187if test -z "$OPENSSL"; then
14188  for ac_prog in openssl
14189do
14190  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
14191set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
14192{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
14193$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
14194if ${ac_cv_path_OPENSSL+:} false; then :
14195  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
14196else
14197  case $OPENSSL in
14198  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
14199  ac_cv_path_OPENSSL="$OPENSSL" # Let the user override the test with a path.
14200  ;;
14201  *)
14202  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
14203for as_dir in $PATH
14204do
14205  IFS=$as_save_IFS
14206  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
14207    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
14208  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
14209    ac_cv_path_OPENSSL="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
14210    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
14211    break 2
14212  fi
14213done
14214  done
14215IFS=$as_save_IFS
14216
14217  ;;
14218esac
14219fi
14220OPENSSL=$ac_cv_path_OPENSSL
14221if test -n "$OPENSSL"; then
14222  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $OPENSSL" >&5
14223$as_echo "$OPENSSL" >&6; }
14224else
14225  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
14226$as_echo "no" >&6; }
14227fi
14228
14229
14230  test -n "$OPENSSL" && break
14231done
14232
14233else
14234  # Report the value of OPENSSL in configure's output in all cases.
14235  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for OPENSSL" >&5
14236$as_echo_n "checking for OPENSSL... " >&6; }
14237  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $OPENSSL" >&5
14238$as_echo "$OPENSSL" >&6; }
14239fi
14240
14241pgac_openssl_version="$($OPENSSL version 2> /dev/null || echo openssl not found)"
14242{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using openssl: $pgac_openssl_version" >&5
14243$as_echo "$as_me: using openssl: $pgac_openssl_version" >&6;}
14244if test "$with_ssl" = openssl ; then
14245  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "openssl/ssl.h" "ac_cv_header_openssl_ssl_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14246if test "x$ac_cv_header_openssl_ssl_h" = xyes; then :
14247
14248else
14249  as_fn_error $? "header file <openssl/ssl.h> is required for OpenSSL" "$LINENO" 5
14250fi
14251
14252
14253  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "openssl/err.h" "ac_cv_header_openssl_err_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14254if test "x$ac_cv_header_openssl_err_h" = xyes; then :

14256else
14257  as_fn_error $? "header file <openssl/err.h> is required for OpenSSL" "$LINENO" 5

14261fi

14349if ${pgac_cv_ldap_safe+:} false; then :

14355#if !defined(LDAP_VENDOR_VERSION) || \
14356     (defined(LDAP_API_FEATURE_X_OPENLDAP) && \
14357      LDAP_VENDOR_VERSION >= 20424 && LDAP_VENDOR_VERSION <= 20431)
14358choke me
14359#endif
14360int
14361main ()
14362{
14363
14364  ;
14365  return 0;
14366}
14367_ACEOF
14368if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :

14370else
14371  pgac_cv_ldap_safe=no
14372fi

14374fi
14375{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_ldap_safe" >&5
14376$as_echo "$pgac_cv_ldap_safe" >&6; }
14377
14378if test "$pgac_cv_ldap_safe" != yes; then
14379  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING:
14380*** With OpenLDAP versions 2.4.24 through 2.4.31, inclusive, each backend
14381*** process that loads libpq (via WAL receiver, dblink, or postgres_fdw) and
14382*** also uses LDAP will crash on exit." >&5
14383$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING:
14384*** With OpenLDAP versions 2.4.24 through 2.4.31, inclusive, each backend
14385*** process that loads libpq (via WAL receiver, dblink, or postgres_fdw) and
14386*** also uses LDAP will crash on exit." >&2;}
14387fi
14388  else
14389     ac_fn_c_check_header_compile "$LINENO" "winldap.h" "ac_cv_header_winldap_h" "$ac_includes_default
14390#include <windows.h>
14391
14392"

14415if test "$with_uuid" = bsd ; then
14416  for ac_header in uuid.h
14417do :
14418  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "uuid.h" "ac_cv_header_uuid_h" "$ac_includes_default"
14419if test "x$ac_cv_header_uuid_h" = xyes; then :
14420  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
14421#define HAVE_UUID_H 1
14422_ACEOF
14423 cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14424/* end confdefs.h.  */
14425#include <uuid.h>
14426
14427_ACEOF
14428if (eval "$ac_cpp conftest.$ac_ext") 2>&5 |
14429  $EGREP "uuid_to_string" >/dev/null 2>&1; then :
14430
14431else
14432  as_fn_error $? "header file <uuid.h> does not match BSD UUID library" "$LINENO" 5
14433fi
14434rm -f conftest*
14435
14436else
14437  as_fn_error $? "header file <uuid.h> is required for BSD UUID" "$LINENO" 5
14438fi
14439
14440done
14441

14453
14454_ACEOF

14456  $EGREP "uuid_generate" >/dev/null 2>&1; then :

14463else

14474
14475_ACEOF
14476if (eval "$ac_cpp conftest.$ac_ext") 2>&5 |

14478
14479else

14481fi

14483
14484else

14569
14570##
14571## Types, structures, compiler characteristics
14572##
14573
14574  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether byte ordering is bigendian" >&5
14575$as_echo_n "checking whether byte ordering is bigendian... " >&6; }
14576if ${ac_cv_c_bigendian+:} false; then :
14577  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
14578else
14579  ac_cv_c_bigendian=unknown
14580    # See if we're dealing with a universal compiler.
14581    cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14582/* end confdefs.h.  */
14583#ifndef __APPLE_CC__
14584	       not a universal capable compiler
14585	     #endif
14586	     typedef int dummy;
14587
14588_ACEOF
14589if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
14590
14591	# Check for potential -arch flags.  It is not universal unless
14592	# there are at least two -arch flags with different values.
14593	ac_arch=
14594	ac_prev=
14595	for ac_word in $CC $CFLAGS $CPPFLAGS $LDFLAGS; do
14596	 if test -n "$ac_prev"; then
14597	   case $ac_word in
14598	     i?86 | x86_64 | ppc | ppc64)
14599	       if test -z "$ac_arch" || test "$ac_arch" = "$ac_word"; then
14600		 ac_arch=$ac_word
14601	       else
14602		 ac_cv_c_bigendian=universal
14603		 break
14604	       fi
14605	       ;;
14606	   esac
14607	   ac_prev=
14608	 elif test "x$ac_word" = "x-arch"; then
14609	   ac_prev=arch
14610	 fi
14611       done
14612fi
14613rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
14614    if test $ac_cv_c_bigendian = unknown; then
14615      # See if sys/param.h defines the BYTE_ORDER macro.
14616      cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14617/* end confdefs.h.  */
14618#include <sys/types.h>
14619	     #include <sys/param.h>
14620
14621int
14622main ()
14623{
14624#if ! (defined BYTE_ORDER && defined BIG_ENDIAN \
14625		     && defined LITTLE_ENDIAN && BYTE_ORDER && BIG_ENDIAN \
14626		     && LITTLE_ENDIAN)
14627	      bogus endian macros
14628	     #endif
14629
14630  ;
14631  return 0;
14632}
14633_ACEOF
14634if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
14635  # It does; now see whether it defined to BIG_ENDIAN or not.
14636	 cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14637/* end confdefs.h.  */
14638#include <sys/types.h>
14639		#include <sys/param.h>
14640
14641int
14642main ()
14643{
14644#if BYTE_ORDER != BIG_ENDIAN
14645		 not big endian
14646		#endif
14647
14648  ;
14649  return 0;
14650}
14651_ACEOF
14652if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
14653  ac_cv_c_bigendian=yes
14654else
14655  ac_cv_c_bigendian=no
14656fi
14657rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
14658fi
14659rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
14660    fi
14661    if test $ac_cv_c_bigendian = unknown; then
14662      # See if <limits.h> defines _LITTLE_ENDIAN or _BIG_ENDIAN (e.g., Solaris).
14663      cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14664/* end confdefs.h.  */
14665#include <limits.h>
14666
14667int
14668main ()
14669{
14670#if ! (defined _LITTLE_ENDIAN || defined _BIG_ENDIAN)
14671	      bogus endian macros
14672	     #endif
14673
14674  ;
14675  return 0;
14676}
14677_ACEOF
14678if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
14679  # It does; now see whether it defined to _BIG_ENDIAN or not.
14680	 cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14681/* end confdefs.h.  */
14682#include <limits.h>
14683
14684int
14685main ()
14686{
14687#ifndef _BIG_ENDIAN
14688		 not big endian
14689		#endif
14690
14691  ;
14692  return 0;
14693}
14694_ACEOF
14695if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
14696  ac_cv_c_bigendian=yes
14697else
14698  ac_cv_c_bigendian=no
14699fi
14700rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
14701fi
14702rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
14703    fi
14704    if test $ac_cv_c_bigendian = unknown; then
14705      # Compile a test program.
14706      if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
14707  # Try to guess by grepping values from an object file.
14708	 cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14709/* end confdefs.h.  */
14710short int ascii_mm[] =
14711		  { 0x4249, 0x4765, 0x6E44, 0x6961, 0x6E53, 0x7953, 0 };
14712		short int ascii_ii[] =
14713		  { 0x694C, 0x5454, 0x656C, 0x6E45, 0x6944, 0x6E61, 0 };
14714		int use_ascii (int i) {
14715		  return ascii_mm[i] + ascii_ii[i];
14716		}
14717		short int ebcdic_ii[] =
14718		  { 0x89D3, 0xE3E3, 0x8593, 0x95C5, 0x89C4, 0x9581, 0 };
14719		short int ebcdic_mm[] =
14720		  { 0xC2C9, 0xC785, 0x95C4, 0x8981, 0x95E2, 0xA8E2, 0 };
14721		int use_ebcdic (int i) {
14722		  return ebcdic_mm[i] + ebcdic_ii[i];
14723		}
14724		extern int foo;
14725
14726int
14727main ()
14728{
14729return use_ascii (foo) == use_ebcdic (foo);
14730  ;
14731  return 0;
14732}
14733_ACEOF
14734if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
14735  if grep BIGenDianSyS conftest.$ac_objext >/dev/null; then
14736	      ac_cv_c_bigendian=yes
14737	    fi
14738	    if grep LiTTleEnDian conftest.$ac_objext >/dev/null ; then
14739	      if test "$ac_cv_c_bigendian" = unknown; then
14740		ac_cv_c_bigendian=no
14741	      else
14742		# finding both strings is unlikely to happen, but who knows?
14743		ac_cv_c_bigendian=unknown
14744	      fi
14745	    fi
14746fi
14747rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
14748else
14749  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
14750/* end confdefs.h.  */
14751$ac_includes_default
14752int
14753main ()
14754{
14755
14756	     /* Are we little or big endian?  From Harbison&Steele.  */
14757	     union
14758	     {
14759	       long int l;
14760	       char c[sizeof (long int)];
14761	     } u;
14762	     u.l = 1;
14763	     return u.c[sizeof (long int) - 1] == 1;
14764
14765  ;
14766  return 0;
14767}
14768_ACEOF
14769if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
14770  ac_cv_c_bigendian=no
14771else
14772  ac_cv_c_bigendian=yes
14773fi
14774rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
14775  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
14776fi
14777
14778    fi
14779fi
14780{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_c_bigendian" >&5
14781$as_echo "$ac_cv_c_bigendian" >&6; }
14782 case $ac_cv_c_bigendian in #(
14783   yes)
14784     $as_echo "#define WORDS_BIGENDIAN 1" >>confdefs.h
14785;; #(
14786   no)
14787      ;; #(
14788   universal)
14789
14790$as_echo "#define AC_APPLE_UNIVERSAL_BUILD 1" >>confdefs.h
14791
14792     ;; #(
14793   *)
14794     as_fn_error $? "unknown endianness
14795 presetting ac_cv_c_bigendian=no (or yes) will help" "$LINENO" 5 ;;
14796 esac
14797

14806/* end confdefs.h.  */

14808typedef int foo_t;
14809static $ac_kw foo_t static_foo () {return 0; }
14810$ac_kw foo_t foo () {return 0; }
14811#endif
14812
14813_ACEOF

14819done
14820
14821fi
14822{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_c_inline" >&5
14823$as_echo "$ac_cv_c_inline" >&6; }
14824
14825case $ac_cv_c_inline in
14826  inline | yes) ;;
14827  *)
14828    case $ac_cv_c_inline in
14829      no) ac_val=;;
14830      *) ac_val=$ac_cv_c_inline;;
14831    esac
14832    cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
14833#ifndef __cplusplus
14834#define inline $ac_val
14835#endif
14836_ACEOF
14837    ;;
14838esac

15280# MSVC doesn't cope well with defining restrict to __restrict, the
15281# spelling it understands, because it conflicts with
15282# __declspec(restrict). Therefore we define pg_restrict to the
15283# appropriate definition, which presumably won't conflict.
15284{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for C/C++ restrict keyword" >&5
15285$as_echo_n "checking for C/C++ restrict keyword... " >&6; }
15286if ${ac_cv_c_restrict+:} false; then :
15287  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15288else
15289  ac_cv_c_restrict=no
15290   # The order here caters to the fact that C++ does not require restrict.
15291   for ac_kw in __restrict __restrict__ _Restrict restrict; do
15292     cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15293/* end confdefs.h.  */
15294typedef int * int_ptr;
15295	int foo (int_ptr $ac_kw ip) {
15296	return ip[0];
15297       }
15298int
15299main ()
15300{
15301int s[1];
15302	int * $ac_kw t = s;
15303	t[0] = 0;
15304	return foo(t)
15305  ;
15306  return 0;
15307}
15308_ACEOF

15310  ac_cv_c_restrict=$ac_kw
15311fi

15313     test "$ac_cv_c_restrict" != no && break
15314   done
15315
15316fi
15317{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_c_restrict" >&5
15318$as_echo "$ac_cv_c_restrict" >&6; }
15319
15320 case $ac_cv_c_restrict in
15321   restrict) ;;
15322   no) $as_echo "#define restrict /**/" >>confdefs.h
15323 ;;
15324   *)  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
15325#define restrict $ac_cv_c_restrict
15326_ACEOF
15327 ;;
15328 esac
15329
15330if test "$ac_cv_c_restrict" = "no"; then
15331  pg_restrict=""
15332else
15333  pg_restrict="$ac_cv_c_restrict"
15334fi
15335
15336cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
15337#define pg_restrict $pg_restrict
15338_ACEOF
15339
15340
15341ac_fn_c_check_type "$LINENO" "struct option" "ac_cv_type_struct_option" "#ifdef HAVE_GETOPT_H
15342#include <getopt.h>
15343#endif
15344"
15345if test "x$ac_cv_type_struct_option" = xyes; then :
15346
15347cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
15348#define HAVE_STRUCT_OPTION 1
15349_ACEOF
15350
15351
15352fi
15353
15354
15355case $host_cpu in
15356  x86_64)
15357    # On x86_64, check if we can compile a popcntq instruction
15358    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether assembler supports x86_64 popcntq" >&5
15359$as_echo_n "checking whether assembler supports x86_64 popcntq... " >&6; }
15360if ${pgac_cv_have_x86_64_popcntq+:} false; then :
15361  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15362else
15363  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15364/* end confdefs.h.  */
15365
15366int
15367main ()
15368{
15369long long x = 1; long long r;
15370	 __asm__ __volatile__ (" popcntq %1,%0\n" : "=q"(r) : "rm"(x));
15371  ;
15372  return 0;
15373}
15374_ACEOF
15375if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
15376  pgac_cv_have_x86_64_popcntq=yes
15377else
15378  pgac_cv_have_x86_64_popcntq=no
15379fi
15380rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
15381fi
15382{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_have_x86_64_popcntq" >&5
15383$as_echo "$pgac_cv_have_x86_64_popcntq" >&6; }
15384    if test x"$pgac_cv_have_x86_64_popcntq" = xyes ; then
15385
15386$as_echo "#define HAVE_X86_64_POPCNTQ 1" >>confdefs.h
15387
15388    fi
15389  ;;
15390  ppc*|powerpc*)
15391    # On PPC, check if compiler accepts "i"(x) when __builtin_constant_p(x).
15392    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether __builtin_constant_p(x) implies \"i\"(x) acceptance" >&5
15393$as_echo_n "checking whether __builtin_constant_p(x) implies \"i\"(x) acceptance... " >&6; }
15394if ${pgac_cv_have_i_constraint__builtin_constant_p+:} false; then :
15395  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15396else
15397  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15398/* end confdefs.h.  */
15399static inline int
15400     addi(int ra, int si)
15401     {
15402         int res = 0;
15403         if (__builtin_constant_p(si))
15404             __asm__ __volatile__(
15405                 " addi %0,%1,%2\n" : "=r"(res) : "b"(ra), "i"(si));
15406         return res;
15407     }
15408     int test_adds(int x) { return addi(3, x) + addi(x, 5); }
15409int
15410main ()
15411{
15412
15413  ;
15414  return 0;
15415}
15416_ACEOF
15417if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
15418  pgac_cv_have_i_constraint__builtin_constant_p=yes
15419else
15420  pgac_cv_have_i_constraint__builtin_constant_p=no
15421fi
15422rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
15423fi
15424{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_have_i_constraint__builtin_constant_p" >&5
15425$as_echo "$pgac_cv_have_i_constraint__builtin_constant_p" >&6; }
15426    if test x"$pgac_cv_have_i_constraint__builtin_constant_p" = xyes ; then
15427
15428$as_echo "#define HAVE_I_CONSTRAINT__BUILTIN_CONSTANT_P 1" >>confdefs.h
15429
15430    fi
15431  ;;
15432esac
15433
15434# Check largefile support.  You might think this is a system service not a
15435# compiler characteristic, but you'd be wrong.  We must check this before
15436# probing existence of related functions such as fseeko, since the largefile
15437# defines can affect what is generated for that.
15438if test "$PORTNAME" != "win32"; then
15439   # Check whether --enable-largefile was given.
15440if test "${enable_largefile+set}" = set; then :
15441  enableval=$enable_largefile;
15442fi
15443
15444if test "$enable_largefile" != no; then
15445
15446  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for special C compiler options needed for large files" >&5
15447$as_echo_n "checking for special C compiler options needed for large files... " >&6; }
15448if ${ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC+:} false; then :
15449  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15450else
15451  ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC=no
15452     if test "$GCC" != yes; then
15453       ac_save_CC=$CC
15454       while :; do
15455	 # IRIX 6.2 and later do not support large files by default,
15456	 # so use the C compiler's -n32 option if that helps.
15457	 cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15458/* end confdefs.h.  */
15459#include <sys/types.h>
15460 /* Check that off_t can represent 2**63 - 1 correctly.
15461    We can't simply define LARGE_OFF_T to be 9223372036854775807,
15462    since some C++ compilers masquerading as C compilers
15463    incorrectly reject 9223372036854775807.  */
15464#define LARGE_OFF_T (((off_t) 1 << 62) - 1 + ((off_t) 1 << 62))
15465  int off_t_is_large[(LARGE_OFF_T % 2147483629 == 721
15466		       && LARGE_OFF_T % 2147483647 == 1)
15467		      ? 1 : -1];
15468int
15469main ()
15470{
15471
15472  ;
15473  return 0;
15474}
15475_ACEOF
15476	 if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
15477  break
15478fi
15479rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext
15480	 CC="$CC -n32"
15481	 if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
15482  ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC=' -n32'; break
15483fi
15484rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext
15485	 break
15486       done
15487       CC=$ac_save_CC
15488       rm -f conftest.$ac_ext
15489    fi
15490fi
15491{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC" >&5
15492$as_echo "$ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC" >&6; }
15493  if test "$ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC" != no; then
15494    CC=$CC$ac_cv_sys_largefile_CC
15495  fi
15496
15497  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for _FILE_OFFSET_BITS value needed for large files" >&5
15498$as_echo_n "checking for _FILE_OFFSET_BITS value needed for large files... " >&6; }
15499if ${ac_cv_sys_file_offset_bits+:} false; then :
15500  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15501else
15502  while :; do
15503  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15504/* end confdefs.h.  */
15505#include <sys/types.h>
15506 /* Check that off_t can represent 2**63 - 1 correctly.
15507    We can't simply define LARGE_OFF_T to be 9223372036854775807,
15508    since some C++ compilers masquerading as C compilers
15509    incorrectly reject 9223372036854775807.  */
15510#define LARGE_OFF_T (((off_t) 1 << 62) - 1 + ((off_t) 1 << 62))
15511  int off_t_is_large[(LARGE_OFF_T % 2147483629 == 721
15512		       && LARGE_OFF_T % 2147483647 == 1)
15513		      ? 1 : -1];
15514int
15515main ()
15516{
15517
15518  ;
15519  return 0;
15520}
15521_ACEOF
15522if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
15523  ac_cv_sys_file_offset_bits=no; break
15524fi
15525rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
15526  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15527/* end confdefs.h.  */
15528#define _FILE_OFFSET_BITS 64
15529#include <sys/types.h>

15531    We can't simply define LARGE_OFF_T to be 9223372036854775807,
15532    since some C++ compilers masquerading as C compilers
15533    incorrectly reject 9223372036854775807.  */

15536		       && LARGE_OFF_T % 2147483647 == 1)
15537		      ? 1 : -1];
15538int
15539main ()
15540{
15541
15542  ;
15543  return 0;
15544}

15596/* end confdefs.h.  */

15598#include <sys/types.h>

15640fi
15641
15642# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler
15643# version HP92453-01 B.11.11.23709.GP, which incorrectly rejects
15644# declarations like `int a3[[(sizeof (unsigned char)) >= 0]];'.
15645# This bug is HP SR number 8606223364.
15646{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking size of off_t" >&5
15647$as_echo_n "checking size of off_t... " >&6; }
15648if ${ac_cv_sizeof_off_t+:} false; then :
15649  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15650else
15651  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) (sizeof (off_t))" "ac_cv_sizeof_off_t"        "$ac_includes_default"; then :
15652
15653else
15654  if test "$ac_cv_type_off_t" = yes; then
15655     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
15656$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
15657as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute sizeof (off_t)
15658See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
15659   else
15660     ac_cv_sizeof_off_t=0
15661   fi
15662fi
15663
15664fi
15665{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sizeof_off_t" >&5
15666$as_echo "$ac_cv_sizeof_off_t" >&6; }
15667
15668
15669
15670cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
15671#define SIZEOF_OFF_T $ac_cv_sizeof_off_t
15672_ACEOF
15673
15674
15675
15676# If we don't have largefile support, can't handle segment size >= 2GB.
15677if test "$ac_cv_sizeof_off_t" -lt 8; then
15678  if expr $RELSEG_SIZE '*' $blocksize '>=' 2 '*' 1024 '*' 1024; then
15679    as_fn_error $? "Large file support is not enabled. Segment size cannot be larger than 1GB." "$LINENO" 5
15680  fi
15681fi
15682
15683
15684##
15685## Functions, global variables
15686##
15687
15688{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for int timezone" >&5
15689$as_echo_n "checking for int timezone... " >&6; }
15690if ${pgac_cv_var_int_timezone+:} false; then :
15691  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15692else
15693  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15694/* end confdefs.h.  */
15695#include <time.h>
15696int res;
15697int
15698main ()
15699{
15700#ifndef __CYGWIN__
15701res = timezone / 60;
15702#else
15703res = _timezone / 60;
15704#endif
15705  ;
15706  return 0;
15707}
15708_ACEOF
15709if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
15710  pgac_cv_var_int_timezone=yes
15711else
15712  pgac_cv_var_int_timezone=no
15713fi
15714rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
15715    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
15716fi
15717{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_var_int_timezone" >&5
15718$as_echo "$pgac_cv_var_int_timezone" >&6; }
15719if test x"$pgac_cv_var_int_timezone" = xyes ; then
15720
15721$as_echo "#define HAVE_INT_TIMEZONE 1" >>confdefs.h
15722
15723fi
15724
15725# Some versions of libedit contain strlcpy(), setproctitle(), and other
15726# symbols that that library has no business exposing to the world.  Pending
15727# acquisition of a clue by those developers, ignore libedit (including its
15728# possible alias of libreadline) while checking for everything else.
15729LIBS_including_readline="$LIBS"
15730LIBS=`echo "$LIBS" | sed -e 's/-ledit//g' -e 's/-lreadline//g'`
15731
15732for ac_func in backtrace_symbols copyfile copy_file_range elf_aux_info getauxval getifaddrs getpeerucred inet_pton kqueue localeconv_l mbstowcs_l posix_fallocate ppoll pthread_is_threaded_np setproctitle setproctitle_fast strsignal syncfs sync_file_range uselocale wcstombs_l
15733do :
15734  as_ac_var=`$as_echo "ac_cv_func_$ac_func" | $as_tr_sh`
15735ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "$ac_func" "$as_ac_var"
15736if eval test \"x\$"$as_ac_var"\" = x"yes"; then :
15737  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
15738#define `$as_echo "HAVE_$ac_func" | $as_tr_cpp` 1
15739_ACEOF
15740
15741fi
15742done

15744

15746{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __builtin_bswap16" >&5

15756{
15757    return __builtin_bswap16(x);
15758}
15759int
15760main ()
15761{
15762
15763  ;
15764  return 0;
15765}

15872    return __builtin_clz(x);
15873}
15874int
15875main ()
15876{
15877
15878  ;
15879  return 0;
15880}
15881_ACEOF
15882if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
15883  pgac_cv__builtin_clz=yes
15884else
15885  pgac_cv__builtin_clz=no
15886fi
15887rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
15888    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
15889fi
15890{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv__builtin_clz" >&5
15891$as_echo "$pgac_cv__builtin_clz" >&6; }
15892if test x"${pgac_cv__builtin_clz}" = xyes ; then
15893
15894cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
15895#define HAVE__BUILTIN_CLZ 1
15896_ACEOF
15897
15898fi
15899{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __builtin_ctz" >&5
15900$as_echo_n "checking for __builtin_ctz... " >&6; }
15901if ${pgac_cv__builtin_ctz+:} false; then :
15902  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15903else
15904  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15905/* end confdefs.h.  */
15906
15907int
15908call__builtin_ctz(unsigned int x)
15909{
15910    return __builtin_ctz(x);
15911}
15912int
15913main ()
15914{
15915
15916  ;
15917  return 0;
15918}

15920if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :

15929$as_echo "$pgac_cv__builtin_ctz" >&6; }

15935

15938$as_echo_n "checking for __builtin_popcount... " >&6; }

15943/* end confdefs.h.  */

15955  return 0;

15958if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :

15967$as_echo "$pgac_cv__builtin_popcount" >&6; }
15968if test x"${pgac_cv__builtin_popcount}" = xyes ; then
15969

15971#define HAVE__BUILTIN_POPCOUNT 1
15972_ACEOF
15973
15974fi
15975# __builtin_frame_address may draw a diagnostic for non-constant argument,
15976# so it needs a different test function.
15977{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __builtin_frame_address" >&5
15978$as_echo_n "checking for __builtin_frame_address... " >&6; }
15979if ${pgac_cv__builtin_frame_address+:} false; then :
15980  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
15981else
15982  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
15983/* end confdefs.h.  */
15984
15985void *
15986call__builtin_frame_address(void)
15987{
15988    return __builtin_frame_address(0);
15989}
15990int
15991main ()
15992{
15993
15994  ;
15995  return 0;
15996}
15997_ACEOF
15998if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
15999  pgac_cv__builtin_frame_address=yes
16000else
16001  pgac_cv__builtin_frame_address=no
16002fi
16003rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16004    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16005fi
16006{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv__builtin_frame_address" >&5
16007$as_echo "$pgac_cv__builtin_frame_address" >&6; }
16008if test x"${pgac_cv__builtin_frame_address}" = xyes ; then
16009
16010cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16011#define HAVE__BUILTIN_FRAME_ADDRESS 1
16012_ACEOF
16013
16014fi
16015
16016# We require 64-bit fseeko() to be available, but run this check anyway
16017# in case it finds that _LARGEFILE_SOURCE has to be #define'd for that.
16018{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for _LARGEFILE_SOURCE value needed for large files" >&5
16019$as_echo_n "checking for _LARGEFILE_SOURCE value needed for large files... " >&6; }
16020if ${ac_cv_sys_largefile_source+:} false; then :
16021  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16022else
16023  while :; do
16024  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16025/* end confdefs.h.  */
16026#include <sys/types.h> /* for off_t */
16027     #include <stdio.h>
16028int
16029main ()
16030{
16031int (*fp) (FILE *, off_t, int) = fseeko;
16032     return fseeko (stdin, 0, 0) && fp (stdin, 0, 0);
16033  ;
16034  return 0;
16035}
16036_ACEOF
16037if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16038  ac_cv_sys_largefile_source=no; break
16039fi
16040rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16041    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16042  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16043/* end confdefs.h.  */
16044#define _LARGEFILE_SOURCE 1
16045#include <sys/types.h> /* for off_t */
16046     #include <stdio.h>
16047int
16048main ()
16049{
16050int (*fp) (FILE *, off_t, int) = fseeko;
16051     return fseeko (stdin, 0, 0) && fp (stdin, 0, 0);
16052  ;
16053  return 0;
16054}
16055_ACEOF
16056if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16057  ac_cv_sys_largefile_source=1; break
16058fi
16059rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16060    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16061  ac_cv_sys_largefile_source=unknown
16062  break
16063done
16064fi
16065{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sys_largefile_source" >&5
16066$as_echo "$ac_cv_sys_largefile_source" >&6; }
16067case $ac_cv_sys_largefile_source in #(
16068  no | unknown) ;;
16069  *)
16070cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16071#define _LARGEFILE_SOURCE $ac_cv_sys_largefile_source
16072_ACEOF
16073;;
16074esac
16075rm -rf conftest*
16076
16077# We used to try defining _XOPEN_SOURCE=500 too, to work around a bug
16078# in glibc 2.1.3, but that breaks too many other things.
16079# If you want fseeko and ftello with glibc, upgrade to a fixed glibc.
16080if test $ac_cv_sys_largefile_source != unknown; then
16081
16082$as_echo "#define HAVE_FSEEKO 1" >>confdefs.h
16083
16084fi
16085
16086
16087# posix_fadvise() is a no-op on Solaris, so don't incur function overhead
16088# by calling it, 2009-04-02
16089# http://src.opensolaris.org/source/xref/onnv/onnv-gate/usr/src/lib/libc/port/gen/posix_fadvise.c
16090# The Clang compiler raises a warning for an undeclared identifier that matches
16091# a compiler builtin function.  All extant Clang versions are affected, as of
16092# Clang 3.6.0.  Test a builtin known to every version.  This problem affects the
16093# C and Objective C languages, but Clang does report an error under C++ and
16094# Objective C++.
16095#
16096# Passing -fno-builtin to the compiler would suppress this problem.  That
16097# strategy would have the advantage of being insensitive to stray warnings, but
16098# it would make tests less realistic.
16099{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking how $CC reports undeclared, standard C functions" >&5
16100$as_echo_n "checking how $CC reports undeclared, standard C functions... " >&6; }
16101if ${ac_cv_c_decl_report+:} false; then :
16102  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16103else
16104  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16105/* end confdefs.h.  */
16106
16107int
16108main ()
16109{
16110(void) strchr;
16111  ;
16112  return 0;
16113}
16114_ACEOF
16115if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
16116  if test -s conftest.err; then :
16117      # For AC_CHECK_DECL to react to warnings, the compiler must be silent on
16118    # valid AC_CHECK_DECL input.  No library function is consistently available
16119    # on freestanding implementations, so test against a dummy declaration.
16120    # Include always-available headers on the off chance that they somehow
16121    # elicit warnings.
16122    cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16123/* end confdefs.h.  */
16124#include <float.h>
16125#include <limits.h>
16126#include <stdarg.h>
16127#include <stddef.h>
16128extern void ac_decl (int, char *);
16129int
16130main ()
16131{
16132#ifdef __cplusplus
16133  (void) ac_decl ((int) 0, (char *) 0);
16134  (void) ac_decl;
16135#else
16136  (void) ac_decl;
16137#endif
16138
16139  ;
16140  return 0;
16141}
16142_ACEOF
16143if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
16144  if test -s conftest.err; then :
16145  { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
16146$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
16147as_fn_error $? "cannot detect from compiler exit status or warnings
16148See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
16149else
16150  ac_cv_c_decl_report=warning
16151fi
16152else
16153  { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
16154$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
16155as_fn_error $? "cannot compile a simple declaration test
16156See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
16157fi
16158rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
16159else
16160  { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
16161$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
16162as_fn_error $? "compiler does not report undeclared identifiers
16163See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
16164fi
16165else
16166  ac_cv_c_decl_report=error
16167fi
16168rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
16169fi
16170{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_c_decl_report" >&5
16171$as_echo "$ac_cv_c_decl_report" >&6; }
16172
16173case $ac_cv_c_decl_report in
16174  warning) ac_c_decl_warn_flag=yes ;;
16175  *) ac_c_decl_warn_flag= ;;
16176esac
16177
16178if test "$PORTNAME" != "solaris"; then :
16179
16180for ac_func in posix_fadvise
16181do :
16182  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "posix_fadvise" "ac_cv_func_posix_fadvise"
16183if test "x$ac_cv_func_posix_fadvise" = xyes; then :
16184  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16185#define HAVE_POSIX_FADVISE 1
16186_ACEOF
16187
16188fi
16189done

16192"

16195else
16196  ac_have_decl=0
16197fi

16201_ACEOF
16202
16203
16204fi # fi

16207"

16212fi
16213

16216_ACEOF
16217
16218ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "strlcat" "ac_cv_have_decl_strlcat" "$ac_includes_default"
16219if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_strlcat" = xyes; then :

16227_ACEOF

16237_ACEOF
16238ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "strnlen" "ac_cv_have_decl_strnlen" "$ac_includes_default"
16239if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_strnlen" = xyes; then :
16240  ac_have_decl=1
16241else
16242  ac_have_decl=0
16243fi

16247_ACEOF
16248ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "strsep" "ac_cv_have_decl_strsep" "$ac_includes_default"
16249if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_strsep" = xyes; then :
16250  ac_have_decl=1
16251else
16252  ac_have_decl=0
16253fi
16254
16255cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF

16257_ACEOF
16258ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "timingsafe_bcmp" "ac_cv_have_decl_timingsafe_bcmp" "$ac_includes_default"
16259if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_timingsafe_bcmp" = xyes; then :
16260  ac_have_decl=1
16261else
16262  ac_have_decl=0
16263fi
16264
16265cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16266#define HAVE_DECL_TIMINGSAFE_BCMP $ac_have_decl
16267_ACEOF
16268
16269
16270# We can't use AC_CHECK_FUNCS to detect these functions, because it
16271# won't handle deployment target restrictions on macOS
16272ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "preadv" "ac_cv_have_decl_preadv" "#include <sys/uio.h>
16273"
16274if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_preadv" = xyes; then :
16275  ac_have_decl=1
16276else
16277  ac_have_decl=0
16278fi
16279

16285"
16286if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_pwritev" = xyes; then :
16287  ac_have_decl=1
16288else
16289  ac_have_decl=0
16290fi
16291
16292cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF

16294_ACEOF

16296ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "strchrnul" "ac_cv_have_decl_strchrnul" "#include <string.h>
16297"
16298if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_strchrnul" = xyes; then :
16299  ac_have_decl=1
16300else

16302fi
16303
16304cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16305#define HAVE_DECL_STRCHRNUL $ac_have_decl
16306_ACEOF
16307
16308ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "memset_s" "ac_cv_have_decl_memset_s" "#define __STDC_WANT_LIB_EXT1__ 1
16309#include <string.h>
16310"
16311if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_memset_s" = xyes; then :
16312  ac_have_decl=1
16313else
16314  ac_have_decl=0
16315fi
16316
16317cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16318#define HAVE_DECL_MEMSET_S $ac_have_decl
16319_ACEOF
16320
16321
16322# This is probably only present on macOS, but may as well check always
16323ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "F_FULLFSYNC" "ac_cv_have_decl_F_FULLFSYNC" "#include <fcntl.h>
16324"
16325if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_F_FULLFSYNC" = xyes; then :
16326  ac_have_decl=1
16327else
16328  ac_have_decl=0
16329fi
16330
16331cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16332#define HAVE_DECL_F_FULLFSYNC $ac_have_decl
16333_ACEOF
16334
16335
16336ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "explicit_bzero" "ac_cv_func_explicit_bzero"
16337if test "x$ac_cv_func_explicit_bzero" = xyes; then :
16338  $as_echo "#define HAVE_EXPLICIT_BZERO 1" >>confdefs.h
16339
16340else

16342  *" explicit_bzero.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16343  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS explicit_bzero.$ac_objext"
16344 ;;
16345esac
16346
16347fi
16348

16352

16357 ;;
16358esac
16359
16360fi
16361

16363if test "x$ac_cv_func_getpeereid" = xyes; then :
16364  $as_echo "#define HAVE_GETPEEREID 1" >>confdefs.h
16365
16366else

16369  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS getpeereid.$ac_objext"
16370 ;;

16372
16373fi
16374

16383 ;;
16384esac
16385
16386fi
16387

16389if test "x$ac_cv_func_mkdtemp" = xyes; then :

16391
16392else

16396 ;;

16399fi

16422 ;;

16430
16431else

16435 ;;
16436esac

16454if test "x$ac_cv_func_timingsafe_bcmp" = xyes; then :

16459  *" timingsafe_bcmp.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16460  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS timingsafe_bcmp.$ac_objext"
16461 ;;
