16466
16467
16468ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "pthread_barrier_wait" "ac_cv_func_pthread_barrier_wait"
16469if test "x$ac_cv_func_pthread_barrier_wait" = xyes; then :
16470  $as_echo "#define HAVE_PTHREAD_BARRIER_WAIT 1" >>confdefs.h
16471
16472else
16473  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16474  *" pthread_barrier_wait.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16475  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS pthread_barrier_wait.$ac_objext"
16476 ;;
16477esac
16478
16479fi
16480
16481
16482
16483if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32" -o "$PORTNAME" = "cygwin"; then
16484	# Cygwin and (apparently, based on test results) Mingw both
16485	# have a broken strtof(), so substitute its implementation.
16486	# That's not a perfect fix, since it doesn't avoid double-rounding,
16487	# but we have no better options.
16488	case " $LIBOBJS " in
16489  *" strtof.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16490  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS strtof.$ac_objext"
16491 ;;
16492esac
16493
16494	{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: On $host_os we will use our strtof wrapper." >&5
16495$as_echo "$as_me: On $host_os we will use our strtof wrapper." >&6;}
16496fi
16497
16498# Similarly, use system's getopt_long() only if system provides struct option.
16499if test x"$ac_cv_type_struct_option" = xyes ; then
16500  ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "getopt_long" "ac_cv_func_getopt_long"
16501if test "x$ac_cv_func_getopt_long" = xyes; then :
16502  $as_echo "#define HAVE_GETOPT_LONG 1" >>confdefs.h
16503
16504else
16505  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16506  *" getopt_long.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16507  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS getopt_long.$ac_objext"
16508 ;;
16509esac
16510
16511fi
16512
16513
16514else
16515  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16516  *" getopt_long.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16517  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS getopt_long.$ac_objext"
16518 ;;
16519esac
16520
16521fi
16522
16523# On OpenBSD and Solaris, getopt() doesn't do what we want for long options
16524# (i.e., allow '-' as a flag character), so use our version on those platforms.
16525if test "$PORTNAME" = "openbsd" -o "$PORTNAME" = "solaris"; then
16526  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16527  *" getopt.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16528  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS getopt.$ac_objext"
16529 ;;
16530esac
16531
16532fi
16533
16534# mingw has adopted a GNU-centric interpretation of optind/optreset,
16535# so always use our version on Windows.
16536if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32"; then
16537  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16538  *" getopt.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16539  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS getopt.$ac_objext"
16540 ;;
16541esac
16542
16543  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16544  *" getopt_long.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16545  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS getopt_long.$ac_objext"
16546 ;;
16547esac
16548
16549fi
16550
16551# Win32 (really MinGW) support
16552if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32"; then
16553  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16554  *" dirmod.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16555  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS dirmod.$ac_objext"
16556 ;;
16557esac
16558
16559  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16560  *" kill.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16561  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS kill.$ac_objext"
16562 ;;
16563esac
16564
16565  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16566  *" open.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16567  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS open.$ac_objext"
16568 ;;
16569esac
16570
16571  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16572  *" system.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16573  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS system.$ac_objext"
16574 ;;
16575esac
16576
16577  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16578  *" win32common.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16579  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32common.$ac_objext"
16580 ;;
16581esac
16582
16583  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16584  *" win32dlopen.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16585  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32dlopen.$ac_objext"
16586 ;;
16587esac
16588
16589  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16590  *" win32env.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16591  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32env.$ac_objext"
16592 ;;
16593esac
16594
16595  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16596  *" win32error.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16597  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32error.$ac_objext"
16598 ;;
16599esac
16600
16601  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16602  *" win32fdatasync.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16603  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32fdatasync.$ac_objext"
16604 ;;
16605esac
16606
16607  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16608  *" win32gai_strerror.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16609  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32gai_strerror.$ac_objext"
16610 ;;
16611esac
16612
16613  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16614  *" win32getrusage.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16615  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32getrusage.$ac_objext"
16616 ;;
16617esac
16618
16619  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16620  *" win32link.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16621  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32link.$ac_objext"
16622 ;;
16623esac
16624
16625  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16626  *" win32ntdll.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16627  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32ntdll.$ac_objext"
16628 ;;
16629esac
16630
16631  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16632  *" win32pread.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16633  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32pread.$ac_objext"
16634 ;;
16635esac
16636
16637  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16638  *" win32pwrite.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16639  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32pwrite.$ac_objext"
16640 ;;
16641esac
16642
16643  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16644  *" win32security.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16645  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32security.$ac_objext"
16646 ;;
16647esac
16648
16649  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16650  *" win32setlocale.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16651  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32setlocale.$ac_objext"
16652 ;;
16653esac
16654
16655  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16656  *" win32stat.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16657  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS win32stat.$ac_objext"
16658 ;;
16659esac
16660
16661fi
16662# Cygwin needs only a bit of that
16663if test "$PORTNAME" = "cygwin"; then
16664  case " $LIBOBJS " in
16665  *" dirmod.$ac_objext "* ) ;;
16666  *) LIBOBJS="$LIBOBJS dirmod.$ac_objext"
16667 ;;
16668esac
16669
16670fi
16671
16672ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "syslog" "ac_cv_func_syslog"
16673if test "x$ac_cv_func_syslog" = xyes; then :
16674  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "syslog.h" "ac_cv_header_syslog_h" "$ac_includes_default"
16675if test "x$ac_cv_header_syslog_h" = xyes; then :
16676
16677$as_echo "#define HAVE_SYSLOG 1" >>confdefs.h
16678
16679fi
16680
16681
16682fi
16683
16684
16685{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for opterr" >&5
16686$as_echo_n "checking for opterr... " >&6; }
16687if ${pgac_cv_var_int_opterr+:} false; then :
16688  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16689else
16690  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16691/* end confdefs.h.  */
16692#include <unistd.h>
16693int
16694main ()
16695{
16696extern int opterr; opterr = 1;
16697  ;
16698  return 0;
16699}
16700_ACEOF
16701if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16702  pgac_cv_var_int_opterr=yes
16703else
16704  pgac_cv_var_int_opterr=no
16705fi
16706rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16707    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16708fi
16709{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_var_int_opterr" >&5
16710$as_echo "$pgac_cv_var_int_opterr" >&6; }
16711if test x"$pgac_cv_var_int_opterr" = x"yes"; then
16712
16713$as_echo "#define HAVE_INT_OPTERR 1" >>confdefs.h
16714
16715fi
16716
16717{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for optreset" >&5
16718$as_echo_n "checking for optreset... " >&6; }
16719if ${pgac_cv_var_int_optreset+:} false; then :
16720  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16721else
16722  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16723/* end confdefs.h.  */
16724#include <unistd.h>
16725int
16726main ()
16727{
16728extern int optreset; optreset = 1;
16729  ;
16730  return 0;
16731}
16732_ACEOF
16733if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16734  pgac_cv_var_int_optreset=yes
16735else
16736  pgac_cv_var_int_optreset=no
16737fi
16738rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16739    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16740fi
16741{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_var_int_optreset" >&5
16742$as_echo "$pgac_cv_var_int_optreset" >&6; }
16743if test x"$pgac_cv_var_int_optreset" = x"yes"; then
16744
16745$as_echo "#define HAVE_INT_OPTRESET 1" >>confdefs.h
16746
16747fi
16748
16749if test "$with_icu" = yes; then
16750  ac_save_CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS
16751  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $ICU_CFLAGS"
16752
16753  # Verify we have ICU's header files
16754  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "unicode/ucol.h" "ac_cv_header_unicode_ucol_h" "$ac_includes_default"
16755if test "x$ac_cv_header_unicode_ucol_h" = xyes; then :
16756
16757else
16758  as_fn_error $? "header file <unicode/ucol.h> is required for ICU" "$LINENO" 5
16759fi
16760
16761
16762
16763  CPPFLAGS=$ac_save_CPPFLAGS
16764fi
16765
16766if test "$with_llvm" = yes; then
16767
16768  # Check which functionality is present
16769  SAVE_CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS"
16770  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $LLVM_CPPFLAGS"
16771  ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "LLVMCreateGDBRegistrationListener" "ac_cv_have_decl_LLVMCreateGDBRegistrationListener" "#include <llvm-c/ExecutionEngine.h>
16772"
16773if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_LLVMCreateGDBRegistrationListener" = xyes; then :
16774  ac_have_decl=1
16775else
16776  ac_have_decl=0
16777fi
16778
16779cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16780#define HAVE_DECL_LLVMCREATEGDBREGISTRATIONLISTENER $ac_have_decl
16781_ACEOF
16782ac_fn_c_check_decl "$LINENO" "LLVMCreatePerfJITEventListener" "ac_cv_have_decl_LLVMCreatePerfJITEventListener" "#include <llvm-c/ExecutionEngine.h>
16783"
16784if test "x$ac_cv_have_decl_LLVMCreatePerfJITEventListener" = xyes; then :
16785  ac_have_decl=1
16786else
16787  ac_have_decl=0
16788fi
16789
16790cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16791#define HAVE_DECL_LLVMCREATEPERFJITEVENTLISTENER $ac_have_decl
16792_ACEOF
16793
16794  CPPFLAGS="$SAVE_CPPFLAGS"
16795
16796fi
16797
16798# Lastly, restore full LIBS list and check for readline/libedit symbols
16799LIBS="$LIBS_including_readline"
16800
16801if test "$with_readline" = yes; then
16802  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for rl_completion_suppress_quote" >&5
16803$as_echo_n "checking for rl_completion_suppress_quote... " >&6; }
16804if ${pgac_cv_var_rl_completion_suppress_quote+:} false; then :
16805  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16806else
16807  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16808/* end confdefs.h.  */

16810#if defined(HAVE_READLINE_READLINE_H)
16811#include <readline/readline.h>
16812#elif defined(HAVE_EDITLINE_READLINE_H)
16813#include <editline/readline.h>
16814#elif defined(HAVE_READLINE_H)
16815#include <readline.h>
16816#endif
16817
16818int
16819main ()
16820{
16821rl_completion_suppress_quote = 1;
16822  ;
16823  return 0;
16824}
16825_ACEOF
16826if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16827  pgac_cv_var_rl_completion_suppress_quote=yes
16828else
16829  pgac_cv_var_rl_completion_suppress_quote=no
16830fi
16831rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16832    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16833fi
16834{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_var_rl_completion_suppress_quote" >&5
16835$as_echo "$pgac_cv_var_rl_completion_suppress_quote" >&6; }
16836if test x"$pgac_cv_var_rl_completion_suppress_quote" = x"yes"; then
16837
16838$as_echo "#define HAVE_RL_COMPLETION_SUPPRESS_QUOTE 1" >>confdefs.h
16839
16840fi
16841{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for rl_filename_quote_characters" >&5
16842$as_echo_n "checking for rl_filename_quote_characters... " >&6; }
16843if ${pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quote_characters+:} false; then :
16844  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16845else
16846  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext

16851#elif defined(HAVE_EDITLINE_READLINE_H)
16852#include <editline/readline.h>
16853#elif defined(HAVE_READLINE_H)
16854#include <readline.h>
16855#endif
16856
16857int
16858main ()
16859{
16860rl_filename_quote_characters = "x";
16861  ;
16862  return 0;
16863}
16864_ACEOF
16865if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16866  pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quote_characters=yes
16867else
16868  pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quote_characters=no
16869fi
16870rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16871    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16872fi
16873{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quote_characters" >&5
16874$as_echo "$pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quote_characters" >&6; }
16875if test x"$pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quote_characters" = x"yes"; then
16876
16877$as_echo "#define HAVE_RL_FILENAME_QUOTE_CHARACTERS 1" >>confdefs.h
16878
16879fi
16880{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for rl_filename_quoting_function" >&5
16881$as_echo_n "checking for rl_filename_quoting_function... " >&6; }
16882if ${pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quoting_function+:} false; then :
16883  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16884else
16885  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16886/* end confdefs.h.  */
16887#include <stdio.h>
16888#if defined(HAVE_READLINE_READLINE_H)
16889#include <readline/readline.h>
16890#elif defined(HAVE_EDITLINE_READLINE_H)
16891#include <editline/readline.h>
16892#elif defined(HAVE_READLINE_H)
16893#include <readline.h>
16894#endif
16895
16896int
16897main ()
16898{
16899rl_filename_quoting_function = 0;
16900  ;
16901  return 0;
16902}
16903_ACEOF
16904if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
16905  pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quoting_function=yes
16906else
16907  pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quoting_function=no
16908fi
16909rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
16910    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
16911fi
16912{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quoting_function" >&5
16913$as_echo "$pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quoting_function" >&6; }
16914if test x"$pgac_cv_var_rl_filename_quoting_function" = x"yes"; then
16915
16916$as_echo "#define HAVE_RL_FILENAME_QUOTING_FUNCTION 1" >>confdefs.h
16917
16918fi
16919
16920  for ac_func in append_history history_truncate_file rl_completion_matches rl_filename_completion_function rl_reset_screen_size rl_variable_bind
16921do :
16922  as_ac_var=`$as_echo "ac_cv_func_$ac_func" | $as_tr_sh`
16923ac_fn_c_check_func "$LINENO" "$ac_func" "$as_ac_var"
16924if eval test \"x\$"$as_ac_var"\" = x"yes"; then :
16925  cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16926#define `$as_echo "HAVE_$ac_func" | $as_tr_cpp` 1
16927_ACEOF
16928
16929fi
16930done
16931
16932fi
16933
16934
16935# This test makes sure that run tests work at all.  Sometimes a shared
16936# library is found by the linker, but the runtime linker can't find it.
16937# This check should come after all modifications of compiler or linker
16938# variables, and before any other run tests.
16939{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking test program" >&5
16940$as_echo_n "checking test program... " >&6; }
16941if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
16942  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: cross-compiling" >&5
16943$as_echo "cross-compiling" >&6; }
16944else
16945  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
16946/* end confdefs.h.  */
16947int main() { return 0; }
16948_ACEOF
16949if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
16950  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: ok" >&5
16951$as_echo "ok" >&6; }
16952else
16953  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: failed" >&5
16954$as_echo "failed" >&6; }
16955as_fn_error $? "
16956Could not execute a simple test program.  This may be a problem
16957related to locating shared libraries.  Check the file 'config.log'
16958for the exact reason." "$LINENO" 5
16959fi
16960rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
16961  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
16962fi
16963
16964
16965# --------------------
16966# Run tests below here
16967# --------------------
16968
16969# Check size of void *, size_t (enables tweaks for > 32bit address space)
16970# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler
16971# version HP92453-01 B.11.11.23709.GP, which incorrectly rejects
16972# declarations like `int a3[[(sizeof (unsigned char)) >= 0]];'.
16973# This bug is HP SR number 8606223364.
16974{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking size of void *" >&5
16975$as_echo_n "checking size of void *... " >&6; }
16976if ${ac_cv_sizeof_void_p+:} false; then :
16977  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
16978else
16979  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) (sizeof (void *))" "ac_cv_sizeof_void_p"        "$ac_includes_default"; then :
16980
16981else
16982  if test "$ac_cv_type_void_p" = yes; then
16983     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
16984$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
16985as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute sizeof (void *)
16986See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
16987   else
16988     ac_cv_sizeof_void_p=0
16989   fi
16990fi
16991
16992fi
16993{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sizeof_void_p" >&5
16994$as_echo "$ac_cv_sizeof_void_p" >&6; }
16995
16996
16997
16998cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
16999#define SIZEOF_VOID_P $ac_cv_sizeof_void_p
17000_ACEOF
17001
17002
17003# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler
17004# version HP92453-01 B.11.11.23709.GP, which incorrectly rejects
17005# declarations like `int a3[[(sizeof (unsigned char)) >= 0]];'.
17006# This bug is HP SR number 8606223364.
17007{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking size of size_t" >&5
17008$as_echo_n "checking size of size_t... " >&6; }
17009if ${ac_cv_sizeof_size_t+:} false; then :
17010  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17011else
17012  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) (sizeof (size_t))" "ac_cv_sizeof_size_t"        "$ac_includes_default"; then :
17013
17014else
17015  if test "$ac_cv_type_size_t" = yes; then
17016     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
17017$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
17018as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute sizeof (size_t)
17019See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17020   else
17021     ac_cv_sizeof_size_t=0
17022   fi
17023fi
17024
17025fi
17026{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sizeof_size_t" >&5
17027$as_echo "$ac_cv_sizeof_size_t" >&6; }
17028
17029
17030
17031cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17032#define SIZEOF_SIZE_T $ac_cv_sizeof_size_t
17033_ACEOF
17034
17035
17036# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler
17037# version HP92453-01 B.11.11.23709.GP, which incorrectly rejects
17038# declarations like `int a3[[(sizeof (unsigned char)) >= 0]];'.
17039# This bug is HP SR number 8606223364.
17040{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking size of long" >&5
17041$as_echo_n "checking size of long... " >&6; }
17042if ${ac_cv_sizeof_long+:} false; then :
17043  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17044else
17045  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) (sizeof (long))" "ac_cv_sizeof_long"        "$ac_includes_default"; then :
17046
17047else
17048  if test "$ac_cv_type_long" = yes; then
17049     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
17050$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
17051as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute sizeof (long)
17052See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17053   else
17054     ac_cv_sizeof_long=0
17055   fi
17056fi
17057
17058fi
17059{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sizeof_long" >&5
17060$as_echo "$ac_cv_sizeof_long" >&6; }
17061
17062
17063
17064cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17065#define SIZEOF_LONG $ac_cv_sizeof_long
17066_ACEOF
17067
17068
17069# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler
17070# version HP92453-01 B.11.11.23709.GP, which incorrectly rejects
17071# declarations like `int a3[[(sizeof (unsigned char)) >= 0]];'.
17072# This bug is HP SR number 8606223364.
17073{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking size of long long" >&5
17074$as_echo_n "checking size of long long... " >&6; }
17075if ${ac_cv_sizeof_long_long+:} false; then :
17076  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17077else
17078  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) (sizeof (long long))" "ac_cv_sizeof_long_long"        "$ac_includes_default"; then :
17079
17080else
17081  if test "$ac_cv_type_long_long" = yes; then
17082     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
17083$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
17084as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute sizeof (long long)
17085See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17086   else
17087     ac_cv_sizeof_long_long=0
17088   fi
17089fi
17090
17091fi
17092{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_sizeof_long_long" >&5
17093$as_echo "$ac_cv_sizeof_long_long" >&6; }

17096
17097cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17098#define SIZEOF_LONG_LONG $ac_cv_sizeof_long_long
17099_ACEOF
17100
17101
17102
17103# Determine memory alignment requirements for the basic C data types.
17104
17105# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler,
17106# see AC_CHECK_SIZEOF for more information.

17108$as_echo_n "checking alignment of short... " >&6; }
17109if ${ac_cv_alignof_short+:} false; then :

17111else
17112  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) offsetof (ac__type_alignof_, y)" "ac_cv_alignof_short"        "$ac_includes_default
17113#ifndef offsetof
17114# define offsetof(type, member) ((char *) &((type *) 0)->member - (char *) 0)
17115#endif
17116typedef struct { char x; short y; } ac__type_alignof_;"; then :
17117
17118else
17119  if test "$ac_cv_type_short" = yes; then
17120     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
17121$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
17122as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute alignment of short
17123See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17124   else
17125     ac_cv_alignof_short=0
17126   fi
17127fi
17128
17129fi
17130{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_alignof_short" >&5
17131$as_echo "$ac_cv_alignof_short" >&6; }
17132
17133
17134
17135cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17136#define ALIGNOF_SHORT $ac_cv_alignof_short
17137_ACEOF
17138
17139

17148#ifndef offsetof

17150#endif
17151typedef struct { char x; int y; } ac__type_alignof_;"; then :
17152
17153else
17154  if test "$ac_cv_type_int" = yes; then
17155     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5

17157as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute alignment of int
17158See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17159   else

17165{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_alignof_int" >&5
17166$as_echo "$ac_cv_alignof_int" >&6; }

17169
17170cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17171#define ALIGNOF_INT $ac_cv_alignof_int
17172_ACEOF
17173
17174
17175# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler,
17176# see AC_CHECK_SIZEOF for more information.
17177{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking alignment of long" >&5
17178$as_echo_n "checking alignment of long... " >&6; }
17179if ${ac_cv_alignof_long+:} false; then :

17185#endif

17192as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute alignment of long
17193See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17194   else

17200{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_alignof_long" >&5

17204

17206#define ALIGNOF_LONG $ac_cv_alignof_long
17207_ACEOF
17208
17209

17211# see AC_CHECK_SIZEOF for more information.
17212{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking alignment of int64_t" >&5

17220#endif

17223else
17224  if test "$ac_cv_type_int64_t" = yes; then
17225     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5

17227as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute alignment of int64_t
17228See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17229   else
17230     ac_cv_alignof_int64_t=0
17231   fi
17232fi
17233
17234fi
17235{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_alignof_int64_t" >&5
17236$as_echo "$ac_cv_alignof_int64_t" >&6; }
17237
17238
17239
17240cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17241#define ALIGNOF_INT64_T $ac_cv_alignof_int64_t
17242_ACEOF
17243
17244
17245# The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler,
17246# see AC_CHECK_SIZEOF for more information.
17247{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking alignment of double" >&5
17248$as_echo_n "checking alignment of double... " >&6; }
17249if ${ac_cv_alignof_double+:} false; then :
17250  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17251else
17252  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) offsetof (ac__type_alignof_, y)" "ac_cv_alignof_double"        "$ac_includes_default
17253#ifndef offsetof
17254# define offsetof(type, member) ((char *) &((type *) 0)->member - (char *) 0)
17255#endif
17256typedef struct { char x; double y; } ac__type_alignof_;"; then :
17257
17258else
17259  if test "$ac_cv_type_double" = yes; then
17260     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
17261$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
17262as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute alignment of double
17263See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17264   else
17265     ac_cv_alignof_double=0
17266   fi
17267fi
17268
17269fi
17270{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_alignof_double" >&5
17271$as_echo "$ac_cv_alignof_double" >&6; }
17272
17273
17274
17275cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17276#define ALIGNOF_DOUBLE $ac_cv_alignof_double
17277_ACEOF
17278
17279
17280
17281# Compute maximum alignment of any basic type.
17282#
17283# We require 'double' to have the strictest alignment among the basic types,
17284# because otherwise the C ABI might impose 8-byte alignment on some of the
17285# other C types that correspond to TYPALIGN_DOUBLE SQL types.  That could
17286# cause a mismatch between the tuple layout and the C struct layout of a
17287# catalog tuple.  We used to carefully order catalog columns such that any
17288# fixed-width, attalign=4 columns were at offsets divisible by 8 regardless
17289# of MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF to avoid that, but we no longer support any platforms
17290# where TYPALIGN_DOUBLE != MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF.
17291#
17292# We assume without checking that long's alignment is at least as strong as
17293# char, short, or int.  Note that we intentionally do not consider any types
17294# wider than 64 bits, as allowing MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF to exceed 8 would be too
17295# much of a penalty for disk and memory space.
17296
17297MAX_ALIGNOF=$ac_cv_alignof_double
17298
17299if test $ac_cv_alignof_long -gt $MAX_ALIGNOF ; then
17300  as_fn_error $? "alignment of 'long' is greater than the alignment of 'double'" "$LINENO" 5
17301fi
17302if test $ac_cv_alignof_int64_t -gt $MAX_ALIGNOF ; then
17303  as_fn_error $? "alignment of 'int64_t' is greater than the alignment of 'double'" "$LINENO" 5
17304fi
17305
17306cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17307#define MAXIMUM_ALIGNOF $MAX_ALIGNOF
17308_ACEOF
17309
17310
17311# Some compilers offer a 128-bit integer scalar type.
17312{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __int128" >&5
17313$as_echo_n "checking for __int128... " >&6; }
17314if ${pgac_cv__128bit_int+:} false; then :
17315  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17316else
17317  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17318/* end confdefs.h.  */
17319
17320/*
17321 * We don't actually run this test, just link it to verify that any support
17322 * functions needed for __int128 are present.
17323 *
17324 * These are globals to discourage the compiler from folding all the
17325 * arithmetic tests down to compile-time constants.  We do not have
17326 * convenient support for 128bit literals at this point...
17327 */
17328__int128 a = 48828125;
17329__int128 b = 97656250;
17330
17331int
17332main ()
17333{
17334
17335__int128 c,d;
17336a = (a << 12) + 1; /* 200000000001 */
17337b = (b << 12) + 5; /* 400000000005 */
17338/* try the most relevant arithmetic ops */
17339c = a * b;
17340d = (c + b) / b;
17341/* must use the results, else compiler may optimize arithmetic away */
17342if (d != a+1)
17343  return 1;
17344
17345  ;
17346  return 0;
17347}
17348_ACEOF
17349if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
17350  pgac_cv__128bit_int=yes
17351else
17352  pgac_cv__128bit_int=no
17353fi
17354rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17355    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17356fi
17357{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv__128bit_int" >&5
17358$as_echo "$pgac_cv__128bit_int" >&6; }
17359if test x"$pgac_cv__128bit_int" = xyes ; then
17360  # Use of non-default alignment with __int128 tickles bugs in some compilers.
17361  # If not cross-compiling, we can test for bugs and disable use of __int128
17362  # with buggy compilers.  If cross-compiling, hope for the best.
17363  # https://gcc.gnu.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=83925
17364  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __int128 alignment bug" >&5
17365$as_echo_n "checking for __int128 alignment bug... " >&6; }
17366if ${pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug+:} false; then :
17367  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17368else
17369  if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
17370  pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug="assuming ok"
17371else
17372  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17373/* end confdefs.h.  */
17374
17375/* This must match the corresponding code in c.h: */
17376#if defined(__GNUC__) || defined(__SUNPRO_C)
17377#define pg_attribute_aligned(a) __attribute__((aligned(a)))
17378#elif defined(_MSC_VER)
17379#define pg_attribute_aligned(a) __declspec(align(a))
17380#endif
17381typedef __int128 int128a
17382#if defined(pg_attribute_aligned)
17383pg_attribute_aligned(8)
17384#endif
17385;
17386int128a holder;
17387void pass_by_val(void *buffer, int128a par) { holder = par; }
17388
17389int
17390main ()
17391{
17392
17393long int i64 = 97656225L << 12;
17394int128a q;
17395pass_by_val(main, (int128a) i64);
17396q = (int128a) i64;
17397if (q != holder)
17398  return 1;
17399
17400  ;
17401  return 0;
17402}
17403_ACEOF
17404if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
17405  pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug=ok
17406else
17407  pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug=broken
17408fi
17409rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
17410  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
17411fi
17412
17413fi
17414{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug" >&5
17415$as_echo "$pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug" >&6; }
17416  if test x"$pgac_cv__128bit_int_bug" != xbroken ; then
17417
17418$as_echo "#define PG_INT128_TYPE __int128" >>confdefs.h
17419
17420    # The cast to long int works around a bug in the HP C Compiler,
17421# see AC_CHECK_SIZEOF for more information.
17422{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking alignment of PG_INT128_TYPE" >&5
17423$as_echo_n "checking alignment of PG_INT128_TYPE... " >&6; }
17424if ${ac_cv_alignof_PG_INT128_TYPE+:} false; then :
17425  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17426else
17427  if ac_fn_c_compute_int "$LINENO" "(long int) offsetof (ac__type_alignof_, y)" "ac_cv_alignof_PG_INT128_TYPE"        "$ac_includes_default
17428#ifndef offsetof
17429# define offsetof(type, member) ((char *) &((type *) 0)->member - (char *) 0)
17430#endif
17431typedef struct { char x; PG_INT128_TYPE y; } ac__type_alignof_;"; then :
17432
17433else
17434  if test "$ac_cv_type_PG_INT128_TYPE" = yes; then
17435     { { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&5
17436$as_echo "$as_me: error: in \`$ac_pwd':" >&2;}
17437as_fn_error 77 "cannot compute alignment of PG_INT128_TYPE
17438See \`config.log' for more details" "$LINENO" 5; }
17439   else
17440     ac_cv_alignof_PG_INT128_TYPE=0
17441   fi
17442fi
17443
17444fi
17445{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_alignof_PG_INT128_TYPE" >&5
17446$as_echo "$ac_cv_alignof_PG_INT128_TYPE" >&6; }
17447
17448
17449
17450cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
17451#define ALIGNOF_PG_INT128_TYPE $ac_cv_alignof_PG_INT128_TYPE
17452_ACEOF
17453
17454
17455  fi
17456fi
17457
17458# Check for various atomic operations now that we have checked how to declare
17459# 64bit integers.
17460{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for builtin __sync char locking functions" >&5
17461$as_echo_n "checking for builtin __sync char locking functions... " >&6; }
17462if ${pgac_cv_gcc_sync_char_tas+:} false; then :
17463  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17464else
17465  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17466/* end confdefs.h.  */
17467
17468int
17469main ()
17470{
17471char lock = 0;
17472   __sync_lock_test_and_set(&lock, 1);
17473   __sync_lock_release(&lock);
17474  ;
17475  return 0;
17476}
17477_ACEOF
17478if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
17479  pgac_cv_gcc_sync_char_tas="yes"
17480else
17481  pgac_cv_gcc_sync_char_tas="no"
17482fi
17483rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17484    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17485fi
17486{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_gcc_sync_char_tas" >&5
17487$as_echo "$pgac_cv_gcc_sync_char_tas" >&6; }
17488if test x"$pgac_cv_gcc_sync_char_tas" = x"yes"; then
17489
17490$as_echo "#define HAVE_GCC__SYNC_CHAR_TAS 1" >>confdefs.h
17491
17492fi
17493{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for builtin __sync int32 locking functions" >&5
17494$as_echo_n "checking for builtin __sync int32 locking functions... " >&6; }
17495if ${pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_tas+:} false; then :
17496  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17497else
17498  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17499/* end confdefs.h.  */
17500
17501int
17502main ()
17503{
17504int lock = 0;
17505   __sync_lock_test_and_set(&lock, 1);
17506   __sync_lock_release(&lock);
17507  ;
17508  return 0;
17509}
17510_ACEOF
17511if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
17512  pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_tas="yes"
17513else
17514  pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_tas="no"
17515fi
17516rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17517    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17518fi
17519{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_tas" >&5
17520$as_echo "$pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_tas" >&6; }
17521if test x"$pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_tas" = x"yes"; then
17522
17523$as_echo "#define HAVE_GCC__SYNC_INT32_TAS 1" >>confdefs.h
17524
17525fi
17526{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for builtin __sync int32 atomic operations" >&5
17527$as_echo_n "checking for builtin __sync int32 atomic operations... " >&6; }
17528if ${pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_cas+:} false; then :
17529  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17530else
17531  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17532/* end confdefs.h.  */
17533
17534int
17535main ()
17536{
17537int val = 0;
17538   __sync_val_compare_and_swap(&val, 0, 37);
17539  ;
17540  return 0;
17541}
17542_ACEOF
17543if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
17544  pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_cas="yes"
17545else
17546  pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_cas="no"
17547fi
17548rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17549    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17550fi
17551{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_cas" >&5
17552$as_echo "$pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_cas" >&6; }
17553if test x"$pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int32_cas" = x"yes"; then
17554
17555$as_echo "#define HAVE_GCC__SYNC_INT32_CAS 1" >>confdefs.h
17556
17557fi
17558{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for builtin __sync int64 atomic operations" >&5
17559$as_echo_n "checking for builtin __sync int64 atomic operations... " >&6; }
17560if ${pgac_cv_gcc_sync_int64_cas+:} false; then :
17561  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17562else
17563  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17564/* end confdefs.h.  */
17565#include <stdint.h>
17566int
17567main ()
17568{
17569int64_t lock = 0;
17570   __sync_val_compare_and_swap(&lock, 0, (int64_t) 37);
17571  ;
17572  return 0;
17573}
17574_ACEOF

17603   __atomic_compare_exchange_n(&val, &expect, 37, 0, __ATOMIC_SEQ_CST, __ATOMIC_RELAXED);
17604  ;
17605  return 0;

17672
17673  ;
17674  return 0;

17798# Check for XSAVE intrinsics
17799#
17800{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for _xgetbv" >&5
17801$as_echo_n "checking for _xgetbv... " >&6; }
17802if ${pgac_cv_xsave_intrinsics+:} false; then :
17803  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17804else
17805  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17806/* end confdefs.h.  */
17807#include <immintrin.h>
17808    #if defined(__has_attribute) && __has_attribute (target)
17809    __attribute__((target("xsave")))
17810    #endif
17811    static int xsave_test(void)
17812    {
17813      return _xgetbv(0) & 0xe0;
17814    }
17815int
17816main ()
17817{
17818return xsave_test();
17819  ;
17820  return 0;
17821}
17822_ACEOF
17823if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :

17825else
17826  pgac_cv_xsave_intrinsics=no
17827fi
17828rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17829    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17830fi
17831{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_xsave_intrinsics" >&5
17832$as_echo "$pgac_cv_xsave_intrinsics" >&6; }
17833if test x"$pgac_cv_xsave_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
17834  pgac_xsave_intrinsics=yes
17835fi
17836

17838
17839$as_echo "#define HAVE_XSAVE_INTRINSICS 1" >>confdefs.h
17840
17841fi
17842
17843# Check for AVX-512 popcount intrinsics
17844#
17845if test x"$host_cpu" = x"x86_64"; then
17846  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for _mm512_popcnt_epi64" >&5
17847$as_echo_n "checking for _mm512_popcnt_epi64... " >&6; }
17848if ${pgac_cv_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics+:} false; then :
17849  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17850else
17851  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17852/* end confdefs.h.  */
17853#include <immintrin.h>
17854    #include <stdint.h>
17855    char buf[sizeof(__m512i)];
17856
17857    #if defined(__has_attribute) && __has_attribute (target)
17858    __attribute__((target("avx512vpopcntdq,avx512bw")))
17859    #endif
17860    static int popcount_test(void)
17861    {
17862      int64_t popcnt = 0;
17863      __m512i accum = _mm512_setzero_si512();
17864      __m512i val = _mm512_maskz_loadu_epi8((__mmask64) 0xf0f0f0f0f0f0f0f0, (const __m512i *) buf);
17865      __m512i cnt = _mm512_popcnt_epi64(val);
17866      accum = _mm512_add_epi64(accum, cnt);
17867      popcnt = _mm512_reduce_add_epi64(accum);
17868      return (int) popcnt;
17869    }
17870int
17871main ()
17872{
17873return popcount_test();
17874  ;
17875  return 0;
17876}
17877_ACEOF
17878if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
17879  pgac_cv_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics=yes
17880else
17881  pgac_cv_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics=no
17882fi
17883rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17884    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17885fi
17886{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics" >&5
17887$as_echo "$pgac_cv_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics" >&6; }
17888if test x"$pgac_cv_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
17889  pgac_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics=yes
17890fi
17891
17892  if test x"$pgac_avx512_popcnt_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
17893
17894$as_echo "#define USE_AVX512_POPCNT_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK 1" >>confdefs.h
17895
17896  fi
17897fi
17898
17899# Check for SVE popcount intrinsics
17900#
17901if test x"$host_cpu" = x"aarch64"; then
17902  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for svcnt_x" >&5
17903$as_echo_n "checking for svcnt_x... " >&6; }
17904if ${pgac_cv_sve_popcnt_intrinsics+:} false; then :
17905  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17906else
17907  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17908/* end confdefs.h.  */
17909#include <arm_sve.h>
17910
17911	char buf[128];
17912
17913	#if defined(__has_attribute) && __has_attribute (target)
17914	__attribute__((target("arch=armv8-a+sve")))
17915	#endif
17916	static int popcount_test(void)
17917	{
17918		svbool_t	pred = svptrue_b64();
17919		svuint8_t	vec8;
17920		svuint64_t	accum1 = svdup_u64(0),
17921					accum2 = svdup_u64(0),
17922					vec64;
17923		char	   *p = buf;
17924		uint64_t	popcnt,
17925					mask = 0x5555555555555555;
17926
17927		vec64 = svand_n_u64_x(pred, svld1_u64(pred, (const uint64_t *) p), mask);
17928		accum1 = svadd_u64_x(pred, accum1, svcnt_u64_x(pred, vec64));
17929		p += svcntb();
17930
17931		vec64 = svand_n_u64_x(pred, svld1_u64(pred, (const uint64_t *) p), mask);
17932		accum2 = svadd_u64_x(pred, accum2, svcnt_u64_x(pred, vec64));
17933		p += svcntb();
17934
17935		popcnt = svaddv_u64(pred, svadd_u64_x(pred, accum1, accum2));
17936
17937		pred = svwhilelt_b8_s32(0, sizeof(buf));
17938		vec8 = svand_n_u8_x(pred, svld1_u8(pred, (const uint8_t *) p), 0x55);
17939		return (int) (popcnt + svaddv_u8(pred, svcnt_u8_x(pred, vec8)));
17940	}
17941int
17942main ()
17943{
17944return popcount_test();
17945  ;
17946  return 0;
17947}
17948_ACEOF
17949if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
17950  pgac_cv_sve_popcnt_intrinsics=yes
17951else
17952  pgac_cv_sve_popcnt_intrinsics=no
17953fi
17954rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
17955    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
17956fi
17957{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_sve_popcnt_intrinsics" >&5
17958$as_echo "$pgac_cv_sve_popcnt_intrinsics" >&6; }
17959if test x"$pgac_cv_sve_popcnt_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
17960  pgac_sve_popcnt_intrinsics=yes
17961fi
17962
17963  if test x"$pgac_sve_popcnt_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
17964
17965$as_echo "#define USE_SVE_POPCNT_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK 1" >>confdefs.h
17966
17967  fi
17968fi
17969
17970# Check for Intel SSE 4.2 intrinsics to do CRC calculations.
17971#
17972{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for _mm_crc32_u8 and _mm_crc32_u32" >&5
17973$as_echo_n "checking for _mm_crc32_u8 and _mm_crc32_u32... " >&6; }
17974if ${pgac_cv_sse42_crc32_intrinsics+:} false; then :
17975  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
17976else
17977  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
17978/* end confdefs.h.  */
17979#include <nmmintrin.h>
17980    unsigned int crc;
17981    #if defined(__has_attribute) && __has_attribute (target)
17982    __attribute__((target("sse4.2")))
17983    #endif
17984    static int crc32_sse42_test(void)
17985    {
17986      crc = _mm_crc32_u8(crc, 0);
17987      crc = _mm_crc32_u32(crc, 0);
17988      /* return computed value, to prevent the above being optimized away */
17989      return crc == 0;
17990    }
17991int
17992main ()
17993{
17994return crc32_sse42_test();
17995  ;
17996  return 0;
17997}
17998_ACEOF
17999if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18000  pgac_cv_sse42_crc32_intrinsics=yes
18001else
18002  pgac_cv_sse42_crc32_intrinsics=no
18003fi
18004rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18005    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
18006fi
18007{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_sse42_crc32_intrinsics" >&5
18008$as_echo "$pgac_cv_sse42_crc32_intrinsics" >&6; }
18009if test x"$pgac_cv_sse42_crc32_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
18010  pgac_sse42_crc32_intrinsics=yes
18011fi
18012
18013
18014# Are we targeting a processor that supports SSE 4.2? gcc, clang and icc all
18015# define __SSE4_2__ in that case.
18016cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18017/* end confdefs.h.  */
18018
18019int
18020main ()
18021{
18022
18023#ifndef __SSE4_2__
18024#error __SSE4_2__ not defined
18025#endif
18026
18027  ;
18028  return 0;
18029}
18030_ACEOF
18031if ac_fn_c_try_compile "$LINENO"; then :
18032  SSE4_2_TARGETED=1
18033fi
18034rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext conftest.$ac_ext
18035
18036# Check for ARMv8 CRC Extension intrinsics to do CRC calculations.
18037#
18038# First check if __crc32c* intrinsics can be used with the default compiler
18039# flags. If not, check if adding "-march=armv8-a+crc+simd" flag helps.
18040# On systems using soft-float ABI, "-march=armv8-a+crc" is required instead.
18041# CFLAGS_CRC is set if the extra flag is required.
18042{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __crc32cb, __crc32ch, __crc32cw, and __crc32cd with CFLAGS=" >&5
18043$as_echo_n "checking for __crc32cb, __crc32ch, __crc32cw, and __crc32cd with CFLAGS=... " >&6; }
18044if ${pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics_+:} false; then :
18045  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18046else
18047  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
18048CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS "
18049cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18050/* end confdefs.h.  */
18051#include <arm_acle.h>
18052unsigned int crc;
18053int
18054main ()
18055{
18056crc = __crc32cb(crc, 0);
18057   crc = __crc32ch(crc, 0);
18058   crc = __crc32cw(crc, 0);
18059   crc = __crc32cd(crc, 0);
18060   /* return computed value, to prevent the above being optimized away */
18061   return crc == 0;
18062  ;
18063  return 0;
18064}
18065_ACEOF
18066if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18067  pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics_=yes
18068else
18069  pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics_=no
18070fi
18071rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18072    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
18073CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
18074fi
18075{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics_" >&5
18076$as_echo "$pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics_" >&6; }
18077if test x"$pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics_" = x"yes"; then
18078  CFLAGS_CRC=""
18079  pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics=yes
18080fi
18081
18082if test x"$pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics" != x"yes"; then
18083  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __crc32cb, __crc32ch, __crc32cw, and __crc32cd with CFLAGS=-march=armv8-a+crc+simd" >&5
18084$as_echo_n "checking for __crc32cb, __crc32ch, __crc32cw, and __crc32cd with CFLAGS=-march=armv8-a+crc+simd... " >&6; }
18085if ${pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrcpsimd+:} false; then :
18086  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18087else
18088  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
18089CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS -march=armv8-a+crc+simd"
18090cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18091/* end confdefs.h.  */
18092#include <arm_acle.h>
18093unsigned int crc;
18094int
18095main ()
18096{
18097crc = __crc32cb(crc, 0);
18098   crc = __crc32ch(crc, 0);
18099   crc = __crc32cw(crc, 0);
18100   crc = __crc32cd(crc, 0);
18101   /* return computed value, to prevent the above being optimized away */
18102   return crc == 0;
18103  ;
18104  return 0;
18105}
18106_ACEOF
18107if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18108  pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrcpsimd=yes
18109else
18110  pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrcpsimd=no
18111fi
18112rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18113    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
18114CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
18115fi
18116{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrcpsimd" >&5
18117$as_echo "$pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrcpsimd" >&6; }
18118if test x"$pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrcpsimd" = x"yes"; then
18119  CFLAGS_CRC="-march=armv8-a+crc+simd"
18120  pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics=yes
18121fi
18122
18123  if test x"$pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics" != x"yes"; then
18124    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __crc32cb, __crc32ch, __crc32cw, and __crc32cd with CFLAGS=-march=armv8-a+crc" >&5
18125$as_echo_n "checking for __crc32cb, __crc32ch, __crc32cw, and __crc32cd with CFLAGS=-march=armv8-a+crc... " >&6; }
18126if ${pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrc+:} false; then :
18127  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18128else
18129  pgac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
18130CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS -march=armv8-a+crc"
18131cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18132/* end confdefs.h.  */
18133#include <arm_acle.h>
18134unsigned int crc;
18135int
18136main ()
18137{
18138crc = __crc32cb(crc, 0);
18139   crc = __crc32ch(crc, 0);
18140   crc = __crc32cw(crc, 0);
18141   crc = __crc32cd(crc, 0);
18142   /* return computed value, to prevent the above being optimized away */
18143   return crc == 0;
18144  ;
18145  return 0;
18146}
18147_ACEOF
18148if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18149  pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrc=yes
18150else
18151  pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrc=no
18152fi
18153rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18154    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
18155CFLAGS="$pgac_save_CFLAGS"
18156fi
18157{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrc" >&5
18158$as_echo "$pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrc" >&6; }
18159if test x"$pgac_cv_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics__march_armv8_apcrc" = x"yes"; then
18160  CFLAGS_CRC="-march=armv8-a+crc"
18161  pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics=yes
18162fi
18163
18164  fi
18165fi
18166
18167# Check for LoongArch CRC intrinsics to do CRC calculations.
18168#
18169# Check if __builtin_loongarch_crcc_* intrinsics can be used
18170# with the default compiler flags.
18171{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_b_w, __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_h_w, __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_w_w and __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_d_w" >&5
18172$as_echo_n "checking for __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_b_w, __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_h_w, __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_w_w and __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_d_w... " >&6; }
18173if ${pgac_cv_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics+:} false; then :
18174  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18175else
18176  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18177/* end confdefs.h.  */
18178unsigned int crc;
18179int
18180main ()
18181{
18182crc = __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_b_w(0, crc);
18183   crc = __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_h_w(0, crc);
18184   crc = __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_w_w(0, crc);
18185   crc = __builtin_loongarch_crcc_w_d_w(0, crc);
18186   /* return computed value, to prevent the above being optimized away */
18187   return crc == 0;
18188  ;
18189  return 0;
18190}
18191_ACEOF
18192if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18193  pgac_cv_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics=yes
18194else
18195  pgac_cv_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics=no
18196fi
18197rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18198    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
18199fi
18200{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics" >&5
18201$as_echo "$pgac_cv_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics" >&6; }
18202if test x"$pgac_cv_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
18203  pgac_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics=yes
18204fi
18205
18206
18207
18208
18209# Select CRC-32C implementation.
18210#
18211# There are three methods of calculating CRC, in order of increasing
18212# performance:
18213#
18214# 1. The fallback using a lookup table, called slicing-by-8
18215# 2. CRC-32C instructions (found in e.g. Intel SSE 4.2 and ARMv8 CRC Extension)
18216# 3. Algorithms using carryless multiplication instructions
18217#    (e.g. Intel PCLMUL and Arm PMULL)
18218#
18219# If we can produce code (via function attributes or additional compiler
18220# flags) that uses #2 (and possibly #3), we compile all implementations
18221# and select which one to use at runtime, depending on what is supported
18222# by the processor we're running on.
18223#
18224# If we are targeting a processor that has #2, we can use that without
18225# runtime selection.
18226#
18227# Note that we do not use __attribute__((target("..."))) for the ARM CRC
18228# instructions because until clang 16, using the ARM intrinsics still requires
18229# special -march flags.  Perhaps we can re-evaluate this decision after some
18230# time has passed.
18231#
18232# You can skip the runtime check by setting the appropriate USE_*_CRC32 flag to 1
18233# in the template or configure command line.
18234#
18235# If we are targeting a LoongArch processor, CRC instructions are
18236# always available (at least on 64 bit), so no runtime check is needed.
18237if test x"$USE_SLICING_BY_8_CRC32C" = x"" && test x"$USE_SSE42_CRC32C" = x"" && test x"$USE_SSE42_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK" = x"" && test x"$USE_ARMV8_CRC32C" = x"" && test x"$USE_ARMV8_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK" = x"" && test x"$USE_LOONGARCH_CRC32C" = x""; then
18238  # Use Intel SSE 4.2 if available.
18239  if test x"$pgac_sse42_crc32_intrinsics" = x"yes" && test x"$SSE4_2_TARGETED" = x"1" ; then
18240    USE_SSE42_CRC32C=1
18241  else
18242    # Intel SSE 4.2, with runtime check? The CPUID instruction is needed for
18243    # the runtime check.
18244    if test x"$pgac_sse42_crc32_intrinsics" = x"yes" && (test x"$pgac_cv__get_cpuid" = x"yes" || test x"$pgac_cv__cpuid" = x"yes"); then
18245      USE_SSE42_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK=1
18246    else
18247      # Use ARM CRC Extension if available.
18248      if test x"$pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics" = x"yes" && test x"$CFLAGS_CRC" = x""; then
18249        USE_ARMV8_CRC32C=1
18250      else
18251        # ARM CRC Extension, with runtime check?
18252        if test x"$pgac_armv8_crc32c_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
18253          USE_ARMV8_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK=1
18254        else
18255          # LoongArch CRCC instructions.
18256          if test x"$pgac_loongarch_crc32c_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
18257            USE_LOONGARCH_CRC32C=1
18258          else
18259            # fall back to slicing-by-8 algorithm, which doesn't require any
18260            # special CPU support.
18261            USE_SLICING_BY_8_CRC32C=1
18262          fi
18263        fi
18264      fi
18265    fi
18266  fi
18267fi
18268
18269# Set PG_CRC32C_OBJS appropriately depending on the selected implementation.
18270{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking which CRC-32C implementation to use" >&5
18271$as_echo_n "checking which CRC-32C implementation to use... " >&6; }
18272if test x"$USE_SSE42_CRC32C" = x"1"; then
18273
18274$as_echo "#define USE_SSE42_CRC32C 1" >>confdefs.h
18275
18276  PG_CRC32C_OBJS="pg_crc32c_sse42.o pg_crc32c_sse42_choose.o"
18277  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: SSE 4.2" >&5
18278$as_echo "SSE 4.2" >&6; }
18279else
18280  if test x"$USE_SSE42_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK" = x"1"; then
18281
18282$as_echo "#define USE_SSE42_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK 1" >>confdefs.h
18283
18284    PG_CRC32C_OBJS="pg_crc32c_sse42.o pg_crc32c_sb8.o pg_crc32c_sse42_choose.o"
18285    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: SSE 4.2 with runtime check" >&5
18286$as_echo "SSE 4.2 with runtime check" >&6; }
18287  else
18288    if test x"$USE_ARMV8_CRC32C" = x"1"; then
18289
18290$as_echo "#define USE_ARMV8_CRC32C 1" >>confdefs.h
18291
18292      PG_CRC32C_OBJS="pg_crc32c_armv8.o"
18293      { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: ARMv8 CRC instructions" >&5
18294$as_echo "ARMv8 CRC instructions" >&6; }
18295    else
18296      if test x"$USE_ARMV8_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK" = x"1"; then
18297
18298$as_echo "#define USE_ARMV8_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK 1" >>confdefs.h
18299
18300        PG_CRC32C_OBJS="pg_crc32c_armv8.o pg_crc32c_sb8.o pg_crc32c_armv8_choose.o"
18301        { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: ARMv8 CRC instructions with runtime check" >&5
18302$as_echo "ARMv8 CRC instructions with runtime check" >&6; }
18303      else
18304        if test x"$USE_LOONGARCH_CRC32C" = x"1"; then
18305
18306$as_echo "#define USE_LOONGARCH_CRC32C 1" >>confdefs.h
18307
18308          PG_CRC32C_OBJS="pg_crc32c_loongarch.o"
18309          { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: LoongArch CRCC instructions" >&5
18310$as_echo "LoongArch CRCC instructions" >&6; }
18311        else
18312
18313$as_echo "#define USE_SLICING_BY_8_CRC32C 1" >>confdefs.h
18314
18315          PG_CRC32C_OBJS="pg_crc32c_sb8.o"
18316          { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: slicing-by-8" >&5
18317$as_echo "slicing-by-8" >&6; }
18318        fi
18319      fi
18320    fi
18321  fi
18322fi
18323
18324
18325# Check for carryless multiplication intrinsics to do vectorized CRC calculations.
18326#
18327if test x"$host_cpu" = x"x86_64"; then
18328  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for _mm512_clmulepi64_epi128" >&5
18329$as_echo_n "checking for _mm512_clmulepi64_epi128... " >&6; }
18330if ${pgac_cv_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics+:} false; then :
18331  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18332else
18333  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18334/* end confdefs.h.  */
18335#include <immintrin.h>
18336    __m512i x;
18337    __m512i y;
18338
18339    #if defined(__has_attribute) && __has_attribute (target)
18340    __attribute__((target("vpclmulqdq,avx512vl")))
18341    #endif
18342    static int avx512_pclmul_test(void)
18343    {
18344      __m128i z;
18345
18346      x = _mm512_xor_si512(_mm512_zextsi128_si512(_mm_cvtsi32_si128(0)), x);
18347      y = _mm512_clmulepi64_epi128(x, y, 0);
18348      z = _mm_ternarylogic_epi64(
18349                _mm512_castsi512_si128(y),
18350                _mm512_extracti32x4_epi32(y, 1),
18351                _mm512_extracti32x4_epi32(y, 2),
18352                0x96);
18353      return _mm_crc32_u64(0, _mm_extract_epi64(z, 0));
18354    }
18355int
18356main ()
18357{
18358return avx512_pclmul_test();
18359  ;
18360  return 0;
18361}
18362_ACEOF
18363if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18364  pgac_cv_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics=yes
18365else
18366  pgac_cv_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics=no
18367fi
18368rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18369    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
18370fi
18371{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics" >&5
18372$as_echo "$pgac_cv_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics" >&6; }
18373if test x"$pgac_cv_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
18374  pgac_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics=yes
18375fi
18376
18377fi
18378
18379{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for vectorized CRC-32C" >&5
18380$as_echo_n "checking for vectorized CRC-32C... " >&6; }
18381if test x"$pgac_avx512_pclmul_intrinsics" = x"yes"; then
18382
18383$as_echo "#define USE_AVX512_CRC32C_WITH_RUNTIME_CHECK 1" >>confdefs.h
18384
18385  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: AVX-512 with runtime check" >&5
18386$as_echo "AVX-512 with runtime check" >&6; }
18387else
18388  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: none" >&5
18389$as_echo "none" >&6; }
18390fi
18391
18392# Select semaphore implementation type.
18393if test "$PORTNAME" != "win32"; then
18394  if test x"$PREFERRED_SEMAPHORES" = x"NAMED_POSIX" ; then
18395    # Need sem_open for this
18396    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing sem_open" >&5
18397$as_echo_n "checking for library containing sem_open... " >&6; }
18398if ${ac_cv_search_sem_open+:} false; then :
18399  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18400else
18401  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
18402cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18403/* end confdefs.h.  */
18404
18405/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
18406   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
18407   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
18408#ifdef __cplusplus
18409extern "C"
18410#endif
18411char sem_open ();
18412int
18413main ()
18414{
18415return sem_open ();
18416  ;
18417  return 0;
18418}
18419_ACEOF
18420for ac_lib in '' rt pthread; do
18421  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
18422    ac_res="none required"
18423  else
18424    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
18425    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
18426  fi
18427  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18428  ac_cv_search_sem_open=$ac_res
18429fi
18430rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18431    conftest$ac_exeext
18432  if ${ac_cv_search_sem_open+:} false; then :
18433  break
18434fi
18435done
18436if ${ac_cv_search_sem_open+:} false; then :
18437
18438else
18439  ac_cv_search_sem_open=no
18440fi
18441rm conftest.$ac_ext
18442LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
18443fi
18444{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_sem_open" >&5
18445$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_sem_open" >&6; }
18446ac_res=$ac_cv_search_sem_open
18447if test "$ac_res" != no; then :
18448  test "$ac_res" = "none required" || LIBS="$ac_res $LIBS"
18449  USE_NAMED_POSIX_SEMAPHORES=1
18450fi
18451
18452  fi
18453  if test x"$PREFERRED_SEMAPHORES" = x"UNNAMED_POSIX" ; then
18454    # Need sem_init for this
18455    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing sem_init" >&5
18456$as_echo_n "checking for library containing sem_init... " >&6; }
18457if ${ac_cv_search_sem_init+:} false; then :
18458  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18459else
18460  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
18461cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18462/* end confdefs.h.  */
18463
18464/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
18465   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
18466   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
18467#ifdef __cplusplus
18468extern "C"
18469#endif
18470char sem_init ();
18471int
18472main ()
18473{
18474return sem_init ();
18475  ;
18476  return 0;
18477}
18478_ACEOF
18479for ac_lib in '' rt pthread; do
18480  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
18481    ac_res="none required"
18482  else
18483    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
18484    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
18485  fi
18486  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18487  ac_cv_search_sem_init=$ac_res
18488fi
18489rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18490    conftest$ac_exeext
18491  if ${ac_cv_search_sem_init+:} false; then :
18492  break
18493fi
18494done
18495if ${ac_cv_search_sem_init+:} false; then :
18496
18497else
18498  ac_cv_search_sem_init=no
18499fi
18500rm conftest.$ac_ext
18501LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
18502fi
18503{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_sem_init" >&5
18504$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_sem_init" >&6; }
18505ac_res=$ac_cv_search_sem_init
18506if test "$ac_res" != no; then :
18507  test "$ac_res" = "none required" || LIBS="$ac_res $LIBS"
18508  USE_UNNAMED_POSIX_SEMAPHORES=1
18509fi
18510
18511  fi
18512  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking which semaphore API to use" >&5
18513$as_echo_n "checking which semaphore API to use... " >&6; }
18514  if test x"$USE_NAMED_POSIX_SEMAPHORES" = x"1" ; then
18515
18516$as_echo "#define USE_NAMED_POSIX_SEMAPHORES 1" >>confdefs.h
18517
18518    SEMA_IMPLEMENTATION="src/backend/port/posix_sema.c"
18519    sematype="named POSIX"
18520  else
18521    if test x"$USE_UNNAMED_POSIX_SEMAPHORES" = x"1" ; then
18522
18523$as_echo "#define USE_UNNAMED_POSIX_SEMAPHORES 1" >>confdefs.h
18524
18525      SEMA_IMPLEMENTATION="src/backend/port/posix_sema.c"
18526      sematype="unnamed POSIX"
18527    else
18528
18529$as_echo "#define USE_SYSV_SEMAPHORES 1" >>confdefs.h
18530
18531      SEMA_IMPLEMENTATION="src/backend/port/sysv_sema.c"
18532      sematype="System V"
18533    fi
18534  fi
18535  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $sematype" >&5
18536$as_echo "$sematype" >&6; }
18537else
18538
18539$as_echo "#define USE_WIN32_SEMAPHORES 1" >>confdefs.h
18540
18541  SEMA_IMPLEMENTATION="src/backend/port/win32_sema.c"
18542fi
18543
18544
18545# Select shared-memory implementation type.
18546if test "$PORTNAME" != "win32"; then
18547
18548$as_echo "#define USE_SYSV_SHARED_MEMORY 1" >>confdefs.h
18549
18550  SHMEM_IMPLEMENTATION="src/backend/port/sysv_shmem.c"
18551else
18552
18553$as_echo "#define USE_WIN32_SHARED_MEMORY 1" >>confdefs.h
18554
18555  SHMEM_IMPLEMENTATION="src/backend/port/win32_shmem.c"
18556fi
18557
18558# Select random number source. If a TLS library is used then it will be the
18559# first choice, else the native platform sources (Windows API or /dev/urandom)
18560# will be used.
18561{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking which random number source to use" >&5
18562$as_echo_n "checking which random number source to use... " >&6; }
18563if test x"$with_ssl" = x"openssl" ; then
18564  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: OpenSSL" >&5
18565$as_echo "OpenSSL" >&6; }
18566elif test x"$PORTNAME" = x"win32" ; then
18567  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: Windows native" >&5
18568$as_echo "Windows native" >&6; }
18569elif test x"$cross_compiling" = x"yes"; then
18570  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: assuming /dev/urandom" >&5
18571$as_echo "assuming /dev/urandom" >&6; }
18572else
18573  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: /dev/urandom" >&5
18574$as_echo "/dev/urandom" >&6; }
18575  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for /dev/urandom" >&5
18576$as_echo_n "checking for /dev/urandom... " >&6; }
18577if ${ac_cv_file__dev_urandom+:} false; then :
18578  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18579else
18580  test "$cross_compiling" = yes &&
18581  as_fn_error $? "cannot check for file existence when cross compiling" "$LINENO" 5
18582if test -r "/dev/urandom"; then
18583  ac_cv_file__dev_urandom=yes
18584else
18585  ac_cv_file__dev_urandom=no
18586fi
18587fi
18588{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_file__dev_urandom" >&5
18589$as_echo "$ac_cv_file__dev_urandom" >&6; }
18590if test "x$ac_cv_file__dev_urandom" = xyes; then :
18591
18592fi
18593
18594
18595  if test x"$ac_cv_file__dev_urandom" = x"no" ; then
18596    as_fn_error $? "
18597no source of strong random numbers was found
18598PostgreSQL can use OpenSSL, native Windows API or /dev/urandom as a source of random numbers." "$LINENO" 5
18599  fi
18600fi
18601
18602# If not set in template file, set bytes to use libc memset()
18603if test x"$MEMSET_LOOP_LIMIT" = x"" ; then
18604  MEMSET_LOOP_LIMIT=1024
18605fi
18606
18607cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
18608#define MEMSET_LOOP_LIMIT ${MEMSET_LOOP_LIMIT}
18609_ACEOF
18610
18611
18612
18613if test "$enable_nls" = yes ; then
18614
18615  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for library containing bind_textdomain_codeset" >&5
18616$as_echo_n "checking for library containing bind_textdomain_codeset... " >&6; }
18617if ${ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset+:} false; then :
18618  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18619else
18620  ac_func_search_save_LIBS=$LIBS
18621cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
18622/* end confdefs.h.  */
18623
18624/* Override any GCC internal prototype to avoid an error.
18625   Use char because int might match the return type of a GCC
18626   builtin and then its argument prototype would still apply.  */
18627#ifdef __cplusplus
18628extern "C"
18629#endif
18630char bind_textdomain_codeset ();
18631int
18632main ()
18633{
18634return bind_textdomain_codeset ();
18635  ;
18636  return 0;
18637}
18638_ACEOF
18639for ac_lib in '' intl; do
18640  if test -z "$ac_lib"; then
18641    ac_res="none required"
18642  else
18643    ac_res=-l$ac_lib
18644    LIBS="-l$ac_lib  $ac_func_search_save_LIBS"
18645  fi
18646  if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
18647  ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset=$ac_res
18648fi
18649rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
18650    conftest$ac_exeext
18651  if ${ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset+:} false; then :
18652  break
18653fi
18654done
18655if ${ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset+:} false; then :
18656
18657else
18658  ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset=no
18659fi
18660rm conftest.$ac_ext
18661LIBS=$ac_func_search_save_LIBS
18662fi
18663{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset" >&5
18664$as_echo "$ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset" >&6; }
18665ac_res=$ac_cv_search_bind_textdomain_codeset
18666if test "$ac_res" != no; then :
18667  test "$ac_res" = "none required" || LIBS="$ac_res $LIBS"
18668
18669else
18670  as_fn_error $? "a gettext implementation is required for NLS" "$LINENO" 5
18671fi
18672
18673  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "libintl.h" "ac_cv_header_libintl_h" "$ac_includes_default"
18674if test "x$ac_cv_header_libintl_h" = xyes; then :
18675
18676else
18677  as_fn_error $? "header file <libintl.h> is required for NLS" "$LINENO" 5
18678fi
18679
18680
18681  if test -z "$MSGFMT"; then
18682  for ac_prog in msgfmt
18683do
18684  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
18685set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
18686{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
18687$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
18688if ${ac_cv_path_MSGFMT+:} false; then :
18689  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18690else
18691  case $MSGFMT in
18692  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
18693  ac_cv_path_MSGFMT="$MSGFMT" # Let the user override the test with a path.
18694  ;;
18695  *)
18696  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
18697for as_dir in $PATH
18698do
18699  IFS=$as_save_IFS
18700  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
18701    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
18702  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
18703    ac_cv_path_MSGFMT="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
18704    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
18705    break 2
18706  fi
18707done
18708  done
18709IFS=$as_save_IFS
18710
18711  ;;
18712esac
18713fi
18714MSGFMT=$ac_cv_path_MSGFMT
18715if test -n "$MSGFMT"; then
18716  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $MSGFMT" >&5
18717$as_echo "$MSGFMT" >&6; }
18718else
18719  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
18720$as_echo "no" >&6; }
18721fi
18722
18723
18724  test -n "$MSGFMT" && break
18725done
18726
18727else
18728  # Report the value of MSGFMT in configure's output in all cases.
18729  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for MSGFMT" >&5
18730$as_echo_n "checking for MSGFMT... " >&6; }
18731  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $MSGFMT" >&5
18732$as_echo "$MSGFMT" >&6; }
18733fi
18734
18735    if test -z "$MSGFMT"; then
18736    as_fn_error $? "msgfmt is required for NLS" "$LINENO" 5
18737  fi
18738  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for msgfmt flags" >&5
18739$as_echo_n "checking for msgfmt flags... " >&6; }
18740if ${pgac_cv_msgfmt_flags+:} false; then :
18741  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18742else
18743  if test x"$MSGFMT" != x"" && "$MSGFMT" --version 2>&1 | grep "GNU" >/dev/null; then
18744    pgac_cv_msgfmt_flags=-c
18745fi
18746fi
18747{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_msgfmt_flags" >&5
18748$as_echo "$pgac_cv_msgfmt_flags" >&6; }
18749  MSGFMT_FLAGS=$pgac_cv_msgfmt_flags
18750
18751  if test -z "$MSGMERGE"; then
18752  for ac_prog in msgmerge
18753do
18754  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
18755set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
18756{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
18757$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
18758if ${ac_cv_path_MSGMERGE+:} false; then :
18759  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18760else
18761  case $MSGMERGE in
18762  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
18763  ac_cv_path_MSGMERGE="$MSGMERGE" # Let the user override the test with a path.
18764  ;;
18765  *)
18766  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
18767for as_dir in $PATH
18768do
18769  IFS=$as_save_IFS
18770  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
18771    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
18772  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
18773    ac_cv_path_MSGMERGE="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
18774    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
18775    break 2
18776  fi
18777done
18778  done
18779IFS=$as_save_IFS
18780
18781  ;;
18782esac
18783fi
18784MSGMERGE=$ac_cv_path_MSGMERGE
18785if test -n "$MSGMERGE"; then
18786  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $MSGMERGE" >&5
18787$as_echo "$MSGMERGE" >&6; }
18788else
18789  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
18790$as_echo "no" >&6; }
18791fi
18792
18793
18794  test -n "$MSGMERGE" && break
18795done
18796
18797else
18798  # Report the value of MSGMERGE in configure's output in all cases.
18799  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for MSGMERGE" >&5
18800$as_echo_n "checking for MSGMERGE... " >&6; }
18801  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $MSGMERGE" >&5
18802$as_echo "$MSGMERGE" >&6; }
18803fi
18804
18805  if test -z "$XGETTEXT"; then
18806  for ac_prog in xgettext
18807do
18808  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
18809set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
18810{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
18811$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
18812if ${ac_cv_path_XGETTEXT+:} false; then :
18813  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18814else
18815  case $XGETTEXT in
18816  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
18817  ac_cv_path_XGETTEXT="$XGETTEXT" # Let the user override the test with a path.
18818  ;;
18819  *)
18820  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
18821for as_dir in $PATH
18822do
18823  IFS=$as_save_IFS
18824  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
18825    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
18826  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
18827    ac_cv_path_XGETTEXT="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
18828    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
18829    break 2
18830  fi
18831done
18832  done
18833IFS=$as_save_IFS
18834
18835  ;;
18836esac
18837fi
18838XGETTEXT=$ac_cv_path_XGETTEXT
18839if test -n "$XGETTEXT"; then
18840  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XGETTEXT" >&5
18841$as_echo "$XGETTEXT" >&6; }
18842else
18843  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
18844$as_echo "no" >&6; }
18845fi
18846
18847
18848  test -n "$XGETTEXT" && break
18849done
18850
18851else
18852  # Report the value of XGETTEXT in configure's output in all cases.
18853  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for XGETTEXT" >&5
18854$as_echo_n "checking for XGETTEXT... " >&6; }
18855  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XGETTEXT" >&5
18856$as_echo "$XGETTEXT" >&6; }
18857fi
18858
18859
18860fi
18861
18862# Check for Tcl configuration script tclConfig.sh
18863if test "$with_tcl" = yes; then
18864    if test -z "$TCLSH"; then
18865  for ac_prog in tclsh tcl tclsh8.6 tclsh86 tclsh8.5 tclsh85 tclsh8.4 tclsh84
18866do
18867  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
18868set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
18869{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
18870$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
18871if ${ac_cv_path_TCLSH+:} false; then :
18872  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
18873else
18874  case $TCLSH in
18875  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
18876  ac_cv_path_TCLSH="$TCLSH" # Let the user override the test with a path.
18877  ;;
18878  *)
18879  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
18880for as_dir in $PATH
18881do
18882  IFS=$as_save_IFS
18883  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
18884    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
18885  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
18886    ac_cv_path_TCLSH="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
18887    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
18888    break 2
18889  fi
18890done
18891  done
18892IFS=$as_save_IFS
18893
18894  ;;
18895esac
18896fi
18897TCLSH=$ac_cv_path_TCLSH
18898if test -n "$TCLSH"; then
18899  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $TCLSH" >&5
18900$as_echo "$TCLSH" >&6; }
18901else
18902  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
18903$as_echo "no" >&6; }
18904fi
18905
18906
18907  test -n "$TCLSH" && break
18908done
18909
18910else
18911  # Report the value of TCLSH in configure's output in all cases.
18912  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for TCLSH" >&5
18913$as_echo_n "checking for TCLSH... " >&6; }
18914  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $TCLSH" >&5
18915$as_echo "$TCLSH" >&6; }
18916fi
18917
18918if test x"$TCLSH" = x""; then
18919  as_fn_error $? "Tcl shell not found" "$LINENO" 5
18920fi
18921
18922{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for tclConfig.sh" >&5
18923$as_echo_n "checking for tclConfig.sh... " >&6; }
18924# Let user override test
18925if test -z "$TCL_CONFIG_SH"; then
18926    pgac_test_dirs="$with_tclconfig"
18927
18928    set X $pgac_test_dirs; shift
18929    if test $# -eq 0; then
18930        test -z "$TCLSH" && as_fn_error $? "unable to locate tclConfig.sh because no Tcl shell was found" "$LINENO" 5
18931        pgac_test_dirs=`echo 'puts $auto_path' | $TCLSH`
18932        # On newer macOS, $auto_path frequently doesn't include the place
18933        # where tclConfig.sh actually lives.  Append that to the end, so as not
18934        # to break cases where a non-default Tcl installation is being used.
18935        if test -d "$PG_SYSROOT/System/Library/Frameworks/Tcl.framework" ; then
18936            pgac_test_dirs="$pgac_test_dirs $PG_SYSROOT/System/Library/Frameworks/Tcl.framework"
18937        fi
18938        set X $pgac_test_dirs; shift
18939    fi
18940
18941    for pgac_dir do
18942        if test -r "$pgac_dir/tclConfig.sh"; then
18943            TCL_CONFIG_SH=$pgac_dir/tclConfig.sh
18944            break
18945        fi
18946    done
18947fi
18948
18949if test -z "$TCL_CONFIG_SH"; then
18950    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
18951$as_echo "no" >&6; }
18952    as_fn_error $? "file 'tclConfig.sh' is required for Tcl" "$LINENO" 5
18953else
18954    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $TCL_CONFIG_SH" >&5
18955$as_echo "$TCL_CONFIG_SH" >&6; }
18956fi
18957
18958
18959
18960    . "$TCL_CONFIG_SH"
18961eval TCL_INCLUDE_SPEC=\"$TCL_INCLUDE_SPEC\"
18962eval TCL_LIBS=\"$TCL_LIBS\"
18963eval TCL_LIB_SPEC=\"$TCL_LIB_SPEC\"
18964eval TCL_SHARED_BUILD=\"$TCL_SHARED_BUILD\"
18965
18966    if test "$TCL_SHARED_BUILD" != 1; then
18967      as_fn_error $? "cannot build PL/Tcl because Tcl is not a shared library
18968Use --without-tcl to disable building PL/Tcl." "$LINENO" 5
18969    fi
18970    # now that we have TCL_INCLUDE_SPEC, we can check for <tcl.h>
18971    ac_save_CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS
18972    CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $TCL_INCLUDE_SPEC"
18973    ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "tcl.h" "ac_cv_header_tcl_h" "$ac_includes_default"
18974if test "x$ac_cv_header_tcl_h" = xyes; then :
18975
18976else
18977  as_fn_error $? "header file <tcl.h> is required for Tcl" "$LINENO" 5
18978fi
18979
18980
18981    CPPFLAGS=$ac_save_CPPFLAGS
18982fi
18983
18984# check for <perl.h>
18985if test "$with_perl" = yes; then
18986  ac_save_CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS
18987  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $perl_includespec"
18988  ac_fn_c_check_header_compile "$LINENO" "perl.h" "ac_cv_header_perl_h" "#include <EXTERN.h>
18989"
18990if test "x$ac_cv_header_perl_h" = xyes; then :
18991
18992else
18993  as_fn_error $? "header file <perl.h> is required for Perl" "$LINENO" 5
18994fi
18995
18996
18997  # While we're at it, check that we can link to libperl.
18998  # On most platforms, if perl.h is there then libperl.so will be too, but at
18999  # this writing Debian packages them separately.  There is no known reason to
19000  # waste cycles on separate probes for the Tcl or Python libraries, though.
19001  # On some Red Hat platforms, the link attempt can fail if we don't use
19002  # CFLAGS_SL while building the test program.
19003  ac_save_CFLAGS=$CFLAGS
19004  CFLAGS="$CFLAGS $CFLAGS_SL"
19005  pgac_save_LIBS=$LIBS
19006  LIBS="$perl_embed_ldflags"
19007  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for libperl" >&5
19008$as_echo_n "checking for libperl... " >&6; }
19009  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
19010/* end confdefs.h.  */
19011
19012#include <EXTERN.h>
19013#include <perl.h>
19014
19015int
19016main ()
19017{
19018perl_alloc();
19019  ;
19020  return 0;
19021}
19022_ACEOF
19023if ac_fn_c_try_link "$LINENO"; then :
19024  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
19025$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
19026else
19027  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
19028$as_echo "no" >&6; }
19029     as_fn_error $? "libperl library is required for Perl" "$LINENO" 5
19030fi
19031rm -f core conftest.err conftest.$ac_objext \
19032    conftest$ac_exeext conftest.$ac_ext
19033  LIBS=$pgac_save_LIBS
19034  CFLAGS=$ac_save_CFLAGS
19035  CPPFLAGS=$ac_save_CPPFLAGS
19036fi
19037
19038# check for <Python.h>
19039if test "$with_python" = yes; then
19040  ac_save_CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS
19041  CPPFLAGS="$CPPFLAGS $python_includespec"
19042  ac_fn_c_check_header_mongrel "$LINENO" "Python.h" "ac_cv_header_Python_h" "$ac_includes_default"
19043if test "x$ac_cv_header_Python_h" = xyes; then :
19044
19045else
19046  as_fn_error $? "header file <Python.h> is required for Python" "$LINENO" 5
19047fi
19048
19049
19050  CPPFLAGS=$ac_save_CPPFLAGS
19051fi
19052
19053#
19054# Check for documentation-building tools
19055#
19056if test -z "$XMLLINT"; then
19057  for ac_prog in xmllint
19058do
19059  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
19060set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
19061{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
19062$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
19063if ${ac_cv_path_XMLLINT+:} false; then :
19064  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19065else
19066  case $XMLLINT in
19067  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
19068  ac_cv_path_XMLLINT="$XMLLINT" # Let the user override the test with a path.
