19069  ;;
19070  *)
19071  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
19072for as_dir in $PATH
19073do
19074  IFS=$as_save_IFS
19075  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
19076    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
19077  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
19078    ac_cv_path_XMLLINT="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
19079    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
19080    break 2
19081  fi
19082done
19083  done
19084IFS=$as_save_IFS
19085
19086  ;;
19087esac
19088fi
19089XMLLINT=$ac_cv_path_XMLLINT
19090if test -n "$XMLLINT"; then
19091  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XMLLINT" >&5
19092$as_echo "$XMLLINT" >&6; }
19093else
19094  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
19095$as_echo "no" >&6; }
19096fi
19097
19098
19099  test -n "$XMLLINT" && break
19100done
19101
19102else
19103  # Report the value of XMLLINT in configure's output in all cases.
19104  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for XMLLINT" >&5
19105$as_echo_n "checking for XMLLINT... " >&6; }
19106  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XMLLINT" >&5
19107$as_echo "$XMLLINT" >&6; }
19108fi
19109
19110if test -z "$XSLTPROC"; then
19111  for ac_prog in xsltproc
19112do
19113  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
19114set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
19115{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
19116$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
19117if ${ac_cv_path_XSLTPROC+:} false; then :
19118  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19119else
19120  case $XSLTPROC in
19121  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
19122  ac_cv_path_XSLTPROC="$XSLTPROC" # Let the user override the test with a path.
19123  ;;
19124  *)
19125  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
19126for as_dir in $PATH
19127do
19128  IFS=$as_save_IFS
19129  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
19130    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
19131  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
19132    ac_cv_path_XSLTPROC="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
19133    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
19134    break 2
19135  fi
19136done
19137  done
19138IFS=$as_save_IFS
19139
19140  ;;
19141esac
19142fi
19143XSLTPROC=$ac_cv_path_XSLTPROC
19144if test -n "$XSLTPROC"; then
19145  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XSLTPROC" >&5
19146$as_echo "$XSLTPROC" >&6; }
19147else
19148  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
19149$as_echo "no" >&6; }
19150fi
19151
19152
19153  test -n "$XSLTPROC" && break
19154done
19155
19156else
19157  # Report the value of XSLTPROC in configure's output in all cases.
19158  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for XSLTPROC" >&5
19159$as_echo_n "checking for XSLTPROC... " >&6; }
19160  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $XSLTPROC" >&5
19161$as_echo "$XSLTPROC" >&6; }
19162fi
19163
19164if test -z "$FOP"; then
19165  for ac_prog in fop
19166do
19167  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
19168set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
19169{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
19170$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
19171if ${ac_cv_path_FOP+:} false; then :
19172  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19173else
19174  case $FOP in
19175  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
19176  ac_cv_path_FOP="$FOP" # Let the user override the test with a path.
19177  ;;
19178  *)
19179  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
19180for as_dir in $PATH
19181do
19182  IFS=$as_save_IFS
19183  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
19184    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
19185  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
19186    ac_cv_path_FOP="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
19187    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
19188    break 2
19189  fi
19190done
19191  done
19192IFS=$as_save_IFS
19193
19194  ;;
19195esac
19196fi
19197FOP=$ac_cv_path_FOP
19198if test -n "$FOP"; then
19199  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $FOP" >&5
19200$as_echo "$FOP" >&6; }
19201else
19202  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
19203$as_echo "no" >&6; }
19204fi
19205
19206
19207  test -n "$FOP" && break
19208done
19209
19210else
19211  # Report the value of FOP in configure's output in all cases.
19212  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for FOP" >&5
19213$as_echo_n "checking for FOP... " >&6; }
19214  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $FOP" >&5
19215$as_echo "$FOP" >&6; }
19216fi
19217
19218if test -z "$DBTOEPUB"; then
19219  for ac_prog in dbtoepub
19220do
19221  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
19222set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
19223{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
19224$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
19225if ${ac_cv_path_DBTOEPUB+:} false; then :
19226  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19227else
19228  case $DBTOEPUB in
19229  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
19230  ac_cv_path_DBTOEPUB="$DBTOEPUB" # Let the user override the test with a path.
19231  ;;
19232  *)
19233  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
19234for as_dir in $PATH
19235do
19236  IFS=$as_save_IFS
19237  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
19238    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
19239  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
19240    ac_cv_path_DBTOEPUB="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
19241    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
19242    break 2
19243  fi
19244done
19245  done
19246IFS=$as_save_IFS
19247
19248  ;;
19249esac
19250fi
19251DBTOEPUB=$ac_cv_path_DBTOEPUB
19252if test -n "$DBTOEPUB"; then
19253  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $DBTOEPUB" >&5
19254$as_echo "$DBTOEPUB" >&6; }
19255else
19256  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
19257$as_echo "no" >&6; }
19258fi
19259
19260
19261  test -n "$DBTOEPUB" && break
19262done
19263
19264else
19265  # Report the value of DBTOEPUB in configure's output in all cases.
19266  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for DBTOEPUB" >&5
19267$as_echo_n "checking for DBTOEPUB... " >&6; }
19268  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $DBTOEPUB" >&5
19269$as_echo "$DBTOEPUB" >&6; }
19270fi
19271
19272
19273#
19274# Check for test tools
19275#
19276if test "$enable_tap_tests" = yes; then
19277  # Make sure we know where prove is.
19278  if test -z "$PROVE"; then
19279  for ac_prog in prove
19280do
19281  # Extract the first word of "$ac_prog", so it can be a program name with args.
19282set dummy $ac_prog; ac_word=$2
19283{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for $ac_word" >&5
19284$as_echo_n "checking for $ac_word... " >&6; }
19285if ${ac_cv_path_PROVE+:} false; then :
19286  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19287else
19288  case $PROVE in
19289  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]*)
19290  ac_cv_path_PROVE="$PROVE" # Let the user override the test with a path.
19291  ;;
19292  *)
19293  as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
19294for as_dir in $PATH
19295do
19296  IFS=$as_save_IFS
19297  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
19298    for ac_exec_ext in '' $ac_executable_extensions; do
19299  if as_fn_executable_p "$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"; then
19300    ac_cv_path_PROVE="$as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext"
19301    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: found $as_dir/$ac_word$ac_exec_ext" >&5
19302    break 2
19303  fi
19304done
19305  done
19306IFS=$as_save_IFS
19307
19308  ;;
19309esac
19310fi
19311PROVE=$ac_cv_path_PROVE
19312if test -n "$PROVE"; then
19313  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PROVE" >&5
19314$as_echo "$PROVE" >&6; }
19315else
19316  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: no" >&5
19317$as_echo "no" >&6; }
19318fi
19319
19320
19321  test -n "$PROVE" && break
19322done
19323
19324else
19325  # Report the value of PROVE in configure's output in all cases.
19326  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for PROVE" >&5
19327$as_echo_n "checking for PROVE... " >&6; }
19328  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $PROVE" >&5
19329$as_echo "$PROVE" >&6; }
19330fi
19331
19332  if test -z "$PROVE"; then
19333    as_fn_error $? "prove not found" "$LINENO" 5
19334  fi
19335  # Check for necessary Perl modules.  You might think we should use
19336  # AX_PROG_PERL_MODULES here, but prove might be part of a different Perl
19337  # installation than perl, eg on MSys, so we have to check using prove.
19338  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking for Perl modules required for TAP tests" >&5
19339$as_echo_n "checking for Perl modules required for TAP tests... " >&6; }
19340  __CONFIG_HOST_OS__=$host_os; export __CONFIG_HOST_OS__
19341  modulestderr=`"$PROVE" "$srcdir/config/check_modules.pl" 2>&1 >/dev/null`
19342  if test $? -eq 0; then
19343    # log the module version details, but don't show them interactively
19344    echo "$modulestderr" >&5
19345    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: yes" >&5
19346$as_echo "yes" >&6; }
19347  else
19348    # on failure, though, show the results to the user
19349    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $modulestderr" >&5
19350$as_echo "$modulestderr" >&6; }
19351    as_fn_error $? "Additional Perl modules are required to run TAP tests" "$LINENO" 5
19352  fi
19353fi
19354
19355# If compiler will take -Wl,--as-needed (or various platform-specific
19356# spellings thereof) then add that to LDFLAGS.  This is much easier than
19357# trying to filter LIBS to the minimum for each executable.
19358# On (at least) some Red-Hat-derived systems, this switch breaks linking to
19359# libreadline; therefore we postpone testing it until we know what library
19360# dependencies readline has.  The test code will try to link with $LIBS.
19361if test "$with_readline" = yes; then
19362  link_test_func=readline
19363else
19364  link_test_func=exit
19365fi
19366
19367if test "$PORTNAME" = "darwin"; then
19368  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether $CC supports -Wl,-dead_strip_dylibs, for LDFLAGS" >&5
19369$as_echo_n "checking whether $CC supports -Wl,-dead_strip_dylibs, for LDFLAGS... " >&6; }
19370if ${pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs+:} false; then :
19371  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19372else
19373  pgac_save_LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS
19374LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS -Wl,-dead_strip_dylibs"
19375if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
19376  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs="assuming no"
19377else
19378  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
19379/* end confdefs.h.  */
19380extern void $link_test_func (); void (*fptr) () = $link_test_func;
19381int
19382main ()
19383{
19384
19385  ;
19386  return 0;
19387}
19388_ACEOF
19389if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
19390  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs=yes
19391else
19392  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs=no
19393fi
19394rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
19395  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
19396fi
19397
19398LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS"
19399fi
19400{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs" >&5
19401$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs" >&6; }
19402if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__dead_strip_dylibs" = x"yes"; then
19403  LDFLAGS="${LDFLAGS} -Wl,-dead_strip_dylibs"
19404fi
19405
19406
19407elif test "$PORTNAME" = "openbsd"; then
19408  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether $CC supports -Wl,-Bdynamic, for LDFLAGS" >&5
19409$as_echo_n "checking whether $CC supports -Wl,-Bdynamic, for LDFLAGS... " >&6; }
19410if ${pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic+:} false; then :
19411  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19412else
19413  pgac_save_LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS
19414LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS -Wl,-Bdynamic"
19415if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
19416  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic="assuming no"
19417else
19418  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
19419/* end confdefs.h.  */
19420extern void $link_test_func (); void (*fptr) () = $link_test_func;
19421int
19422main ()
19423{
19424
19425  ;
19426  return 0;
19427}
19428_ACEOF
19429if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
19430  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic=yes
19431else
19432  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic=no
19433fi
19434rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
19435  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
19436fi
19437
19438LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS"
19439fi
19440{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic" >&5
19441$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic" >&6; }
19442if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl__Bdynamic" = x"yes"; then
19443  LDFLAGS="${LDFLAGS} -Wl,-Bdynamic"
19444fi
19445
19446
19447else
19448  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether $CC supports -Wl,--as-needed, for LDFLAGS" >&5
19449$as_echo_n "checking whether $CC supports -Wl,--as-needed, for LDFLAGS... " >&6; }
19450if ${pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed+:} false; then :
19451  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19452else
19453  pgac_save_LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS
19454LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS -Wl,--as-needed"
19455if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
19456  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed="assuming no"
19457else
19458  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
19459/* end confdefs.h.  */
19460extern void $link_test_func (); void (*fptr) () = $link_test_func;
19461int
19462main ()
19463{
19464
19465  ;
19466  return 0;
19467}
19468_ACEOF
19469if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
19470  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed=yes
19471else
19472  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed=no
19473fi
19474rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
19475  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
19476fi
19477
19478LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS"
19479fi
19480{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed" >&5
19481$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed" >&6; }
19482if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS__Wl___as_needed" = x"yes"; then
19483  LDFLAGS="${LDFLAGS} -Wl,--as-needed"
19484fi
19485
19486
19487fi
19488
19489# For linkers that understand --export-dynamic, add that to the LDFLAGS_EX_BE
19490# (backend specific ldflags). One some platforms this will always fail (e.g.,
19491# windows), but on others it depends on the choice of linker (e.g., solaris).
19492# macOS uses -export_dynamic instead.  (On macOS, the option is only
19493# needed when also using -flto, but we add it anyway since it's
19494# harmless.)
19495{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether $CC supports -Wl,--export-dynamic, for LDFLAGS_EX_BE" >&5
19496$as_echo_n "checking whether $CC supports -Wl,--export-dynamic, for LDFLAGS_EX_BE... " >&6; }
19497if ${pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic+:} false; then :
19498  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19499else
19500  pgac_save_LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS
19501LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS -Wl,--export-dynamic"
19502if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
19503  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic="assuming no"
19504else
19505  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
19506/* end confdefs.h.  */
19507extern void $link_test_func (); void (*fptr) () = $link_test_func;
19508int
19509main ()
19510{
19511
19512  ;
19513  return 0;
19514}
19515_ACEOF
19516if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
19517  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic=yes
19518else
19519  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic=no
19520fi
19521rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
19522  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
19523fi
19524
19525LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS"
19526fi
19527{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic" >&5
19528$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic" >&6; }
19529if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl___export_dynamic" = x"yes"; then
19530  LDFLAGS_EX_BE="${LDFLAGS_EX_BE} -Wl,--export-dynamic"
19531fi
19532
19533if test x"$LDFLAGS_EX_BE" = x""; then
19534  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: checking whether $CC supports -Wl,-export_dynamic, for LDFLAGS_EX_BE" >&5
19535$as_echo_n "checking whether $CC supports -Wl,-export_dynamic, for LDFLAGS_EX_BE... " >&6; }
19536if ${pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic+:} false; then :
19537  $as_echo_n "(cached) " >&6
19538else
19539  pgac_save_LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS
19540LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS -Wl,-export_dynamic"
19541if test "$cross_compiling" = yes; then :
19542  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic="assuming no"
19543else
19544  cat confdefs.h - <<_ACEOF >conftest.$ac_ext
19545/* end confdefs.h.  */
19546extern void $link_test_func (); void (*fptr) () = $link_test_func;
19547int
19548main ()
19549{
19550
19551  ;
19552  return 0;
19553}
19554_ACEOF
19555if ac_fn_c_try_run "$LINENO"; then :
19556  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic=yes
19557else
19558  pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic=no
19559fi
19560rm -f core *.core core.conftest.* gmon.out bb.out conftest$ac_exeext \
19561  conftest.$ac_objext conftest.beam conftest.$ac_ext
19562fi
19563
19564LDFLAGS="$pgac_save_LDFLAGS"
19565fi
19566{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: $pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic" >&5
19567$as_echo "$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic" >&6; }
19568if test x"$pgac_cv_prog_cc_LDFLAGS_EX_BE__Wl__export_dynamic" = x"yes"; then
19569  LDFLAGS_EX_BE="${LDFLAGS_EX_BE} -Wl,-export_dynamic"
19570fi
19571
19572fi
19573
19574
19575# Create compiler version string
19576if test x"$GCC" = x"yes" ; then
19577  cc_string=`${CC} --version | sed q`
19578  case $cc_string in [A-Za-z]*) ;; *) cc_string="GCC $cc_string";; esac
19579elif test x"$SUN_STUDIO_CC" = x"yes" ; then
19580  cc_string=`${CC} -V 2>&1 | sed q`
19581else
19582  cc_string=$CC
19583fi
19584
19585
19586cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
19587#define PG_VERSION_STR "PostgreSQL $PG_VERSION on $host, compiled by $cc_string, `expr $ac_cv_sizeof_void_p \* 8`-bit"
19588_ACEOF
19589
19590
19591# Supply a numeric version string for use by 3rd party add-ons
19592# awk -F is a regex on some platforms, and not on others, so make "." a tab
19593PG_VERSION_NUM="`echo $PG_MAJORVERSION $PG_MINORVERSION |
19594$AWK '{printf "%d%04d", $1, $2}'`"
19595
19596cat >>confdefs.h <<_ACEOF
19597#define PG_VERSION_NUM $PG_VERSION_NUM
19598_ACEOF
19599
19600
19601
19602# If we are inserting PG_SYSROOT into CPPFLAGS, do so symbolically not
19603# literally, so that it's possible to override it at build time using
19604# a command like "make ... PG_SYSROOT=path".  This has to be done after
19605# we've finished all configure checks that depend on CPPFLAGS.
19606# The same for LDFLAGS, too.
19607if test x"$PG_SYSROOT" != x; then
19608  CPPFLAGS=`echo "$CPPFLAGS" | sed -e "s| $PG_SYSROOT | \\\$(PG_SYSROOT) |"`
19609  LDFLAGS=`echo "$LDFLAGS" | sed -e "s| $PG_SYSROOT | \\\$(PG_SYSROOT) |"`
19610fi
19611
19612
19613
19614# Begin output steps
19615
19616{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using compiler=$cc_string" >&5
19617$as_echo "$as_me: using compiler=$cc_string" >&6;}
19618{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using CFLAGS=$CFLAGS" >&5
19619$as_echo "$as_me: using CFLAGS=$CFLAGS" >&6;}
19620{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS" >&5
19621$as_echo "$as_me: using CPPFLAGS=$CPPFLAGS" >&6;}
19622{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS" >&5
19623$as_echo "$as_me: using LDFLAGS=$LDFLAGS" >&6;}
19624# Currently only used when LLVM is used
19625if test "$with_llvm" = yes ; then
19626   { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using CXX=$CXX" >&5
19627$as_echo "$as_me: using CXX=$CXX" >&6;}
19628   { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using CXXFLAGS=$CXXFLAGS" >&5
19629$as_echo "$as_me: using CXXFLAGS=$CXXFLAGS" >&6;}
19630   { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using CLANG=$CLANG" >&5
19631$as_echo "$as_me: using CLANG=$CLANG" >&6;}
19632   { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using BITCODE_CFLAGS=$BITCODE_CFLAGS" >&5
19633$as_echo "$as_me: using BITCODE_CFLAGS=$BITCODE_CFLAGS" >&6;}
19634   { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: using BITCODE_CXXFLAGS=$BITCODE_CXXFLAGS" >&5
19635$as_echo "$as_me: using BITCODE_CXXFLAGS=$BITCODE_CXXFLAGS" >&6;}
19636fi
19637
19638# prepare build tree if outside source tree
19639# Note 1: test -ef might not exist, but it's more reliable than `pwd`.
19640# Note 2: /bin/pwd might be better than shell's built-in at getting
19641#         a symlink-free name.
19642if ( test "$srcdir" -ef . ) >/dev/null 2>&1 || test "`cd $srcdir && /bin/pwd`" = "`/bin/pwd`"; then
19643  vpath_build=no
19644else
19645  vpath_build=yes
19646  if test "$no_create" != yes; then
19647    $as_echo_n "preparing build tree... " >&6
19648    pgac_abs_top_srcdir=`cd "$srcdir" && pwd`
19649    $SHELL "$ac_aux_dir/prep_buildtree" "$pgac_abs_top_srcdir" "." \
19650      || as_fn_error $? "failed" "$LINENO" 5
19651    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: result: done" >&5
19652$as_echo "done" >&6; }
19653  fi
19654fi
19655
19656
19657
19658ac_config_files="$ac_config_files GNUmakefile src/Makefile.global"
19659
19660
19661ac_config_links="$ac_config_links src/backend/port/pg_sema.c:${SEMA_IMPLEMENTATION} src/backend/port/pg_shmem.c:${SHMEM_IMPLEMENTATION} src/include/pg_config_os.h:src/include/port/${template}.h src/Makefile.port:src/makefiles/Makefile.${template}"
19662
19663
19664if test "$PORTNAME" = "win32"; then
19665ac_config_commands="$ac_config_commands check_win32_symlinks"
19666
19667fi
19668
19669ac_config_headers="$ac_config_headers src/include/pg_config.h"
19670
19671
19672ac_config_headers="$ac_config_headers src/interfaces/ecpg/include/ecpg_config.h"
19673
19674
19675cat >confcache <<\_ACEOF
19676# This file is a shell script that caches the results of configure
19677# tests run on this system so they can be shared between configure
19678# scripts and configure runs, see configure's option --config-cache.
19679# It is not useful on other systems.  If it contains results you don't
19680# want to keep, you may remove or edit it.
19681#
19682# config.status only pays attention to the cache file if you give it
19683# the --recheck option to rerun configure.
19684#
19685# `ac_cv_env_foo' variables (set or unset) will be overridden when
19686# loading this file, other *unset* `ac_cv_foo' will be assigned the
19687# following values.
19688
19689_ACEOF
19690
19691# The following way of writing the cache mishandles newlines in values,
19692# but we know of no workaround that is simple, portable, and efficient.
19693# So, we kill variables containing newlines.
19694# Ultrix sh set writes to stderr and can't be redirected directly,
19695# and sets the high bit in the cache file unless we assign to the vars.
19696(
19697  for ac_var in `(set) 2>&1 | sed -n 's/^\([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*\)=.*/\1/p'`; do
19698    eval ac_val=\$$ac_var
19699    case $ac_val in #(
19700    *${as_nl}*)
19701      case $ac_var in #(
19702      *_cv_*) { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: cache variable $ac_var contains a newline" >&5
19703$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: cache variable $ac_var contains a newline" >&2;} ;;
19704      esac
19705      case $ac_var in #(
19706      _ | IFS | as_nl) ;; #(
19707      BASH_ARGV | BASH_SOURCE) eval $ac_var= ;; #(
19708      *) { eval $ac_var=; unset $ac_var;} ;;
19709      esac ;;
19710    esac
19711  done
19712
19713  (set) 2>&1 |
19714    case $as_nl`(ac_space=' '; set) 2>&1` in #(
19715    *${as_nl}ac_space=\ *)
19716      # `set' does not quote correctly, so add quotes: double-quote
19717      # substitution turns \\\\ into \\, and sed turns \\ into \.
19718      sed -n \
19719	"s/'/'\\\\''/g;
19720	  s/^\\([_$as_cr_alnum]*_cv_[_$as_cr_alnum]*\\)=\\(.*\\)/\\1='\\2'/p"
19721      ;; #(
19722    *)
19723      # `set' quotes correctly as required by POSIX, so do not add quotes.
19724      sed -n "/^[_$as_cr_alnum]*_cv_[_$as_cr_alnum]*=/p"
19725      ;;
19726    esac |
19727    sort
19728) |
19729  sed '
19730     /^ac_cv_env_/b end
19731     t clear
19732     :clear
19733     s/^\([^=]*\)=\(.*[{}].*\)$/test "${\1+set}" = set || &/
19734     t end
19735     s/^\([^=]*\)=\(.*\)$/\1=${\1=\2}/
19736     :end' >>confcache
19737if diff "$cache_file" confcache >/dev/null 2>&1; then :; else
19738  if test -w "$cache_file"; then
19739    if test "x$cache_file" != "x/dev/null"; then
19740      { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: updating cache $cache_file" >&5
19741$as_echo "$as_me: updating cache $cache_file" >&6;}
19742      if test ! -f "$cache_file" || test -h "$cache_file"; then
19743	cat confcache >"$cache_file"
19744      else
19745        case $cache_file in #(
19746        */* | ?:*)
19747	  mv -f confcache "$cache_file"$$ &&
19748	  mv -f "$cache_file"$$ "$cache_file" ;; #(
19749        *)
19750	  mv -f confcache "$cache_file" ;;
19751	esac
19752      fi
19753    fi
19754  else
19755    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: not updating unwritable cache $cache_file" >&5
19756$as_echo "$as_me: not updating unwritable cache $cache_file" >&6;}
19757  fi
19758fi
19759rm -f confcache
19760
19761test "x$prefix" = xNONE && prefix=$ac_default_prefix
19762# Let make expand exec_prefix.
19763test "x$exec_prefix" = xNONE && exec_prefix='${prefix}'
19764
19765DEFS=-DHAVE_CONFIG_H
19766
19767ac_libobjs=
19768ac_ltlibobjs=
19769U=
19770for ac_i in : $LIBOBJS; do test "x$ac_i" = x: && continue
19771  # 1. Remove the extension, and $U if already installed.
19772  ac_script='s/\$U\././;s/\.o$//;s/\.obj$//'
19773  ac_i=`$as_echo "$ac_i" | sed "$ac_script"`
19774  # 2. Prepend LIBOBJDIR.  When used with automake>=1.10 LIBOBJDIR
19775  #    will be set to the directory where LIBOBJS objects are built.
19776  as_fn_append ac_libobjs " \${LIBOBJDIR}$ac_i\$U.$ac_objext"
19777  as_fn_append ac_ltlibobjs " \${LIBOBJDIR}$ac_i"'$U.lo'
19778done
19779LIBOBJS=$ac_libobjs
19780
19781LTLIBOBJS=$ac_ltlibobjs
19782
19783
19784
19785
19786: "${CONFIG_STATUS=./config.status}"
19787ac_write_fail=0
19788ac_clean_files_save=$ac_clean_files
19789ac_clean_files="$ac_clean_files $CONFIG_STATUS"
19790{ $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: creating $CONFIG_STATUS" >&5
19791$as_echo "$as_me: creating $CONFIG_STATUS" >&6;}
19792as_write_fail=0
19793cat >$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ASEOF || as_write_fail=1
19794#! $SHELL
19795# Generated by $as_me.
19796# Run this file to recreate the current configuration.
19797# Compiler output produced by configure, useful for debugging
19798# configure, is in config.log if it exists.
19799
19800debug=false
19801ac_cs_recheck=false
19802ac_cs_silent=false
19803
19804SHELL=\${CONFIG_SHELL-$SHELL}
19805export SHELL
19806_ASEOF
19807cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ASEOF || as_write_fail=1
19808## -------------------- ##
19809## M4sh Initialization. ##
19810## -------------------- ##
19811
19812# Be more Bourne compatible
19813DUALCASE=1; export DUALCASE # for MKS sh
19814if test -n "${ZSH_VERSION+set}" && (emulate sh) >/dev/null 2>&1; then :
19815  emulate sh
19816  NULLCMD=:
19817  # Pre-4.2 versions of Zsh do word splitting on ${1+"$@"}, which
19818  # is contrary to our usage.  Disable this feature.
19819  alias -g '${1+"$@"}'='"$@"'
19820  setopt NO_GLOB_SUBST
19821else
19822  case `(set -o) 2>/dev/null` in #(
19823  *posix*) :
19824    set -o posix ;; #(
19825  *) :
19826     ;;
19827esac
19828fi
19829
19830
19831as_nl='
19832'
19833export as_nl
19834# Printing a long string crashes Solaris 7 /usr/bin/printf.
19835as_echo='\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\'
19836as_echo=$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo
19837as_echo=$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo$as_echo
19838# Prefer a ksh shell builtin over an external printf program on Solaris,
19839# but without wasting forks for bash or zsh.
19840if test -z "$BASH_VERSION$ZSH_VERSION" \
19841    && (test "X`print -r -- $as_echo`" = "X$as_echo") 2>/dev/null; then
19842  as_echo='print -r --'
19843  as_echo_n='print -rn --'
19844elif (test "X`printf %s $as_echo`" = "X$as_echo") 2>/dev/null; then
19845  as_echo='printf %s\n'
19846  as_echo_n='printf %s'
19847else
19848  if test "X`(/usr/ucb/echo -n -n $as_echo) 2>/dev/null`" = "X-n $as_echo"; then
19849    as_echo_body='eval /usr/ucb/echo -n "$1$as_nl"'
19850    as_echo_n='/usr/ucb/echo -n'
19851  else
19852    as_echo_body='eval expr "X$1" : "X\\(.*\\)"'
19853    as_echo_n_body='eval
19854      arg=$1;
19855      case $arg in #(
19856      *"$as_nl"*)
19857	expr "X$arg" : "X\\(.*\\)$as_nl";
19858	arg=`expr "X$arg" : ".*$as_nl\\(.*\\)"`;;
19859      esac;
19860      expr "X$arg" : "X\\(.*\\)" | tr -d "$as_nl"
19861    '
19862    export as_echo_n_body
19863    as_echo_n='sh -c $as_echo_n_body as_echo'
19864  fi
19865  export as_echo_body
19866  as_echo='sh -c $as_echo_body as_echo'
19867fi
19868
19869# The user is always right.
19870if test "${PATH_SEPARATOR+set}" != set; then
19871  PATH_SEPARATOR=:
19872  (PATH='/bin;/bin'; FPATH=$PATH; sh -c :) >/dev/null 2>&1 && {
19873    (PATH='/bin:/bin'; FPATH=$PATH; sh -c :) >/dev/null 2>&1 ||
19874      PATH_SEPARATOR=';'
19875  }
19876fi
19877
19878
19879# IFS
19880# We need space, tab and new line, in precisely that order.  Quoting is
19881# there to prevent editors from complaining about space-tab.
19882# (If _AS_PATH_WALK were called with IFS unset, it would disable word
19883# splitting by setting IFS to empty value.)
19884IFS=" ""	$as_nl"
19885
19886# Find who we are.  Look in the path if we contain no directory separator.
19887as_myself=
19888case $0 in #((
19889  *[\\/]* ) as_myself=$0 ;;
19890  *) as_save_IFS=$IFS; IFS=$PATH_SEPARATOR
19891for as_dir in $PATH
19892do
19893  IFS=$as_save_IFS
19894  test -z "$as_dir" && as_dir=.
19895    test -r "$as_dir/$0" && as_myself=$as_dir/$0 && break
19896  done
19897IFS=$as_save_IFS
19898
19899     ;;
19900esac
19901# We did not find ourselves, most probably we were run as `sh COMMAND'
19902# in which case we are not to be found in the path.
19903if test "x$as_myself" = x; then
19904  as_myself=$0
19905fi
19906if test ! -f "$as_myself"; then
19907  $as_echo "$as_myself: error: cannot find myself; rerun with an absolute file name" >&2
19908  exit 1
19909fi
19910
19911# Unset variables that we do not need and which cause bugs (e.g. in
19912# pre-3.0 UWIN ksh).  But do not cause bugs in bash 2.01; the "|| exit 1"
19913# suppresses any "Segmentation fault" message there.  '((' could
19914# trigger a bug in pdksh 5.2.14.
19915for as_var in BASH_ENV ENV MAIL MAILPATH
19916do eval test x\${$as_var+set} = xset \
19917  && ( (unset $as_var) || exit 1) >/dev/null 2>&1 && unset $as_var || :
19918done
19919PS1='$ '
19920PS2='> '
19921PS4='+ '
19922
19923# NLS nuisances.
19924LC_ALL=C
19925export LC_ALL
19926LANGUAGE=C
19927export LANGUAGE
19928
19929# CDPATH.
19930(unset CDPATH) >/dev/null 2>&1 && unset CDPATH
19931
19932
19933# as_fn_error STATUS ERROR [LINENO LOG_FD]
19934# ----------------------------------------
19935# Output "`basename $0`: error: ERROR" to stderr. If LINENO and LOG_FD are
19936# provided, also output the error to LOG_FD, referencing LINENO. Then exit the
19937# script with STATUS, using 1 if that was 0.
19938as_fn_error ()
19939{
19940  as_status=$1; test $as_status -eq 0 && as_status=1
19941  if test "$4"; then
19942    as_lineno=${as_lineno-"$3"} as_lineno_stack=as_lineno_stack=$as_lineno_stack
19943    $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: error: $2" >&$4
19944  fi
19945  $as_echo "$as_me: error: $2" >&2
19946  as_fn_exit $as_status
19947} # as_fn_error
19948
19949
19950# as_fn_set_status STATUS
19951# -----------------------
19952# Set $? to STATUS, without forking.
19953as_fn_set_status ()
19954{
19955  return $1
19956} # as_fn_set_status
19957
19958# as_fn_exit STATUS
19959# -----------------
19960# Exit the shell with STATUS, even in a "trap 0" or "set -e" context.
19961as_fn_exit ()
19962{
19963  set +e
19964  as_fn_set_status $1
19965  exit $1
19966} # as_fn_exit
19967
19968# as_fn_unset VAR
19969# ---------------
19970# Portably unset VAR.
19971as_fn_unset ()
19972{
19973  { eval $1=; unset $1;}
19974}
19975as_unset=as_fn_unset
19976# as_fn_append VAR VALUE
19977# ----------------------
19978# Append the text in VALUE to the end of the definition contained in VAR. Take
19979# advantage of any shell optimizations that allow amortized linear growth over
19980# repeated appends, instead of the typical quadratic growth present in naive
19981# implementations.
19982if (eval "as_var=1; as_var+=2; test x\$as_var = x12") 2>/dev/null; then :
19983  eval 'as_fn_append ()
19984  {
19985    eval $1+=\$2
19986  }'
19987else
19988  as_fn_append ()
19989  {
19990    eval $1=\$$1\$2
19991  }
19992fi # as_fn_append
19993
19994# as_fn_arith ARG...
19995# ------------------
19996# Perform arithmetic evaluation on the ARGs, and store the result in the
19997# global $as_val. Take advantage of shells that can avoid forks. The arguments
19998# must be portable across $(()) and expr.
19999if (eval "test \$(( 1 + 1 )) = 2") 2>/dev/null; then :
20000  eval 'as_fn_arith ()
20001  {
20002    as_val=$(( $* ))
20003  }'
20004else
20005  as_fn_arith ()
20006  {
20007    as_val=`expr "$@" || test $? -eq 1`
20008  }
20009fi # as_fn_arith
20010
20011
20012if expr a : '\(a\)' >/dev/null 2>&1 &&
20013   test "X`expr 00001 : '.*\(...\)'`" = X001; then
20014  as_expr=expr
20015else
20016  as_expr=false
20017fi
20018
20019if (basename -- /) >/dev/null 2>&1 && test "X`basename -- / 2>&1`" = "X/"; then
20020  as_basename=basename
20021else
20022  as_basename=false
20023fi
20024
20025if (as_dir=`dirname -- /` && test "X$as_dir" = X/) >/dev/null 2>&1; then
20026  as_dirname=dirname
20027else
20028  as_dirname=false
20029fi
20030
20031as_me=`$as_basename -- "$0" ||
20032$as_expr X/"$0" : '.*/\([^/][^/]*\)/*$' \| \
20033	 X"$0" : 'X\(//\)$' \| \
20034	 X"$0" : 'X\(/\)' \| . 2>/dev/null ||
20035$as_echo X/"$0" |
20036    sed '/^.*\/\([^/][^/]*\)\/*$/{
20037	    s//\1/
20038	    q
20039	  }
20040	  /^X\/\(\/\/\)$/{
20041	    s//\1/
20042	    q
20043	  }
20044	  /^X\/\(\/\).*/{
20045	    s//\1/
20046	    q
20047	  }
20048	  s/.*/./; q'`
20049
20050# Avoid depending upon Character Ranges.
20051as_cr_letters='abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
20052as_cr_LETTERS='ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ'
20053as_cr_Letters=$as_cr_letters$as_cr_LETTERS
20054as_cr_digits='0123456789'
20055as_cr_alnum=$as_cr_Letters$as_cr_digits
20056
20057ECHO_C= ECHO_N= ECHO_T=
20058case `echo -n x` in #(((((
20059-n*)
20060  case `echo 'xy\c'` in
20061  *c*) ECHO_T='	';;	# ECHO_T is single tab character.
20062  xy)  ECHO_C='\c';;
20063  *)   echo `echo ksh88 bug on AIX 6.1` > /dev/null
20064       ECHO_T='	';;
20065  esac;;
20066*)
20067  ECHO_N='-n';;
20068esac
20069
20070rm -f conf$$ conf$$.exe conf$$.file
20071if test -d conf$$.dir; then
20072  rm -f conf$$.dir/conf$$.file
20073else
20074  rm -f conf$$.dir
20075  mkdir conf$$.dir 2>/dev/null
20076fi
20077if (echo >conf$$.file) 2>/dev/null; then
20078  if ln -s conf$$.file conf$$ 2>/dev/null; then
20079    as_ln_s='ln -s'
20080    # ... but there are two gotchas:
20081    # 1) On MSYS, both `ln -s file dir' and `ln file dir' fail.
20082    # 2) DJGPP < 2.04 has no symlinks; `ln -s' creates a wrapper executable.
20083    # In both cases, we have to default to `cp -pR'.
20084    ln -s conf$$.file conf$$.dir 2>/dev/null && test ! -f conf$$.exe ||
20085      as_ln_s='cp -pR'
20086  elif ln conf$$.file conf$$ 2>/dev/null; then
20087    as_ln_s=ln
20088  else
20089    as_ln_s='cp -pR'
20090  fi
20091else
20092  as_ln_s='cp -pR'
20093fi
20094rm -f conf$$ conf$$.exe conf$$.dir/conf$$.file conf$$.file
20095rmdir conf$$.dir 2>/dev/null
20096
20097
20098# as_fn_mkdir_p
20099# -------------
20100# Create "$as_dir" as a directory, including parents if necessary.
20101as_fn_mkdir_p ()
20102{
20103
20104  case $as_dir in #(
20105  -*) as_dir=./$as_dir;;
20106  esac
20107  test -d "$as_dir" || eval $as_mkdir_p || {
20108    as_dirs=
20109    while :; do
20110      case $as_dir in #(
20111      *\'*) as_qdir=`$as_echo "$as_dir" | sed "s/'/'\\\\\\\\''/g"`;; #'(
20112      *) as_qdir=$as_dir;;
20113      esac
20114      as_dirs="'$as_qdir' $as_dirs"
20115      as_dir=`$as_dirname -- "$as_dir" ||
20116$as_expr X"$as_dir" : 'X\(.*[^/]\)//*[^/][^/]*/*$' \| \
20117	 X"$as_dir" : 'X\(//\)[^/]' \| \
20118	 X"$as_dir" : 'X\(//\)$' \| \
20119	 X"$as_dir" : 'X\(/\)' \| . 2>/dev/null ||
20120$as_echo X"$as_dir" |
20121    sed '/^X\(.*[^/]\)\/\/*[^/][^/]*\/*$/{
20122	    s//\1/
20123	    q
20124	  }
20125	  /^X\(\/\/\)[^/].*/{
20126	    s//\1/
20127	    q
20128	  }
20129	  /^X\(\/\/\)$/{
20130	    s//\1/
20131	    q
20132	  }
20133	  /^X\(\/\).*/{
20134	    s//\1/
20135	    q
20136	  }
20137	  s/.*/./; q'`
20138      test -d "$as_dir" && break
20139    done
20140    test -z "$as_dirs" || eval "mkdir $as_dirs"
20141  } || test -d "$as_dir" || as_fn_error $? "cannot create directory $as_dir"
20142
20143
20144} # as_fn_mkdir_p
20145if mkdir -p . 2>/dev/null; then
20146  as_mkdir_p='mkdir -p "$as_dir"'
20147else
20148  test -d ./-p && rmdir ./-p
20149  as_mkdir_p=false
20150fi
20151
20152
20153# as_fn_executable_p FILE
20154# -----------------------
20155# Test if FILE is an executable regular file.
20156as_fn_executable_p ()
20157{
20158  test -f "$1" && test -x "$1"
20159} # as_fn_executable_p
20160as_test_x='test -x'
20161as_executable_p=as_fn_executable_p
20162
20163# Sed expression to map a string onto a valid CPP name.
20164as_tr_cpp="eval sed 'y%*$as_cr_letters%P$as_cr_LETTERS%;s%[^_$as_cr_alnum]%_%g'"
20165
20166# Sed expression to map a string onto a valid variable name.
20167as_tr_sh="eval sed 'y%*+%pp%;s%[^_$as_cr_alnum]%_%g'"
20168
20169
20170exec 6>&1
20171## ----------------------------------- ##
20172## Main body of $CONFIG_STATUS script. ##
20173## ----------------------------------- ##
20174_ASEOF
20175test $as_write_fail = 0 && chmod +x $CONFIG_STATUS || ac_write_fail=1
20176
20177cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20178# Save the log message, to keep $0 and so on meaningful, and to
20179# report actual input values of CONFIG_FILES etc. instead of their
20180# values after options handling.
20181ac_log="
20182This file was extended by PostgreSQL $as_me 18.4, which was
20183generated by GNU Autoconf 2.69.  Invocation command line was
20184
20185  CONFIG_FILES    = $CONFIG_FILES
20186  CONFIG_HEADERS  = $CONFIG_HEADERS
20187  CONFIG_LINKS    = $CONFIG_LINKS
20188  CONFIG_COMMANDS = $CONFIG_COMMANDS
20189  $ $0 $@
20190
20191on `(hostname || uname -n) 2>/dev/null | sed 1q`
20192"
20193
20194_ACEOF
20195
20196case $ac_config_files in *"
20197"*) set x $ac_config_files; shift; ac_config_files=$*;;
20198esac
20199
20200case $ac_config_headers in *"
20201"*) set x $ac_config_headers; shift; ac_config_headers=$*;;
20202esac
20203
20204
20205cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20206# Files that config.status was made for.
20207config_files="$ac_config_files"
20208config_headers="$ac_config_headers"
20209config_links="$ac_config_links"
20210config_commands="$ac_config_commands"
20211
20212_ACEOF
20213
20214cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20215ac_cs_usage="\
20216\`$as_me' instantiates files and other configuration actions
20217from templates according to the current configuration.  Unless the files
20218and actions are specified as TAGs, all are instantiated by default.
20219
20220Usage: $0 [OPTION]... [TAG]...
20221
20222  -h, --help       print this help, then exit
20223  -V, --version    print version number and configuration settings, then exit
20224      --config     print configuration, then exit
20225  -q, --quiet, --silent
20226                   do not print progress messages
20227  -d, --debug      don't remove temporary files
20228      --recheck    update $as_me by reconfiguring in the same conditions
20229      --file=FILE[:TEMPLATE]
20230                   instantiate the configuration file FILE
20231      --header=FILE[:TEMPLATE]
20232                   instantiate the configuration header FILE
20233
20234Configuration files:
20235$config_files
20236
20237Configuration headers:
20238$config_headers
20239
20240Configuration links:
20241$config_links
20242
20243Configuration commands:
20244$config_commands
20245
20246Report bugs to <pgsql-bugs@lists.postgresql.org>.
20247PostgreSQL home page: <https://www.postgresql.org/>."
20248
20249_ACEOF
20250cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20251ac_cs_config="`$as_echo "$ac_configure_args" | sed 's/^ //; s/[\\""\`\$]/\\\\&/g'`"
20252ac_cs_version="\\
20253PostgreSQL config.status 18.4
20254configured by $0, generated by GNU Autoconf 2.69,
20255  with options \\"\$ac_cs_config\\"
20256
20257Copyright (C) 2012 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
20258This config.status script is free software; the Free Software Foundation
20259gives unlimited permission to copy, distribute and modify it."
20260
20261ac_pwd='$ac_pwd'
20262srcdir='$srcdir'
20263INSTALL='$INSTALL'
20264MKDIR_P='$MKDIR_P'
20265AWK='$AWK'
20266test -n "\$AWK" || AWK=awk
20267_ACEOF
20268
20269cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20270# The default lists apply if the user does not specify any file.
20271ac_need_defaults=:
20272while test $# != 0
20273do
20274  case $1 in
20275  --*=?*)
20276    ac_option=`expr "X$1" : 'X\([^=]*\)='`
20277    ac_optarg=`expr "X$1" : 'X[^=]*=\(.*\)'`
20278    ac_shift=:
20279    ;;
20280  --*=)
20281    ac_option=`expr "X$1" : 'X\([^=]*\)='`
20282    ac_optarg=
20283    ac_shift=:
20284    ;;
20285  *)
20286    ac_option=$1
20287    ac_optarg=$2
20288    ac_shift=shift
20289    ;;
20290  esac
20291
20292  case $ac_option in
20293  # Handling of the options.
20294  -recheck | --recheck | --rechec | --reche | --rech | --rec | --re | --r)
20295    ac_cs_recheck=: ;;
20296  --version | --versio | --versi | --vers | --ver | --ve | --v | -V )
20297    $as_echo "$ac_cs_version"; exit ;;
20298  --config | --confi | --conf | --con | --co | --c )
20299    $as_echo "$ac_cs_config"; exit ;;
20300  --debug | --debu | --deb | --de | --d | -d )
20301    debug=: ;;
20302  --file | --fil | --fi | --f )
20303    $ac_shift
20304    case $ac_optarg in
20305    *\'*) ac_optarg=`$as_echo "$ac_optarg" | sed "s/'/'\\\\\\\\''/g"` ;;
20306    '') as_fn_error $? "missing file argument" ;;
20307    esac
20308    as_fn_append CONFIG_FILES " '$ac_optarg'"
20309    ac_need_defaults=false;;
20310  --header | --heade | --head | --hea )
20311    $ac_shift
20312    case $ac_optarg in
20313    *\'*) ac_optarg=`$as_echo "$ac_optarg" | sed "s/'/'\\\\\\\\''/g"` ;;
20314    esac
20315    as_fn_append CONFIG_HEADERS " '$ac_optarg'"
20316    ac_need_defaults=false;;
20317  --he | --h)
20318    # Conflict between --help and --header
20319    as_fn_error $? "ambiguous option: \`$1'
20320Try \`$0 --help' for more information.";;
20321  --help | --hel | -h )
20322    $as_echo "$ac_cs_usage"; exit ;;
20323  -q | -quiet | --quiet | --quie | --qui | --qu | --q \
20324  | -silent | --silent | --silen | --sile | --sil | --si | --s)
20325    ac_cs_silent=: ;;
20326
20327  # This is an error.
20328  -*) as_fn_error $? "unrecognized option: \`$1'
20329Try \`$0 --help' for more information." ;;
20330
20331  *) as_fn_append ac_config_targets " $1"
20332     ac_need_defaults=false ;;
20333
20334  esac
20335  shift
20336done
20337
20338ac_configure_extra_args=
20339
20340if $ac_cs_silent; then
20341  exec 6>/dev/null
20342  ac_configure_extra_args="$ac_configure_extra_args --silent"
20343fi
20344
20345_ACEOF
20346cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20347if \$ac_cs_recheck; then
20348  set X $SHELL '$0' $ac_configure_args \$ac_configure_extra_args --no-create --no-recursion
20349  shift
20350  \$as_echo "running CONFIG_SHELL=$SHELL \$*" >&6
20351  CONFIG_SHELL='$SHELL'
20352  export CONFIG_SHELL
20353  exec "\$@"
20354fi
20355
20356_ACEOF
20357cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20358exec 5>>config.log
20359{
20360  echo
20361  sed 'h;s/./-/g;s/^.../## /;s/...$/ ##/;p;x;p;x' <<_ASBOX
20362## Running $as_me. ##
20363_ASBOX
20364  $as_echo "$ac_log"
20365} >&5
20366
20367_ACEOF
20368cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20369_ACEOF
20370
20371cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20372
20373# Handling of arguments.
20374for ac_config_target in $ac_config_targets
20375do
20376  case $ac_config_target in
20377    "src/backend/port/tas.s") CONFIG_LINKS="$CONFIG_LINKS src/backend/port/tas.s:src/backend/port/tas/${tas_file}" ;;
20378    "GNUmakefile") CONFIG_FILES="$CONFIG_FILES GNUmakefile" ;;
20379    "src/Makefile.global") CONFIG_FILES="$CONFIG_FILES src/Makefile.global" ;;
20380    "src/backend/port/pg_sema.c") CONFIG_LINKS="$CONFIG_LINKS src/backend/port/pg_sema.c:${SEMA_IMPLEMENTATION}" ;;
20381    "src/backend/port/pg_shmem.c") CONFIG_LINKS="$CONFIG_LINKS src/backend/port/pg_shmem.c:${SHMEM_IMPLEMENTATION}" ;;
20382    "src/include/pg_config_os.h") CONFIG_LINKS="$CONFIG_LINKS src/include/pg_config_os.h:src/include/port/${template}.h" ;;
20383    "src/Makefile.port") CONFIG_LINKS="$CONFIG_LINKS src/Makefile.port:src/makefiles/Makefile.${template}" ;;
20384    "check_win32_symlinks") CONFIG_COMMANDS="$CONFIG_COMMANDS check_win32_symlinks" ;;
20385    "src/include/pg_config.h") CONFIG_HEADERS="$CONFIG_HEADERS src/include/pg_config.h" ;;
20386    "src/interfaces/ecpg/include/ecpg_config.h") CONFIG_HEADERS="$CONFIG_HEADERS src/interfaces/ecpg/include/ecpg_config.h" ;;
20387
20388  *) as_fn_error $? "invalid argument: \`$ac_config_target'" "$LINENO" 5;;
20389  esac
20390done
20391
20392
20393# If the user did not use the arguments to specify the items to instantiate,
20394# then the envvar interface is used.  Set only those that are not.
20395# We use the long form for the default assignment because of an extremely
20396# bizarre bug on SunOS 4.1.3.
20397if $ac_need_defaults; then
20398  test "${CONFIG_FILES+set}" = set || CONFIG_FILES=$config_files
20399  test "${CONFIG_HEADERS+set}" = set || CONFIG_HEADERS=$config_headers
20400  test "${CONFIG_LINKS+set}" = set || CONFIG_LINKS=$config_links
20401  test "${CONFIG_COMMANDS+set}" = set || CONFIG_COMMANDS=$config_commands
20402fi
20403
20404# Have a temporary directory for convenience.  Make it in the build tree
20405# simply because there is no reason against having it here, and in addition,
20406# creating and moving files from /tmp can sometimes cause problems.
20407# Hook for its removal unless debugging.
20408# Note that there is a small window in which the directory will not be cleaned:
20409# after its creation but before its name has been assigned to `$tmp'.
20410$debug ||
20411{
20412  tmp= ac_tmp=
20413  trap 'exit_status=$?
20414  : "${ac_tmp:=$tmp}"
20415  { test ! -d "$ac_tmp" || rm -fr "$ac_tmp"; } && exit $exit_status
20416' 0
20417  trap 'as_fn_exit 1' 1 2 13 15
20418}
20419# Create a (secure) tmp directory for tmp files.
20420
20421{
20422  tmp=`(umask 077 && mktemp -d "./confXXXXXX") 2>/dev/null` &&
20423  test -d "$tmp"
20424}  ||
20425{
20426  tmp=./conf$$-$RANDOM
20427  (umask 077 && mkdir "$tmp")
20428} || as_fn_error $? "cannot create a temporary directory in ." "$LINENO" 5
20429ac_tmp=$tmp
20430
20431# Set up the scripts for CONFIG_FILES section.
20432# No need to generate them if there are no CONFIG_FILES.
20433# This happens for instance with `./config.status config.h'.
20434if test -n "$CONFIG_FILES"; then
20435
20436
20437ac_cr=`echo X | tr X '\015'`
20438# On cygwin, bash can eat \r inside `` if the user requested igncr.
20439# But we know of no other shell where ac_cr would be empty at this
20440# point, so we can use a bashism as a fallback.
20441if test "x$ac_cr" = x; then
20442  eval ac_cr=\$\'\\r\'
20443fi
20444ac_cs_awk_cr=`$AWK 'BEGIN { print "a\rb" }' </dev/null 2>/dev/null`
20445if test "$ac_cs_awk_cr" = "a${ac_cr}b"; then
20446  ac_cs_awk_cr='\\r'
20447else
20448  ac_cs_awk_cr=$ac_cr
20449fi
20450
20451echo 'BEGIN {' >"$ac_tmp/subs1.awk" &&
20452_ACEOF
20453
20454
20455{
20456  echo "cat >conf$$subs.awk <<_ACEOF" &&
20457  echo "$ac_subst_vars" | sed 's/.*/&!$&$ac_delim/' &&
20458  echo "_ACEOF"
20459} >conf$$subs.sh ||
20460  as_fn_error $? "could not make $CONFIG_STATUS" "$LINENO" 5
20461ac_delim_num=`echo "$ac_subst_vars" | grep -c '^'`
20462ac_delim='%!_!# '
20463for ac_last_try in false false false false false :; do
20464  . ./conf$$subs.sh ||
20465    as_fn_error $? "could not make $CONFIG_STATUS" "$LINENO" 5
20466
20467  ac_delim_n=`sed -n "s/.*$ac_delim\$/X/p" conf$$subs.awk | grep -c X`
20468  if test $ac_delim_n = $ac_delim_num; then
20469    break
20470  elif $ac_last_try; then
20471    as_fn_error $? "could not make $CONFIG_STATUS" "$LINENO" 5
20472  else
20473    ac_delim="$ac_delim!$ac_delim _$ac_delim!! "
20474  fi
20475done
20476rm -f conf$$subs.sh
20477
20478cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20479cat >>"\$ac_tmp/subs1.awk" <<\\_ACAWK &&
20480_ACEOF
20481sed -n '
20482h
20483s/^/S["/; s/!.*/"]=/
20484p
20485g
20486s/^[^!]*!//
20487:repl
20488t repl
20489s/'"$ac_delim"'$//
20490t delim
20491:nl
20492h
20493s/\(.\{148\}\)..*/\1/
20494t more1
20495s/["\\]/\\&/g; s/^/"/; s/$/\\n"\\/
20496p
20497n
20498b repl
20499:more1
20500s/["\\]/\\&/g; s/^/"/; s/$/"\\/
20501p
20502g
20503s/.\{148\}//
20504t nl
20505:delim
20506h
20507s/\(.\{148\}\)..*/\1/
20508t more2
20509s/["\\]/\\&/g; s/^/"/; s/$/"/
20510p
20511b
20512:more2
20513s/["\\]/\\&/g; s/^/"/; s/$/"\\/
20514p
20515g
20516s/.\{148\}//
20517t delim
20518' <conf$$subs.awk | sed '
20519/^[^""]/{
20520  N
20521  s/\n//
20522}
20523' >>$CONFIG_STATUS || ac_write_fail=1
20524rm -f conf$$subs.awk
20525cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20526_ACAWK
20527cat >>"\$ac_tmp/subs1.awk" <<_ACAWK &&
20528  for (key in S) S_is_set[key] = 1
20529  FS = ""
20530
20531}
20532{
20533  line = $ 0
20534  nfields = split(line, field, "@")
20535  substed = 0
20536  len = length(field[1])
20537  for (i = 2; i < nfields; i++) {
20538    key = field[i]
20539    keylen = length(key)
20540    if (S_is_set[key]) {
20541      value = S[key]
20542      line = substr(line, 1, len) "" value "" substr(line, len + keylen + 3)
20543      len += length(value) + length(field[++i])
20544      substed = 1
20545    } else
20546      len += 1 + keylen
20547  }
20548
20549  print line
20550}
20551
20552_ACAWK
20553_ACEOF
20554cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20555if sed "s/$ac_cr//" < /dev/null > /dev/null 2>&1; then
20556  sed "s/$ac_cr\$//; s/$ac_cr/$ac_cs_awk_cr/g"
20557else
20558  cat
20559fi < "$ac_tmp/subs1.awk" > "$ac_tmp/subs.awk" \
20560  || as_fn_error $? "could not setup config files machinery" "$LINENO" 5
20561_ACEOF
20562
20563# VPATH may cause trouble with some makes, so we remove sole $(srcdir),
20564# ${srcdir} and @srcdir@ entries from VPATH if srcdir is ".", strip leading and
20565# trailing colons and then remove the whole line if VPATH becomes empty
20566# (actually we leave an empty line to preserve line numbers).
20567if test "x$srcdir" = x.; then
20568  ac_vpsub='/^[	 ]*VPATH[	 ]*=[	 ]*/{
20569h
20570s///
20571s/^/:/
20572s/[	 ]*$/:/
20573s/:\$(srcdir):/:/g
20574s/:\${srcdir}:/:/g
20575s/:@srcdir@:/:/g
20576s/^:*//
20577s/:*$//
20578x
20579s/\(=[	 ]*\).*/\1/
20580G
20581s/\n//
20582s/^[^=]*=[	 ]*$//
20583}'
20584fi
20585
20586cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20587fi # test -n "$CONFIG_FILES"
20588
20589# Set up the scripts for CONFIG_HEADERS section.
20590# No need to generate them if there are no CONFIG_HEADERS.
20591# This happens for instance with `./config.status Makefile'.
20592if test -n "$CONFIG_HEADERS"; then
20593cat >"$ac_tmp/defines.awk" <<\_ACAWK ||
20594BEGIN {
20595_ACEOF
20596
20597# Transform confdefs.h into an awk script `defines.awk', embedded as
20598# here-document in config.status, that substitutes the proper values into
20599# config.h.in to produce config.h.
20600
20601# Create a delimiter string that does not exist in confdefs.h, to ease
20602# handling of long lines.
20603ac_delim='%!_!# '
20604for ac_last_try in false false :; do
20605  ac_tt=`sed -n "/$ac_delim/p" confdefs.h`
20606  if test -z "$ac_tt"; then
20607    break
20608  elif $ac_last_try; then
20609    as_fn_error $? "could not make $CONFIG_HEADERS" "$LINENO" 5
20610  else
20611    ac_delim="$ac_delim!$ac_delim _$ac_delim!! "
20612  fi
20613done
20614
20615# For the awk script, D is an array of macro values keyed by name,
20616# likewise P contains macro parameters if any.  Preserve backslash
20617# newline sequences.
20618
20619ac_word_re=[_$as_cr_Letters][_$as_cr_alnum]*
20620sed -n '
20621s/.\{148\}/&'"$ac_delim"'/g
20622t rset
20623:rset
20624s/^[	 ]*#[	 ]*define[	 ][	 ]*/ /
20625t def
20626d
20627:def
20628s/\\$//
20629t bsnl
20630s/["\\]/\\&/g
20631s/^ \('"$ac_word_re"'\)\(([^()]*)\)[	 ]*\(.*\)/P["\1"]="\2"\
20632D["\1"]=" \3"/p
20633s/^ \('"$ac_word_re"'\)[	 ]*\(.*\)/D["\1"]=" \2"/p
20634d
20635:bsnl
20636s/["\\]/\\&/g
20637s/^ \('"$ac_word_re"'\)\(([^()]*)\)[	 ]*\(.*\)/P["\1"]="\2"\
20638D["\1"]=" \3\\\\\\n"\\/p
20639t cont
20640s/^ \('"$ac_word_re"'\)[	 ]*\(.*\)/D["\1"]=" \2\\\\\\n"\\/p
20641t cont
20642d
20643:cont
20644n
20645s/.\{148\}/&'"$ac_delim"'/g
20646t clear
20647:clear
20648s/\\$//
20649t bsnlc
20650s/["\\]/\\&/g; s/^/"/; s/$/"/p
20651d
20652:bsnlc
20653s/["\\]/\\&/g; s/^/"/; s/$/\\\\\\n"\\/p
20654b cont
20655' <confdefs.h | sed '
20656s/'"$ac_delim"'/"\\\
20657"/g' >>$CONFIG_STATUS || ac_write_fail=1
20658
20659cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20660  for (key in D) D_is_set[key] = 1
20661  FS = ""
20662}
20663/^[\t ]*#[\t ]*(define|undef)[\t ]+$ac_word_re([\t (]|\$)/ {
20664  line = \$ 0
20665  split(line, arg, " ")
20666  if (arg[1] == "#") {
20667    defundef = arg[2]
20668    mac1 = arg[3]
20669  } else {
20670    defundef = substr(arg[1], 2)
20671    mac1 = arg[2]
20672  }
20673  split(mac1, mac2, "(") #)
20674  macro = mac2[1]
20675  prefix = substr(line, 1, index(line, defundef) - 1)
20676  if (D_is_set[macro]) {
20677    # Preserve the white space surrounding the "#".
20678    print prefix "define", macro P[macro] D[macro]
20679    next
20680  } else {
20681    # Replace #undef with comments.  This is necessary, for example,
20682    # in the case of _POSIX_SOURCE, which is predefined and required
20683    # on some systems where configure will not decide to define it.
20684    if (defundef == "undef") {
20685      print "/*", prefix defundef, macro, "*/"
20686      next
20687    }
20688  }
20689}
20690{ print }
20691_ACAWK
20692_ACEOF
20693cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20694  as_fn_error $? "could not setup config headers machinery" "$LINENO" 5
20695fi # test -n "$CONFIG_HEADERS"
20696
20697
20698eval set X "  :F $CONFIG_FILES  :H $CONFIG_HEADERS  :L $CONFIG_LINKS  :C $CONFIG_COMMANDS"
20699shift
20700for ac_tag
20701do
20702  case $ac_tag in
20703  :[FHLC]) ac_mode=$ac_tag; continue;;
20704  esac
20705  case $ac_mode$ac_tag in
20706  :[FHL]*:*);;
20707  :L* | :C*:*) as_fn_error $? "invalid tag \`$ac_tag'" "$LINENO" 5;;
20708  :[FH]-) ac_tag=-:-;;
20709  :[FH]*) ac_tag=$ac_tag:$ac_tag.in;;
20710  esac
20711  ac_save_IFS=$IFS
20712  IFS=:
20713  set x $ac_tag
20714  IFS=$ac_save_IFS
20715  shift
20716  ac_file=$1
20717  shift
20718
20719  case $ac_mode in
20720  :L) ac_source=$1;;
20721  :[FH])
20722    ac_file_inputs=
20723    for ac_f
20724    do
20725      case $ac_f in
20726      -) ac_f="$ac_tmp/stdin";;
20727      *) # Look for the file first in the build tree, then in the source tree
20728	 # (if the path is not absolute).  The absolute path cannot be DOS-style,
20729	 # because $ac_f cannot contain `:'.
20730	 test -f "$ac_f" ||
20731	   case $ac_f in
20732	   [\\/$]*) false;;
20733	   *) test -f "$srcdir/$ac_f" && ac_f="$srcdir/$ac_f";;
20734	   esac ||
20735	   as_fn_error 1 "cannot find input file: \`$ac_f'" "$LINENO" 5;;
20736      esac
20737      case $ac_f in *\'*) ac_f=`$as_echo "$ac_f" | sed "s/'/'\\\\\\\\''/g"`;; esac
20738      as_fn_append ac_file_inputs " '$ac_f'"
20739    done
20740
20741    # Let's still pretend it is `configure' which instantiates (i.e., don't
20742    # use $as_me), people would be surprised to read:
20743    #    /* config.h.  Generated by config.status.  */
20744    configure_input='Generated from '`
20745	  $as_echo "$*" | sed 's|^[^:]*/||;s|:[^:]*/|, |g'
20746	`' by configure.'
20747    if test x"$ac_file" != x-; then
20748      configure_input="$ac_file.  $configure_input"
20749      { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: creating $ac_file" >&5
20750$as_echo "$as_me: creating $ac_file" >&6;}
20751    fi
20752    # Neutralize special characters interpreted by sed in replacement strings.
20753    case $configure_input in #(
20754    *\&* | *\|* | *\\* )
20755       ac_sed_conf_input=`$as_echo "$configure_input" |
20756       sed 's/[\\\\&|]/\\\\&/g'`;; #(
20757    *) ac_sed_conf_input=$configure_input;;
20758    esac
20759
20760    case $ac_tag in
20761    *:-:* | *:-) cat >"$ac_tmp/stdin" \
20762      || as_fn_error $? "could not create $ac_file" "$LINENO" 5 ;;
20763    esac
20764    ;;
20765  esac
20766
20767  ac_dir=`$as_dirname -- "$ac_file" ||
20768$as_expr X"$ac_file" : 'X\(.*[^/]\)//*[^/][^/]*/*$' \| \
20769	 X"$ac_file" : 'X\(//\)[^/]' \| \
20770	 X"$ac_file" : 'X\(//\)$' \| \
20771	 X"$ac_file" : 'X\(/\)' \| . 2>/dev/null ||
20772$as_echo X"$ac_file" |
20773    sed '/^X\(.*[^/]\)\/\/*[^/][^/]*\/*$/{
20774	    s//\1/
20775	    q
20776	  }
20777	  /^X\(\/\/\)[^/].*/{
20778	    s//\1/
20779	    q
20780	  }
20781	  /^X\(\/\/\)$/{
20782	    s//\1/
20783	    q
20784	  }
20785	  /^X\(\/\).*/{
20786	    s//\1/
20787	    q
20788	  }
20789	  s/.*/./; q'`
20790  as_dir="$ac_dir"; as_fn_mkdir_p
20791  ac_builddir=.
20792
20793case "$ac_dir" in
20794.) ac_dir_suffix= ac_top_builddir_sub=. ac_top_build_prefix= ;;
20795*)
20796  ac_dir_suffix=/`$as_echo "$ac_dir" | sed 's|^\.[\\/]||'`
20797  # A ".." for each directory in $ac_dir_suffix.
20798  ac_top_builddir_sub=`$as_echo "$ac_dir_suffix" | sed 's|/[^\\/]*|/..|g;s|/||'`
20799  case $ac_top_builddir_sub in
20800  "") ac_top_builddir_sub=. ac_top_build_prefix= ;;
20801  *)  ac_top_build_prefix=$ac_top_builddir_sub/ ;;
20802  esac ;;
20803esac
20804ac_abs_top_builddir=$ac_pwd
20805ac_abs_builddir=$ac_pwd$ac_dir_suffix
20806# for backward compatibility:
20807ac_top_builddir=$ac_top_build_prefix
20808
20809case $srcdir in
20810  .)  # We are building in place.
20811    ac_srcdir=.
20812    ac_top_srcdir=$ac_top_builddir_sub
20813    ac_abs_top_srcdir=$ac_pwd ;;
20814  [\\/]* | ?:[\\/]* )  # Absolute name.
20815    ac_srcdir=$srcdir$ac_dir_suffix;
20816    ac_top_srcdir=$srcdir
20817    ac_abs_top_srcdir=$srcdir ;;
20818  *) # Relative name.
20819    ac_srcdir=$ac_top_build_prefix$srcdir$ac_dir_suffix
20820    ac_top_srcdir=$ac_top_build_prefix$srcdir
20821    ac_abs_top_srcdir=$ac_pwd/$srcdir ;;
20822esac
20823ac_abs_srcdir=$ac_abs_top_srcdir$ac_dir_suffix
20824
20825
20826  case $ac_mode in
20827  :F)
20828  #
20829  # CONFIG_FILE
20830  #
20831
20832  case $INSTALL in
20833  [\\/$]* | ?:[\\/]* ) ac_INSTALL=$INSTALL ;;
20834  *) ac_INSTALL=$ac_top_build_prefix$INSTALL ;;
20835  esac
20836  ac_MKDIR_P=$MKDIR_P
20837  case $MKDIR_P in
20838  [\\/$]* | ?:[\\/]* ) ;;
20839  */*) ac_MKDIR_P=$ac_top_build_prefix$MKDIR_P ;;
20840  esac
20841_ACEOF
20842
20843cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20844# If the template does not know about datarootdir, expand it.
20845# FIXME: This hack should be removed a few years after 2.60.
20846ac_datarootdir_hack=; ac_datarootdir_seen=
20847ac_sed_dataroot='
20848/datarootdir/ {
20849  p
20850  q
20851}
20852/@datadir@/p
20853/@docdir@/p
20854/@infodir@/p
20855/@localedir@/p
20856/@mandir@/p'
20857case `eval "sed -n \"\$ac_sed_dataroot\" $ac_file_inputs"` in
20858*datarootdir*) ac_datarootdir_seen=yes;;
20859*@datadir@*|*@docdir@*|*@infodir@*|*@localedir@*|*@mandir@*)
20860  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: $ac_file_inputs seems to ignore the --datarootdir setting" >&5
20861$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: $ac_file_inputs seems to ignore the --datarootdir setting" >&2;}
20862_ACEOF
20863cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20864  ac_datarootdir_hack='
20865  s&@datadir@&$datadir&g
20866  s&@docdir@&$docdir&g
20867  s&@infodir@&$infodir&g
20868  s&@localedir@&$localedir&g
20869  s&@mandir@&$mandir&g
20870  s&\\\${datarootdir}&$datarootdir&g' ;;
20871esac
20872_ACEOF
20873
20874# Neutralize VPATH when `$srcdir' = `.'.
20875# Shell code in configure.ac might set extrasub.
20876# FIXME: do we really want to maintain this feature?
20877cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20878ac_sed_extra="$ac_vpsub
20879$extrasub
20880_ACEOF
20881cat >>$CONFIG_STATUS <<\_ACEOF || ac_write_fail=1
20882:t
20883/@[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z_0-9]*@/!b
20884s|@configure_input@|$ac_sed_conf_input|;t t
20885s&@top_builddir@&$ac_top_builddir_sub&;t t
20886s&@top_build_prefix@&$ac_top_build_prefix&;t t
20887s&@srcdir@&$ac_srcdir&;t t
20888s&@abs_srcdir@&$ac_abs_srcdir&;t t
20889s&@top_srcdir@&$ac_top_srcdir&;t t
20890s&@abs_top_srcdir@&$ac_abs_top_srcdir&;t t
20891s&@builddir@&$ac_builddir&;t t
20892s&@abs_builddir@&$ac_abs_builddir&;t t
20893s&@abs_top_builddir@&$ac_abs_top_builddir&;t t
20894s&@INSTALL@&$ac_INSTALL&;t t
20895s&@MKDIR_P@&$ac_MKDIR_P&;t t
20896$ac_datarootdir_hack
20897"
20898eval sed \"\$ac_sed_extra\" "$ac_file_inputs" | $AWK -f "$ac_tmp/subs.awk" \
20899  >$ac_tmp/out || as_fn_error $? "could not create $ac_file" "$LINENO" 5
20900
20901test -z "$ac_datarootdir_hack$ac_datarootdir_seen" &&
20902  { ac_out=`sed -n '/\${datarootdir}/p' "$ac_tmp/out"`; test -n "$ac_out"; } &&
20903  { ac_out=`sed -n '/^[	 ]*datarootdir[	 ]*:*=/p' \
20904      "$ac_tmp/out"`; test -z "$ac_out"; } &&
20905  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: $ac_file contains a reference to the variable \`datarootdir'
20906which seems to be undefined.  Please make sure it is defined" >&5
20907$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: $ac_file contains a reference to the variable \`datarootdir'
20908which seems to be undefined.  Please make sure it is defined" >&2;}
20909
20910  rm -f "$ac_tmp/stdin"
20911  case $ac_file in
20912  -) cat "$ac_tmp/out" && rm -f "$ac_tmp/out";;
20913  *) rm -f "$ac_file" && mv "$ac_tmp/out" "$ac_file";;
20914  esac \
20915  || as_fn_error $? "could not create $ac_file" "$LINENO" 5
20916 ;;
20917  :H)
20918  #
20919  # CONFIG_HEADER
20920  #
20921  if test x"$ac_file" != x-; then
20922    {
20923      $as_echo "/* $configure_input  */" \
20924      && eval '$AWK -f "$ac_tmp/defines.awk"' "$ac_file_inputs"
20925    } >"$ac_tmp/config.h" \
20926      || as_fn_error $? "could not create $ac_file" "$LINENO" 5
20927    if diff "$ac_file" "$ac_tmp/config.h" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
20928      { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: $ac_file is unchanged" >&5
20929$as_echo "$as_me: $ac_file is unchanged" >&6;}
20930    else
20931      rm -f "$ac_file"
20932      mv "$ac_tmp/config.h" "$ac_file" \
20933	|| as_fn_error $? "could not create $ac_file" "$LINENO" 5
20934    fi
20935  else
20936    $as_echo "/* $configure_input  */" \
20937      && eval '$AWK -f "$ac_tmp/defines.awk"' "$ac_file_inputs" \
20938      || as_fn_error $? "could not create -" "$LINENO" 5
20939  fi
20940 ;;
20941  :L)
20942  #
20943  # CONFIG_LINK
20944  #
20945
20946  if test "$ac_source" = "$ac_file" && test "$srcdir" = '.'; then
20947    :
20948  else
20949    # Prefer the file from the source tree if names are identical.
20950    if test "$ac_source" = "$ac_file" || test ! -r "$ac_source"; then
20951      ac_source=$srcdir/$ac_source
20952    fi
20953
20954    { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: linking $ac_source to $ac_file" >&5
20955$as_echo "$as_me: linking $ac_source to $ac_file" >&6;}
20956
20957    if test ! -r "$ac_source"; then
20958      as_fn_error $? "$ac_source: file not found" "$LINENO" 5
20959    fi
20960    rm -f "$ac_file"
20961
20962    # Try a relative symlink, then a hard link, then a copy.
20963    case $ac_source in
20964    [\\/$]* | ?:[\\/]* ) ac_rel_source=$ac_source ;;
20965	*) ac_rel_source=$ac_top_build_prefix$ac_source ;;
20966    esac
20967    ln -s "$ac_rel_source" "$ac_file" 2>/dev/null ||
20968      ln "$ac_source" "$ac_file" 2>/dev/null ||
20969      cp -p "$ac_source" "$ac_file" ||
20970      as_fn_error $? "cannot link or copy $ac_source to $ac_file" "$LINENO" 5
20971  fi
20972 ;;
20973  :C)  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: executing $ac_file commands" >&5
20974$as_echo "$as_me: executing $ac_file commands" >&6;}
20975 ;;
20976  esac
20977
20978
20979  case $ac_file$ac_mode in
20980    "check_win32_symlinks":C)
20981# Links sometimes fail undetected on Mingw -
20982# so here we detect it and warn the user
20983for FILE in $CONFIG_LINKS
20984 do
20985	# test -e works for symlinks in the MinGW console
20986	test -e `expr "$FILE" : '\([^:]*\)'` || { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: *** link for $FILE -- please fix by hand" >&5
20987$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: *** link for $FILE -- please fix by hand" >&2;}
20988 done
20989 ;;
20990    "src/include/pg_config.h":H)
20991# Update timestamp for pg_config.h (see Makefile.global)
20992echo >src/include/stamp-h
20993 ;;
20994    "src/interfaces/ecpg/include/ecpg_config.h":H) echo >src/interfaces/ecpg/include/stamp-h ;;
20995
20996  esac
20997done # for ac_tag
20998
20999
21000as_fn_exit 0
21001_ACEOF
21002ac_clean_files=$ac_clean_files_save
21003
21004test $ac_write_fail = 0 ||
21005  as_fn_error $? "write failure creating $CONFIG_STATUS" "$LINENO" 5
21006
21007
21008# configure is writing to config.log, and then calls config.status.
21009# config.status does its own redirection, appending to config.log.
21010# Unfortunately, on DOS this fails, as config.log is still kept open
21011# by configure, so config.status won't be able to write to it; its
21012# output is simply discarded.  So we exec the FD to /dev/null,
21013# effectively closing config.log, so it can be properly (re)opened and
21014# appended to by config.status.  When coming back to configure, we
21015# need to make the FD available again.
21016if test "$no_create" != yes; then
21017  ac_cs_success=:
21018  ac_config_status_args=
21019  test "$silent" = yes &&
21020    ac_config_status_args="$ac_config_status_args --quiet"
21021  exec 5>/dev/null
21022  $SHELL $CONFIG_STATUS $ac_config_status_args || ac_cs_success=false
21023  exec 5>>config.log
21024  # Use ||, not &&, to avoid exiting from the if with $? = 1, which
21025  # would make configure fail if this is the last instruction.
21026  $ac_cs_success || as_fn_exit 1
21027fi
21028if test -n "$ac_unrecognized_opts" && test "$enable_option_checking" != no; then
21029  { $as_echo "$as_me:${as_lineno-$LINENO}: WARNING: unrecognized options: $ac_unrecognized_opts" >&5
21030$as_echo "$as_me: WARNING: unrecognized options: $ac_unrecognized_opts" >&2;}
21031fi
21032
21033
21034# Ensure that any meson build directories would reconfigure and see that
21035# there's a conflicting in-tree build and can error out.
21036if test "$vpath_build" = "no"; then
21037  touch meson.build
21038fi
